title: Left side stopped working after one month. Update: Tried three EarPods, all had same issue.
text:
The left ear pod stopped working after just one month of use. I should have listen to the other reviewers. When they were working the sound was great from them. When walking on a treadmill or going for a light run they would bounce out of the ear and would need to be pushed back in all the time. I would not buy these if you are going to do any fast walking or more.

Update:
So after three tries with the EarPods I finally just received a full refund from Amazon. On all three EarPods the left one stopped working right around after a months time. Two stopped working after the return window with Amazon however the Amazon Product Support Expert I chatted with was great. The first two times they sent me a new pair, and this last time they were still able to provide a refund.

Do not buy this product.

I bought these to replace a pair of AirPods Pro that I sent through the washing machine and find that I line the fit of these better (the pro version has rubber tips which make them prone to sliding out of my ears). And while the corded version is less cool than the wireless ones I actually enjoy it more because I never have to worry about my headphones being charged or breaking the Bluetooth connection. I have a gen 1 version of the AirPods where the left one often loses connection and when that happens it pauses whatever you are listening to. With these that won’t happen. The biggest downsides to the corded version I have experienced is that you can’t pair it to the Apple Watch, and if you need to charge your phone with the lightning port then you can’t use the headphones until you are done

title: Good value for a Smart Watch
text:
As far as I can tell, this is a really good value for what you get. It's a strong 4 star but lacking important details. The band is also on the weaker side.

This Smart Watch CANNOT properly function without first charging and using the APP for pairing. It does not pair directly to your Bluetooth but through the APP!!! I was able to pair/bind this using an Android phone with not issues. You will need to carefully follow screen prompts. After pairing with APP, the watch will automatically set time/date and language. Watch is ready to wear!!!

Here are the issues. There are no real instructions/directions, basically you will need to use common sense when trying to configure watch to notify you of incoming messages, SMS and other things like FB, IG, etc. The directions are very lacking to say the least. Paper instructions are much too vague with only a few hints, etc. You cannot use all options without first using the settings in the APP. The watch will most likely "ov

title: Bang for your buck
text:
If you want a bang for your buck this watch is the way to go.

Pros:
Very easy to use. Took 3 seconds to sync if that.
You can control alarms, timers, etc right from app.
User friendly, not as complicated as the expensive devices yet does the job
Nice ability to control your music using spotify (among a few others)
Sleep monitor works great
Walking and running feature shows calories burned which is nice right on the watch
Came fully charged
Easy to read manual, though it's not much needed
Nice silicone-like wrist band

Cons:
Charger is product specific...
Will not work with micro usb basics.
Do not get text message alerts

Overall I recommend this over the more expensive ones if your looking for the basics.

title: battery life is great, backlight goes on randomly the night sometimes waking me or my wife up
text:
'Wrist sense' feature doesn't work well, it kept lighting up the screen at night when I would roll over... or sometime just a minor arm movement. The backlight is very bright, so I had to turn off that feature.

Sleep tracking seems okay, not sure how accurate that is. The app is so so. For heart rate all it tracks is 'resting' which seems a bit silly.

'Activity' feature doesn't seem very useful, other than displaying end of activity information. Can't find any of that in the app.

Battery life for my usage has been excellent, at 24 days battery life was down to 10%.

** UPDATE ** The backlight comes on randomly. It will wake me and/or my wife up. Pressing the button sometimes doesn't turn it off. Seems pointless tracking sleep and then waking the user up with random bright light. The do not disturb feature doesn't keep it from lighting up...and wrist sense is already off. Reboot did not resolve.

** UPDATE 2 ** Seller reached

title: Had to Send Back because of Static Issues and Microphone Complaints.
text:
I had to send back because I had a weird static issue in the one earbud and people complained about the microphone quality. Based off of other reviews, I think I just got a bad set of the Ankers. Bottom line: I was disappointed in these, compared to my other two sets of earbuds. There are better "true wireless" earbuds out there, even for the price.

Below is a review based on the three sets earbuds that I have (or have tried for extended periods). The three sets include: these Aukey earbuds, Anker's Soundcore Life P2, and Sony's WF-1000XM3. Of the three, Aukey is by far the easiest to carry around, due to the case size, and is far cheaper than the rest. I use the my Aukey's for my work-phone calls, and while doing messy projects, while my Sony's for relaxing and listening to high quality music, and for personal phone calls. I used to use the Ankers for work phone calls, until I returned them. I tried not to let the probable defects interfere with my judgement... to a point.<

title: Better than most if you're sitting still, keeping them in your ear while walking not so much
text: These work well for the majority of my needs, which is sitting in my office on video calls, and the sound quality is very good and people can hear me well in most cases. Like just about any Bluetooth device, when the battery starts to get below 50%, there is an echo, but this is not unique to these earbuds. The good news is that the battery lasts through multiple calls, back-to-back if necessary, and they seem to charge very quickly in the charger/storage container. The noise canceling is OK, but not great, for instance, if outside with a little wind, but not bad for indoors. The biggest problem I have with them is that I can't get them to stay in my ear if I'm moving around much at all. I've tried just about every different ear bud that come with them, which are several, and they always work out of my ear when I'm moving around. title: Great or maybe not so much - what do you want earbuds for?
text:
Soundcore is a line of products from Anker. I was unable to find this Life P2 model on the soundcore.com site but didn't need any support so that was okay. The site/product line are apparently still under development as many of the products that appear say "coming soon!"

This is a set of two buds in a carry case that serves as a charge cradle as well as a small battery bank that can recharge the buds on the go. You connect the case to power with a USB type A male cable on one end and a USB type C cable on the other (supplied).

Operating instructions are small-print hieroglyphics that you may need a magnifying glass to decipher. Nothing unusual there. Fortunately these things don't require a lot of setup so a quick once-over will probably be the last time you need them.

title: Terrific.  Top-notch so far.  And a great price point.
text:
Fantastic. I was not a believer in these kinds of earphones. This primarily came form an experience with a set from about two years ago that I found inadequate in several regards. Wow.

1. What's in the box?: You receive the earphones inside a case, plus some extra ear tips (small, large and extra large), and the usual instructions.

2. Set-up? You are told to charge the box for about 5 minutes. Once I did that, I pulled these out and hit the button and was able to connect with my phone.

3. Use?: Once set-up, I immediately started listening to music. These things re quite good. The fit is great, I used a different set of ear tips and frankly it is like I can't feel then at all. As to the sound, it is very good and has what is termed "passive noise cancelling" which really means that the thing is not going to block the world but considering you will use this outside in situations where some listening is required, this is fine. You cannot tur

title: Too much of a crapshoot, quality-wise.
text:
I've returned these twice for two different issues: Battery and general troubles.

The first set worked perfectly, but one battery would only reach 70% or so. It was clearly charging (from 10% to 70%) but never fully.

The second set hit 100%, but had all sorts of connection issues — multiple copies (yes, I removed the old pair), sometimes one side would connect, sometimes both. See the image for one example.

When it finally DID connect to the PC, it had an awful buzzing sound at anything more than about 55% volume. (Other Bluetooth earbuds did not have this issue.) It was fine connected to my phone.

After two tries, as good as the sound quality was, I'm moving on.

title: A Good Bang For Your Money

In my opinion, these gave me the best fit (not too big or not too small), pretty good quality in sound and even better for the price. The reason why I gave it a 4 star is a personal preference. I like my sounds/music really loud. I specifically use my earbuds for the gym (like loud music) and while riding my Harley (need to be loud due to the elements of noise). In my opinion,, it could be a little louder. But then again, they are not headphones. They do stay in place, even when sweating or riding on the expressway. The controls are easy to use and not easy to accidentally press. The best part is that the microphone(s) work well when you're talking on the phone unlike other brands that sound like you're far away. title: Good sound but not great for using while on the move
text:
I bought these headphones as a cheaper alternative to a more robust pair of wireless headphones, like the pixel buds or airpods. Really, I bought them in late 2019 as a hold out for the pixel buds 2 and the USB C cable on my pixel 3 keeps giving out and I just didn't want to deal with the hassle of wired headphones any longer.

The sound quality is good. As someone who is used to listening on a respectable set of AKG 702s or Sony MDR-7056s they obviously don't stack up, but it's better than I would have thought to achieve for the price point, especially in the width of the soundstage.

My main gripe is the in-ear feel. They encompass your whole ear canal with sweaty, grippy plastic. The bad kind. Like the kind that makes it really hard to talk while wearing them, or when you move you hear a muffled rumpling with every step. I personally can't stand that and would prefer a more open ear feel, but that's just me. Just be aware of what you are buying, I find it hard to

title: Great for business use
text: I now own two pairs of the Soundcore buds. I own the Note and now the Life P2. I use them only for work. I have one pair at home, for when I work from home, and one pair in my work office. I use my iPhone 11 for work. These Anker Soundcore products work terrific. They pair very fast, last all day, and sound great while on the phone. I simply have no complaints about using them for work. Heck, even the microphones on each bud sound crystal clear, to the folks on the other end, talking with me. If you are using these for work, jump on them, as they are crazy good and work each and every time. I find they are also very comfy. They come with multiple size ear buds, so it is easy to make them comfy for your specific ear. Another benefit is each bud can be used independently of the other. I have never had a bud run out of battery, but it is super cool knowing I can just throw on the second bud and keep working, should I need to. I can't imagine ever needing to do that though, as these buds l

title: Stopped working before a year was up
text: They started off pretty good, but I started getting Bluetooth connectivity issues even when I was in the same room as my phone and I couldn't deal with it. Stopped using them for a while. I tried another brand and sent those back, so thought I'd pull these back out again to use as a backup. The headphones wouldn't connect to my phone at all! The left earbud was overheating... like uncomfortably hot! It wouldn't turn on or reset at all so I took it out of the case because I didn't want anything to overheat. The right earbud would connect for a few seconds, say it was at 100% battery, then power off. Then it stopped connecting all together. Usually I really love Anker's quality... But if you want earbuds that are going to last longer than a year, I suggest looking elsewhere.

title: Great for Conference Calls, Fine for Music
text:
I use these primarily for my excessive meeting schedule via videoconference (Microsoft Teams). I end up wearing these for 6+ hours a day with no discomfort. Audio quality is great both ways. My wife has these, too, and I hear her loud and clear. If phone and conference calls are your primary use, these are excellent.

For music, they are fine. They block out some noise, but are certainly not active noise canceling. Note the "noise canceling" in the produce description refers to blocking out ambient noise from the microphones during phone calls. Not blocking noise entering your ears like Sony or Bose. They are light on bass. For the price, I would still rate them good for music. If music is your primary use, these are fine but not excellent. I still use them around the yard and in the shop.

title: Pass on them
text:
Pros:

Cons:
The case is pretty large and bulky compared to other earbuds.
After a while of wearing, my ears hurt really bad. To get the best sound, I have to push them further into my ear to create that seal for noise cancellation , which makes the plastic part of the earbud push against my ear and it hurts after a while. I think the actual earbud itself is a little too large. Also, you have to place the earbuds back into the case in a particular way for the connectors to line up right. If they’re not, your phone will stay connected to them and it becomes really frustrating. If you disconnect, and then walk near where you have them stored, it’ll reconnect. title: I like it a lot but can definitely understand the negative opinions.
text:
[[VIDEOID:6a8eb6efb8ddfcbebd8792861c4af225]] After using it 5 days...Overall I like it. I bought this primarily because I needed more information about my heart rhythm and I also wanted a device that provided fall detection do to my solo outdoor activities.

Previously, I used a Garmen Instinct which has GPS far superior, obviously, the battery of the Garmen is VASTLY superior.

The display of the Galaxy Watch is gorgeous and the touch screen is great. Having to charge it daily is kind of drag (more the once if I am using multiple applications -- I just run the minimum I need, but it is definitely a flaw.)

I am not super in love with Samsungs Health App as this watch requires several apps to use all the features of the watch which feels super bloaty. It does seamlessly connect to my phone.

For my day to day needs I am satisfied. Long distance athletes or serious weight lifters will probably be disappointed and probably should stick to Garmen.

title: Features on this watch and how well it syncs with my Android are awesome!
text: I love this watch and the features like phone answering, text messages, etc.  It has a lot of features and many that I have not yet discovered.  The battery life could be a little longer but I have learned to put it on the charger first thing in the morning and then I am good for the remainder of the day/night.  The phone  feature on this watch makes me laugh out loud - almost like "Dick Tracey" and his watch phone from long, long ago!

title: Requires Samsung registration and Samsung apps
text:
For the first time ever on Amazon, my initial review was rejected. Wow.

Nice looking product. However, health apps require Samsung store do which I am not going to do. Same with pay and other features.

Product went back.

title: Great watch
text: Dont be fooled by poor battery life at first,  let it build your use/habits and dont forget to update and battery improves greatly. Mine last 3 days after it adjust itself

title: My favorite watch
text: I used to have a Michael Kors Sofie watch and switched to this one. I love this watch. I can customize the background, it fits well, tracks everything I care about tracking, and connects easily to my phone. My Sofie kept disconnecting randomly and I don't have that problem with this one. It's durable and comfortable. 100% recommend.

title: No
text: Battery not that great compared to my 5 year old watch. No physical bezel to navigate. No integration with google assistant. Not compatible with many old phone apps for some reason

title: Battery life is horrible!!!
text:
I charged new watch out of the box to 100%. Turned off all connections to make a clean test. Put the watch on the table and did not touch it. In about 23 hours the charge was 20% without actively using the watch at all or turning on the screen. It is absolutely horrible. Dormant watch consumed 80% of power.

I am wearing Huawei 6 band for a week and it is still running on the same charge, it's around 15% now. It has the same sport and health functions like Samsung watch and I paid $40 for it. It is also not cumbersome like Samsung watch.

Samsung needs to fix their battery life. Thinking about return.

ECG tracker stopped working. When I try to run it on the watch , it says "New version is available. Install the update from Galaxy App Store to continue using the app. If I click on the Update button on the watch, I get "Page not found" on the phone browser.

I've been through several support chats, calls, sms, they tried to remote to my phone!

title: Just wait for Google watch
text:
1) Galaxy Wearable app is terrible. It is very, very, slow and often fails to select a custom photo without any error. The notification cannot be dismissed and it always sits at the top.
2) Also, it promotes the Samsung ecosystem too much. ECG doesn't work on non-Samsung phones (unless you installed an unprotected app), and only Samsung pay is directly supported.
3) Not 100% their fault but it's quite challenging to set this up for AT&T. Doesn't work with Google Fi.

Verdict: It might still be the best watch for Android, but I would not recommend buying it. Just wait for Google watch, or switch to Apple if you're ok with Apple's closed ecosystem.

title: great feature, short battery life
text: I like the sleep monitoring and snore detection. It was a bit weird to hear myself snoring but helpful in motivating me to get a some help from the sleep clinic. I get too many calendar notifications. Having trouble trying to turn that off. I appreciate being able to hang up on calls I don't want to answer without having to look for my phone. I wish the battery lasted longer. It needs to be on the charger almost every day.

title: Already Returned
text: I was so excited about this watch!  I had it fully charged before I went to bed and Power Saver was on and it was already at 83% battery when I got up.  I refuse to spend my life charging a watch.

title: Battery life sucks
text: It's a nice looking watch, app and face options are great however it will not last more than 1 day without charging.  Battery sucks.  Maybe there are some better power saving options that can be programmed but the default out of box is terrible.

text:
My Fitbit Versa 2 was becoming wonky, so I took the advice of my local cellular store and got the Samsung Galaxy 4. Or I may find that as of 3pm, it has counted 40 steps. And it changes my settings, so I’m always fiddling with the app to fix. I have an Android, but not a Samsung, so some stuff, like ECG, doesn’t work. Wish I had just waited and switched everything to Apple - phone, watch, etc.

Update after 5 months, lowering to 1 star, will try to get a refund. I hate this watch The battery runs out and it goes blank with little notice. This is when it’s been charging all night, dead within 3 hours. I put it on the charger and it gives me a caution symbol which apparently doesn’t have a purpose other than to let you know something is wrong. I’ve researched and done everything possible to extend battery life. It may

title: Worst customer service and warranty.
text:
Watched was a great product until it stopped working. Set it on the charger for about thirty minutes after it died. When I went to turn it back on the screen wouldn't come on, but I could tell it was on because it was vibrating for phone calls and messages. The backside looked like the battery leaked acid out.

Being only 8 months old Samsung told me it was still under warranty and sent me to a repair shop about 40 minutes away, who told me that they cannot repair watches, which Samsung knows but sends people to anyways, and I'd have to send it to Samsung.

The day Samsung received the watch they sent me a bill for $176 saying it was out of warranty. I called and they said it had liquid damage (if you google,many people have been told the same thing when it breaks) and was no longer powering on. The watch has never been in the shower or pool, only in rain which it is supposedly water resistant up to 200' of water. It also powered on before sending it to them.

title: Terrible fitness watch
text:
It's been disappointing from day one of getting this watch. As a galaxy phone user, I decided to upgrade from a 7 year old watch to this, thinking it can only be better. I was so wrong... Bugs that I've noticed so far:

1. Always on display is dim, so when running outside in the sun you can't see anything. There's no way to make it brighter, you have to touch the watch/raise wrist to turn it back on, rendering AOD worthless
2. GPS is slow and inaccurate. Example: running 100m sprints, according to the watch, my top speed was 10mph - yes, 10!!! This is my max out of 8 x 100m sprints. I run 10k at 10mph pace.
3. There is no heart rate zone training in the app. Old watch has it
4. Can't adjust your heart rate zones. My maximum heart rate is much higher than the watch has by default which makes all of my zones wrong. My old watch can be adjusted
5. My old watch has a mode where you race vs your previous run. Not GW4
6. It loses my heart rate all the time

title: worse battery life than prior models...
text: I don't have too many apps on this watch, about the same as the previous watch I traded in. I have to re-charge after 12 hours of use. My other watch lasted all day. I do like it's slimmer and has some extra features. The band is useless to me to struggle with tucking it under. I replaced it with my old watch band that has a buckle.

title: 40mm vs 44mm
text: Just returned this 40mm watch because the battery life is AWFUL.  I have a Galaxy 2 and this was a downgrade.  What I didn't realize, is that my Galaxy 2 was a 44mm size which also means it has bigger battery than the 40mm. If you don't mind the watch only holding a charge for a day, get the 40, but if you want a 2-3 day charge, get the 44mm.  I returned this and bought a 44.  Otherwise, all the features are pretty standard with my old Samsung with a few little upgrades

title: Watch Face is ridiculous
text:
The watch screen cracked after 40 days of wear, even with a screen protector. I am highly disappointed with the screen as it did not receive any impacts outside of a normal desk job. My FitBit lasted over 4 years with no issues, this barely lasted 4 weeks! So now $125 later to fix the screen, I am scared to wear it.

***Even better-apparently if you purchase through Amazon, the warranty is only 30 days if anything else goes wrong. Wish that would have been stated prior to me wasting $175 dollars buying it

title: I love it!
text:
I love my watch! I've used it with my galaxy s21 ultra. I have used it with my boxing workouts and with a wraps and a 12oz glove it hasnt had any issues. Heart rate was checked manually as well. It has worked well at tracking my sleep and recording snoring episodes (husband had a kick listening to it)
I use this to manage spotify while working out. Take messages and calls with a vibration and knock noise. You can only record heart beats with a samsung phone. It has recorded my body fat,water, skeletal muscle as well.
I have it tracking my menstrual cycle as well.
It lasts 3 days without always on display. With it on only 2 days.
The band is ridged sport in black. I would recommend the extreme sport for boxing though. Will purchase later. I haven't had any problems with sensitivity or bezel. I have also used the keyboard on the watch which is nice but I would rather speech to text and it has been flawless. No misheard words!

I haven't used swimming mode ye

title: 1st time galaxy watch user
text:
*Sat 8:31pm Fully charged 100%
*Sun 8:30am 90%
*Monday 8:30am 53%
*Tuesday 8:30am 22%
*Tuesday 5:30pm 7%
*Tuesday 11:14pm fully discharged
Waited until watch fully discharged and turned off on its own before charging it.

*Went to sleep while wearing watch after it was charged Sat 8:31pm.
Sunday relaxed
More active worked Monday & Tuesday
Watch was never removed, worn from
Sat 8:31pm until Tues 11:14 pm
I only use certain features...measure steps, sleep, weather, Battery life, I also send and receive texts and calls.
I found that by letting it Waited fully discharged and turn off on its own and then charge it, it seemed to give me slightly longer battery life.

title: Everything matched my expectations
text:
I use it for cycling and running (jogging really). It provides some nice analyses. The running distance is sometimes high and sometimes low. The biking distance is always a little low, about 1/4 mile out of 15 total. I have no other way of checking my heart rate during exercise but it shoots up to as high as 165. Resting it's 55. It does a good job of measuring sleep. It reports awake time, REM, light sleep, and deep sleep along with a score and oxygen. When I get a high score, I also feel more rested.

I charge it when I shower, so once a day. Takes a long time, almost 2 hours. Would be nice if it charged quicker. My showers aren't that long.

The watch faces are customizable. For some reason the different display options are called complications. You can choose from temperature, heartbeat, battery level, compass, exercise, etc. The watch face I use (Info Board) has 5 complications along with time, day, and 4 hour temperature forecast.

title: It's great but not perfect
text:
Good looking watch and nice resolution. Most health apps are good. It uses the Samsung Messages app, so if you don't like the Google Messages app, get this watch. Hopefully they don't update it to use only the Google Messages. You'll need to search for and download the G-board keyboard if you want a regular keyboard.

Once every other week, the watch will become unresponsive. I see just a black screen. The buttons don't do anything other than prompt me to turn off the watch if I hold the red button long enough. Otherwise, it's just a black screen.

The screen saver (digital clock) is horrible when you use the Always On Display (AOD). What is the point of having AOD if the screen saver mode keeps overriding the AOD display? Don't you use AOD because you want to see the beautiful watch face you've so carefully chosen? But no, the digital clock shows instead. Search for it on online forums -- others have the same gripe and there is no fix for it.

title: Bought for my mother!
text: Purchased this for my mother to replace her “Life Alert”.  It was a great purchase.  She hasn’t reported the battery dying.  It’s awesome for when she forgets her phone at home.

title: Fitness tracker apps for phone and watch did not work!
text: I was unable to get the apps for the phone or the watch to work.  For sleep all it would tell me is the hours sleep, everything else was just blank.  For heart rate just high low and a small chart on the watch that you cant really tell anything from.  The phone app was no better.  I was very disappointed with this product.  it really seems like it was just half baked.  lots of very important details like battery life and app design were horrible.

title: I already had Active 2, this one is better
text:
The big takeaway is in the review's headline; the Galaxy Watch 4 is, IMHO, the best watch -- and best value -- Samsung has made to date. Mine has the 44mm bezel, as opposed to my Active 2's 40mm bezel; considerably larger display, and I think it looks better on my wrist. New features my other watch doesn't have, and possibly the health-related features are a little faster to measure/report.

All this comes at a cost, though. Battery life per charge is shorter, barely two days versus 3-pluf for the Active 2. That's still roughly twice as long as the battery life of my daughter's Apple Watch. And if I forget to recharge ... well, I can always switch to my other watch, which is fully charged and turned off (so it doesn't discharge).

title: Poor battery life
text: Watch is great with one flaw - its battery life.  The battery could last a day with LTE off, however, when it's set to auto it could only last for six hours or less in stand by mode!  If you make a few calls the watch gets overheated very quickly, I regret getting the LTE version because of the poor battery, it kind of defeats the purpose of having a data line if I have to keep LTE off.

title: Love this watch!!!!
text: Love everything about this watch except the battery.

title: Very Frustrating
text: If you love spending hours searching every forum trying in vain to figure out why your watch won't pair to your phone after your 6,748,435 attempt, then you'll love this watch!

title: Love, Love, Love this Smartwatch!
text: Easy to set up and use. Comfortable to wear. Charges quickly. Has a really good sleep app. Records your snoring, which is both a good and bad thing. 😳 Love that you can set the watch face with your four favorite apps. I just tap the screen to start the hiking app, see my step counter, and view my texts and calls. Clear, bright, easy-to-read watch face, which is a big factor for me. Easy to see and answer texts, even has voice mode. Has sensors that track heart rate, blood pressure, stress level, and blood oxygen level with a touch of a button. Has calculator, calendar, alarm, and timer. Exercise apps track time, distance, mph, heart rate, and will show a map of your route when you’re done. Battery lasts a bit over a day. TIP: To extend battery life, be sure to regularly tap the app icon and close the ones you’re not using. Can’t say enough good things about this Smartwatch.

title: Burned and Disappointed
text:
I bought this Samsung watch recently as my previous Samsung watch was on it's last leg. My previous watch (Active) lasted a long time and had no issues.

I ended up returning the watch for a few reasons.
1) main reason is the watch triggered burn marks on my wrists. Pic included.
2) battery life is a joke. It required charge in less than 24 hours. Even on battery saving mode, it was used up super quickly.

Avoid this product at all costs. That is coming from a huge fan of Samsung.

title: Love
text: I'm in love with my Galaxy Watch 4. The battery life is decent but I can't go more than about 2 & a half days without needing to charge it but I charge it every night anyway. There aren't a whole lot of options in terms of cute & practical watch faces but that's okay. The heart rate monitor & ECG abilities are very handy. Seems to be pretty accurate. I also like that when I set it to "walk" it keeps track of my heart rate as I'm walking. You can track your stress level & it gives you ways to manage your stress. It has a TON of great things. Give it a try.

title: Nice looking watch
text: OK, so I got this for the traditional watch feature.  I've been adding some more features to it, slowly...so far so good.  I'm a senior, so it's taking me a while to get used to everything this watch can do.  No sure I want to "add a line" to use all the features as I always have my cell phone with me.  But time will tell.  I have added photos to the background, love that.  Like that I can get messages and sending brief messages it easy to do.  The battery does run out fairly quick if you use all the features.  I use a CPAP, so I can charge at night not missing the sleep function.

title: Better battery life would be nice.
text: The battery could be better along with a bit more control for when it monitors spo2 and other things. I got this as a gift for my gf to replace her old Samsung watch with a terrible battery life. I use garmin watchs and I have to say even my Venu got 2-3 days if I had active days. The battery in this lasts between 14-16hrs without tracking activities. My gf often puts it on the charger at night and forgets to put it back on before going to bed

title: Good buy
text: It tracks SO much more than my Samsung Active 2 Watch! The only downside is the battery runs out quickly depending on what you do with it and what you have running.

title: does so much more than last years.
text: does so much more than last years.

title: Awesome smart watch, gotta charge it everyday though
text: The sleep and stress tracking is fairly accurate, I love that it's dust and water resistance is IP68, though I take it off if I'm washing my hands, taking a shower, or having to submerge my hands and arms (I hate the feeling of water under a watch band). The sleep tracking can also monitor your O2 levels and detect any kind of snoring. The only downsides to me is the short battery life. It will last maybe 30 hours if you don't check on notifications or use it a lot. If you're checking it regularly and have power saving on, it'll last between 20-24 hours so you gotta charge it everyday which doesn't take long (at most 2 hours) thankfully. It's a great watch though. I would recommend getting a screen protector for it. I know I'm really rough on anything I wear on my hands or wrists so I opted for one.

title: Samsung galaxy watch

title: Love this watch!!!
text: I have a Google pixel phone and this watch is amazing.  I had Fitbit for several years and they would disconnect.  I have had this watch for several months now and had zero connectivity issues.  The only complaint is the battery life.  I keep it on battery saver, so it lasts a little longer but I still get my notifications.  I love the features on it, too (steps, HR, stress level, water intake, workouts).

title: Nothing special
text: The size of the watch was a good size.  The band was comfortable.  However, I didn't like the keyboard and I never knew when the battery was running low.  It was not easy to navigate!

title: Nice upgrade from my other smart watch
text:
This samsung galaxy 4 44mm watch is probably smarter than I am. It has so many features that I still haven't used them all. My favorite of those features are sleep tracking, being able to read and send text messages. Also surprisingly it connects to my Ring doorbell and Ring app. It doesn't show the Ring video, just a thumbnail, but it let's you know there is someone at your door. That blew me away when I found that out after I bought the watch.

The drawbacks for this watch are I get a contact rash when I wear it to long. The rash is under the watch itself as well as the band so changing the band wouldn't make a difference. I googled about the watch and rashes and apparently it is a thing that is happening to other people too. The other thing is I have to charge the watch every other day, but it charges in about an hour so that's not so bad.

Overall it is a great smart watch and I am happy with my purchase.

title: Horrible battery life
text: Can't make it a day without the watch going into battery saving mode

title: Not what I hoped for
text: Not the fault of the product.  I am constantly frustrated in their sleep monitor.  If you're just lying quietly, they assume you're asleep, which really skews the readings.  As a 50 year insomniac, I will tell you that when I wake up during the night, the last thing I want to do is be terribly active so the damned watch realizes I'm awake.  I WANT to go back to sleep, so I lie quietly hoping sleep will come and embrace me once again.  I wouldn't buy this again, but it nice that my watch now has my baby girl's hooding ceremony on the face when I want to see the time.

title: I’m so disappointed!
text:
I’ve tried several smartwatches because I really want one. I haven’t had much luck with getting what I want in a watch. I decided to just spend the money and get this watch because I have a Samsung phone. I was sure this would do everything. I am unable to read messenger messages which is a big thing for me. Also the different settings don’t always work. I have the touch screen set to wake when touch and the lifting my wrist setting and neither works. I have to switch it on every time which is a pain because I don’t want it to be on all the time. The battery already runs down quickly. Doesn’t even make it a whole day. Also, if you change a setting on your watch it also changes the setting on your phone. This watch is not at all what I expected and I even researched it.
Update: after several people looking at it I finally got messenger to work. That was frustrating but I guess my advice would be don't give up it can be done.

title: Battery life awful
text: The headphones fit well into my ear and stay in through hard running, but since I first received them, and over the entire 6 months of use, they have been really hard to charge. I use the included charger, and about 4 days out of 7, the battery dies within 10-30 minutes of my turning them on, even when I've charged overnight. I'm not sure if I got a lemon or not, but I am going to try to return these. The longest charge I've ever gotten is about 60 minutes. I would not recommend these.

title: But I'm glad i went with these and extremely happy with my ...
text:
I was torn between these and the Beats comparable model. But I'm glad i went with these and extremely happy with my choice. The sound quality is amazing and extremely comfortable in your ears. While the rated battery life is less than some of the competition its a non-issue. How many people have the time of day to listen to over 6 hours of music in a single sitting. And if you do listen to a ton of music over the day just get one of the cases with a built in charger and you're good to go.

I've Compared these to the Beats my buddy has and trust me... do yourself a favor and go with the headphones from a real audio company not a marketing company.

title: Won't stay in my ears

title: So Far So Good!
text: I have now had these Bose Soundsport headphones for a few weeks. I use them primarily for workouts and walks. First impressions are very good. The fit and finish is what you would expect from a $150 set of headphones. Sound wise they sound good but audiophiles will probably think they are bass heavy. I don't really mind.  I have always been partial to high end headphones and while i prefer the overall sound profile of the wired Etymotic and Klipsch headphones i have, i don't find the sound quality of the Bose Soundsports off putting. Just different.  They are very comfortable, stay in place,  and the earpieces do a decent (if not stellar) job of noise isolation. The other benefit is the little wing that slides into you ear to hold the headphones in places is part of the ear plug. title: Once again, right earbud nearly dead
text: This is the second pair of identical Bose headphones I bought from Amazon over the past 3 years. This pair that I bought in November of 2020 is having the same issue as the one I bought in 2018. Out of nowhere the right sound earbud goes very tinny and silent. I tried resetting the headphones by plugging in waiting 5 seconds, and then unplugging and waiting a minute. None of these issues have helped. The last time this happened and sent me a refurbished set. I will be contacting customer support again and letting them know how unhappy I am with this product.

title: Terrible customer service
text:
I think Bose makes great products, usually. I like their quality. I've had good experiences, until now. The product that arrived did not consistently connect to the Bluetooth in my mobile device and laptop. After a few days of having to re-pair the Soundsport with my devices multiple times, while powering everything off and on again, I called Bose to troubleshoot. They immediately said to ship them the headphones and they'd replace them. (This tells me the problem is not unique, but common.) I was told I'd receive the new ones in 2-3 days as a courtesy (usually 10 days), and that they did not need to receive my return before mailing out the new ones. 10 days later when I did not receive anything from Bose, I called and was told they were not in stock. I'd not been contacted at all. I then asked for a refund, which had to be issued through Amazon, where I bought them.
Now I'll decide if I want to take another chance on these headphones and purchase them again.

title: Volume all the way up and can barely hear my music.
text: I had High hopes for these but they just never panned out.  No matter how high the volume or how much I tried to custom fit the buds I could never get good volume and in fact my left ear specially could hardly hear anything.  I just couldn't get them to work for me so I purchased iPod Pro and what a difference.  Not only can I hear everything crisp and clear they have great noise canceling tech also. Sorry I wasted money on these Bose.

title: Decent sound but after a full charge, when turned ...
text: Decent sound but after a full charge, when turned off and stored in case, the battery charge decreases about 10-15% per day which is a big problem if you don't use for a few days.  It becomes necessary to recharge them immediately prior to every use if you plan to use them more than briefly.

title: Degrades quickly, irritating, and Bluetooth is garbage
text:
7 Month Update: Knocking off the stars I gave before, because the cable sheath has already fallen apart (Bose, you've been screwing this up for at least eight years of revisions - fix this already!), the microphone doesn't work anymore, and the battery life is now compete garbage (3 hours on a good day).

Additionally, the low battery warning is obnoxious, long, frequent, and starts too early. It mutes what you're listening to, and the voice says "Battery low. Please charge now." I know that "battery low" means it needs charging, and you don't have to talk over my podcast every two minutes to remind me and reinform me that it needs to be charged. I will not charge now - I will use it until either the battery dies or I get to a point where it's convenient to charge. The only thing the stupid voice accomplishes is to irritate me.

After eight years of Bose headphones, it seems likely this will be my last - they clearly have trouble learning from their mistakes, and th

title: Great purchase!
text: Great sound from these small earbuds.  They twist in easily and are super comfortable for my small ears.  I have tried numerous earbuds and never found a good fit.  I use these running on the trail, in the gym, or around the house.  They are not noise canceling, but they will drown out just about everything.  Several rubber ear pieces to fit any size ear too.  Great product for the price, and stays charged longer than the product reviews state.  Carrying case comes in very handy for gym goers like myself.

title: Perfect fit and sound.
text: I don't understand the negative reviews posted. These fit in the ear better than any other earpods that I have used. And believe me I have tried many. The sound is fantastic. I use them with my Apple watch when exercising. That way I don't have to carry my phone with me. As far as how well they hold up, only time will tell.

title: terrific
text:
I got these for my fiancé because the mic on his iphone is damaged and he had been coveting my wireless VModa Crossfades. He needs to be able to multitask and talk for his day job in real estate- but as a DJ he also requires beautiful clear, crisp, balanced sound when casually listening to music. Enter these.
He has very fancy wired headphones, but he needed to replace older earbuds. We live in nyc where portability is key, and he's thoroughly impressed by their comfort, compact style and usability. They fit his needs perfectly and he couldn't be more pleased. The bluetooth receiver is strong, he can hear calls clearly and he was surprised by the clarity of sound wirelessly.

title: Nice sound, could be better
text:
Sound is good, to be a bose, honestly could be better.. takes a while to get used to, they stay put good so far.. haven’t taken them for a spin, but will
Update how they hold under sweat. I Like the interaction with the phone and app for control.

title: Great earphones with one exception
text: I really like these earphones. The sound quality is what you'd expect from Bose. Very balanced, not too much bass. Really my only gripe is the ridiculously short charging cord and the lack of a charging block to plug it into. If they'd supplied a 3 ft cord and a charging block this would easily be a 5 star review.

title: DO NOT BUY
text: Does not get loud at all I've lost faith in bose headsets at this point. The lower end headsets I have tried demolished these. High advise you do not get these. Waste of time and money.

title: Back to Bose Ear Buds...
text:
I've used Bose wire ear buds for years and love them. Very comfortable and great sound.
But like everything else, if you use them a lot, they will wear out some how.
I recently "inherited" my daughters iPhone 7 Plus and needed a wireless solution. After researching ear buds, I settled on Jabra 65t's.
They were nice, sounded good and had the features I wanted. Unfortunately, I could not keep them from falling out, even after trying to use foam ear tips. I sent the Jabras back.
I didn't want the Frankenstein Bose wireless buds, so settled on these, even though they are wired together.
These fit similarly to the wired Bose that I have and do not fall out!
Sound is great and longevity will tell with time.
I will update this review if things change.

title: Ouch
text: Not worth the price. Not the best sounds when you use youtubes 10 hour videos

title: Nice.
text:
yeah, pretty nice, okay their ------g excellent. I love um.
I put 4 stars so you would read this. Yes you pay for them but their BOSE !
and they are excellent. you won't be sorry. Life is short have cool ear buds.

title: Great set of "wireless" headphones
text: Wife was yearning for a set of wireless headphones, but had some negative experiences with the attached design of many competitor models.  Bought this on a whim to see if she'd like them, as she kept stealing my tethered bose headphones.  Long story short, she loves these and uses them all the time.  Batteries last around 6 hours, and they're comfortable for her ears.  I've found you get what you pay for when purchasing headphones, and would buy these again in a heartbeat.

title: Will stop working after a few months, so stay away!
text: Would strongly recommend not to buy. It stopped working after a couple of months. Would not charge up. I got a replacement under warranty and it stopped working as well. There seems to be a quality issue. Stay away!

title: Battery Life Terrible!
text: Sound is good, but battery life stinks!  Sometimes they only charged to 90 percent, even after two days.  Then, they would drop to 40 percent over just my 35 min drive to work.  The battery life is not good.  I used to have LG and the battery life was great, but they died every 6 mos to a year so was looking for something better that would last.  I sent the headphones back a week ago and haven't heard anything from Bose.  Thought these would be a quality product since it's Bose and they cost an area and leg.

title: Overall good product
text: My boyfriend always used this at the gym so I decided to buy one too. The music quality is great. It does kind of get annoying when trying to jog because the cord is moving around making you think its going to fall out at any second, but I guess that is just normal. Overall, I would recommend this product. The price is high, but I have had it for over 6 months now and it hasn't broken like a 5 dollar pair would.

title: Simply the best
text: I have two pair for battery continuity.  They're that good.  I know people complain about Bose, but these are simply the best headphones I have ever owned.  Bass response is amazing.  High notes are amazing.  I love the easy to use control on the cord.  This is my go to.  I have other earbuds and what not, but don't like them.  This is what I use and you should to.  You won't be disappointed.

title: Ergonomics sacrificed for sound
text: Sound is great. Ergonomics not so much. Relatively heavy ear pieces expected to stay in place based on some assumed universal ear anatomy. If for instance you try to run with them, prepare to continually fidget with them as you try to keep them jammed in your ears.

title: First off i love the sound that this product outputs what bose could ...
text: Hey, First off i love the sound that this product outputs what bose could have done better was with the appearance of this product the bulkiness of the product and with long periods of time in my ears it started to feel a bit uncomfortable they could have also reduced the weight a little bit but we can only hope for those improvements with the second generation. But other wise the headphones does well with many generes of music crisp sound with very nice bass not to overpowering out of respect the product is also great on battery life sometimes it has a hard time of staying in my ears dont know if its the weight of the product or the shape of my ears but i tend to have to fix them too many times while taking a jog or while taking a simple but for everyday i had to put these down because of it being uncomfortable but if i were you i would still give the product a chance it may work for you and not for me not every product is for everyone and i wish everyone the best ✔️

title: Nope
text: Not impressed at all. It said it was compatible with my husband's type of phone but when it came in the mail it said on the Box that it was not. We had to return it.

title: My iPhone music and audiobooks sound amazing with these and make me want to workout longer ...
text: Say goodbye to tangled earbuds once you try these Bose Soundsport Wireless gems.  My iPhone music and audiobooks sound amazing with these and make me want to workout longer and harder. If you are looking for fantastic balance and sound and a comfortable fit, TRUST the BOSE name as this product does not disappoint.

title: Good headphones for a workout.
text: Got this for exercise for myself and these do the trick! Great sound, and sweat proof. Would not suggest these to someone who wants to listen to music and just sit around, while the sound quality is good, it is not great and there are better headphones which aren't sweatproof for this price. But this is exactly what I needed.

title: My husband’s only choice.
text: My husband has bought other ear buds before but was super disappointed in the sound. He loves these! It cuts out background noise so that he can hear well. Plus, if he’s talking on the phone with them, he doesn’t sound like he’s in a tunnel to the person he’s talking to. He won’t use any other ear buds but theae.

title: Love em but they don't work anymore
text: UPDATE;  18 month later these earbuds will not hold a connection for more than 20 seconds...they keep unpairing from my computer.  Apparently there are numerous other owners with the same problem and Bose will not offer any solution other than to recharge, which doesn't work to fix the problem.  I will not replace with the same product.

title: Expensive but excellent
text:
Pros:
Uses a regular micro usb
Very light weight
Headphones tell you the battery life upon power on
Good quality sound
Upgradeable firmware with the Bose Connect phone App
Very easy to feel your way across the buttons
Buttons actually work with android (amazon music, spotify, google play)
7hr battery life
1yr warranty thru bose
Extremely water/sweat resistant

Cons:
Connecting cable is long (for me)
Buttons are a little firm
Expensive

title: WOW...very impressed!
text:
Update 5 - 9.07.17: after 7 months of ownership I have to say that these headphones have been excellent. Very few Bluetooth drops, plenty loud and very nice bass (I do use an EQ on Spotify).

No problems with static or the rubber casing peeling. Understand that I don’t use these for working out, so the only sweat they have seen is from yard work.

They have been outstanding and when I last checked, they were still playing on about 6 hours per charge!

Update 4: Well I have had these since 2.16.17 (today is 5.13.17) and put many hours of listening on them. While I don't work out with them, they have had their fair share of sweat from working in the yard. I have had zero problems with them and just bought my wife a pair yesterday. They still sound excellent and the bass response is phenomenal. I use Spotify with the EQ set to ROCK since that's what I mainly listen too. I will say that after many hours of use, they sound better than when they were new and the

title: 1.5 year update
text:
After a little past the 1 year mark, these kind of took a a downturn. The rubber around the power button began to come undone, so it will start to malfunction from sweat as there isn't any protection at all.

The battery lasts about 50% as long and will not charge above 80% (as indicated by the voice when you turn on the headphones).

The sound is still fine, and the rubber earpieces held up. So that was a huge plus.

I used a case or had them sitting on a tabletop probably 99%+ of the time I wasn't wearing them. I used them anywhere from 2-6 hours a day for the first 6 months, then less as the battery degraded.

(Old review below, was 4 stars, is now 3)

Had these for a few months now.

Comfort -

It took some getting used to but they are pretty comfortable. I've worn better and wish these were more compatible with Comply eartips. If you try and wear them without the right tips on them, they will fall out after you

title: Very impressed - Great sound across all genres
text:
Very impressed with the sound quality.

I've been a Bose fan for quite some time, and after a lot of consideration I decided to try out these. I wanted the same quality as my plug in Bose headphones. I am very happy with my purchase. I'd recommend downloading the Bose Connect App to help upgrade the software and easily connect your device.

title: Broken
text: Headphone sounds good but was sent a broken charger cord.

title: Good product
text: These are great sounding headphones! I didn’t end up keeping them because of how much they pertrude out of my ears. But other than that they are great. They have that classic Bose sound. Not as crisp Highs as the Jaybirds but a very well balanced sound.

title: Best wireless headphone ever
text:
Best wireless headphone ever. Fit perfectly and solidly into the ears. Take into account the headphones protrudes somewhat from the ear but I guess the device must accommodate the electronics. Bluetooth is easily set, connects & works flawlessly with multiple devices.
The quality of the sound is superb for this size device. Sound source responds flawlessly to the control and the mic picks up the voice very clearly. I was told that I never sounded so clear.
I love it.

title: Bad choice for running
text: Loud wind noise in small breeze, buds are heavy with poor battery life. standby mode consumes battery very fast. Must remember to turn if off after use. Buds protrude too much outside of ear canal and make loud noise when breaks with wind

title: Great, quality earbuds by Bose with Tile app; battery life needs improvement
text: Overall, the durability and performance  of these earbuds is excellent. The sound quality is very good and the noise cancellation is highly rated. I work in a lab environment where constant mechanical humming is a mild irritant. The noise cancellation blocks this at very high volumes, while allowing some ambient noise at lower volumes. This feature is exceptional! The sound quality is great, but not too heavy on bass if you are looking for a product specifically for 808 bass tones. The battery life is average; I get about 6 hours if I play music and podcasts from 100% to dead. The trouble with the battery is that the device does not dictate precise percentages—only estimations. But that’s about the only issue I have with the device. The coolest feature is the Tile app linked to it. The buds make a SUPER LOUD chiming sound that helps you find them easily. It’s the primary reason I bought them and it has saved me so much money! I constantly lose earbuds because they catch onto things, ge

title: Always count on Bose
text:
After 3 yrs and a heavy rain storm I had to replace my old Bose headset. These are great for any sport and the comfort is off the charts!!

I can't do the cordless ones, because I'm too afraid I'll loose one while riding.

title: Bose vs. Beats? There is no comparison.. BOSE!
text: I'll keep this short and sweet... Bose vs. Beats? There is no comparison.. I have been working out with these for about a month and never once have I had them fall out or need adjusting like most every other headphone I have ever owned! I do about 30 minutes of cardio followed by heavy lifting for 45 minutes and they stay put through EVERY workout and cardio machine!! HIGHLY recommend these, you will not be disappointed!!

title: A thousand times better than air pods
text: I absolutely love these headphones. This is my second pair. My first pair lasted over 3.5 years with daily use throughout my work day. They are a comfortable fit and I don’t ever have to worry about them falling out of my ears. I would recommend these all day, every day.

title: Higher price, lowest reliability.
text: Never heard the sound. Bose sent me replacement buds three time, before I gave up and asked for a refund. These are high end headphones. Not cheap. But they would not power-on. I could not connect with Bluetooth. Worst product I have ever purchased through Amazon. Back to the gym with wired headphones, the reliable choice.

title: Broke after 1 year
text: The connection breaks up only a short distance from the phone. Also, I would like to mention that my headphones stopped working just after 1 year. The power button stopped working. Since the warranty is only for 1 year I had to repurchase. I am disappointed in the product. Bose held a higher standard in my mind and the product did not live up to my expectations.

title: Bose almost got it right
text: Got these as a Christmas gift for myself. Great material and feels solid. Made sure to charge these to 100% and during first use they lasted a little less than an hour. After recharging they will last me over a weeks worth of visits to the gym, which is great. Not sure what the issue was. They have amazing sound, I just really wish they were louder. Purchasing Bose I just really expected more sound and to feel more bass.

title: Works good- most of the time
text:
These are affective at canceling out noise, are comfortable in my ear and provide a good tone.
However, they frequently disconnect from my phone and have a short battery life.
Very disappointing for a Bose product

title: Wires too short, falls off constantly
text:
They made these for PENCIL NECKS! They have a clip, but I screwed up and they got CRUSHED.

#SquaretradeWarraty? HA HA HA! Dream on! They only cover repairs AFTER manufacturer warranty expires, NOT ACCIDENTAL DAMAGE!

title: Five Stars
text: Awesome quality sound, stays in your ear even when you are sweating and bouncing.

title: These are perfect for runs, or hitting the gym!
text:
I have tried many different wireless earbuds for workouts. These have been the best I have tried so far. They come with three different ear inserts, to match the size of your ear. Once in, they fit snugly and do not feel as if they will pop out. Even if they should, these come with a handy clip to clip onto your shirt collar. So glad I made another attempt at this purchase after the Galaxy Pro buds did not pan out.

The only thing that could use improvement with these, is the battery life. 6 hours of juice will not get me through a long hike, or flight.

title: So far so good. These wireless earbuds are a life changer
text: So far so good. These wireless earbuds are a life changer. I can run more easily without having to worry about the wires slipping out of my ear. As a female, clothes with pockets are hard to come by, even workout clothes. Now I can just put my phone down and do whatever workout I need. (Or I'll use a fanny pack) I'm so mobile!!

title: Randomly would stop playing while still connected
text: So wanted to love these, big Bose fan.  Great fit, comfortable, stayed put.  But it had a fundamental issue--it would stop working during media play.  I be connected, doing my thing, then it would just stop making sound.  I'd check my phone, and it was still connected, but nothing was coming out.  I could make calls, and open new media, but the original media just dropped.  the timing of the drop wa random--sometimes 20 minutes, sometimes 7 minutes (yes, I timed it it happened so often).  It may be app specific, but either way not for me.

title: Rage! Bring back headphones that stay in users ears!!!
text: These stupid headphones fall out more during regular use than than any other headphone I've ever used. Absolutely garbage! Yes, the sound quality is great, until they fall on the ground and can't hear them at all anymore.

title: BE CAREFUL YOU GET WHAT YOU WANT
text:
I FOUND THE BOSE SOUNDSPORT PULSE. IT CAME IN DIFFERENT COLORS. I CHOSE A DIFFERENT COLOR BUT THE OTHER COLORS WERE NOT, NOT, NOT, THE PULSE!! I DID NOT REALIZE IT UNTIL I RECEIVED THE WRONG ITEM.
NORMALLY ONE WOULD EXPECT THE OTHER COLOR OPTIONS WERE THE SAME ITME... BUT IN THIS CASE IT IS A DIFFERENT ITEM!!! I WANTED THE HEART MONITOR BUT DIDN’T GET IT.

title: Great earbuds
text:
I was gifted a pair of these and they are so nice I decided to leave a review 2 years later. The battery is still holding up (it only lasts about 4.5 hours now, but that is still more than enough for any workout or commute).

The sound quality is excellent and they are very easy to pair with a smartphone.

They fit in the ear perfectly without going in too deep. They don't fall out either. If they do, the wire helps stop you from losing them. You can clip the wire so it doesn't snag on anything and yank them out.

title: Product DEAD in under a year!
text: I was always a big Bose fan but this product died in under a year and Bose only offered a discounted price on the same product.  Big disappointment.  I will never recommend Bose again.  Battery will no longer charge.

title: Lives Up to My Expectations
text: I'm a long-time Bose fan and expect nothing less than the best.  These headphones live up to my expectations.  The setup instructions are pretty basic, and even though I'm not the sharpest knife in the drawer when it comes to electronics, I hooked up with both my iPod and MacBook Pro without difficulty.  I did have to figure out how to adjust the volume; you have to adjust the volume level on your device first, then you can fine tune the level on the headphone wire.  The earplugs were easy to insert in my ear (am using the medium size which came with the headphones) and they feel secure.  Some reviewers commented that these stick out from your head unusually far, but I don't think so.  The sound quality is totally outstanding (both bass and treble), and if you insert the plugs in your ear well, you barely notice extraneous noise.  They are lighter in weight than I though they would be, so they aren't bothersome in that regard.  Highly recommend these wireless headphones.

title: Disappointing
text: Started falling apart roughly 6 months into owning them, various pieces would fall off like the bose logo on the side, the sound volume button rubber, and the rubber around each ear. I took good care of them, left them in a case when unused. At the 13 month mark, it randomly shut off despite having a full charge and will not turn back on.

title: They are crap!!!
text: I’ve tried to make these work for awhile. They started falling apart after the first year. With the price being what it is, as well as Bose being a superior brand . I was greatly disappointed. Going forward I will stay with sennheiser. They are good enough for professional audio studios, who will touch Bose!!!

title: These have become an essential part of my day. ...
text: These have become an essential part of my day. I use them constantly for calls at work, for listening to music on my Mac, and for listening to audiobooks on my phone when I'm walking or at the gym. The handoff between devices is nearly seamless, and it just seems to know the priority -- calls from my phone are more important than tunes from the computer. The charge lasts a long time, although I am somewhat strategic about when I charge them at my desk during the day.

title: Ok...zero noise cancellation
text: Just ok. Noice cancellation is zero. I hear more outside noise with these then a cheap pair of earbuds I got for $5. Sound itself is very good and clear. I travel a lot and haven't had success on the plane. Pick up too much background noise. Considering returning might keep as backup.

title: a diamond in the rough
text: i was incredibly surprised at the sound quality.  It was comparable to the high quality wired bose earbuds.  There is no difference in quality between these and their wired cousins at all, and the battery life is phenomenal, even more so if you turn them off after every use. overall id give them a 9.5/to, with my only "complaint" being i wish they came in more colors

title: Sounded great the few times I wore them that they WORKED :(
text: Used less than a dozen times and they will not turn on.  Green light shows charged, unplug them and there is NOTHING.  No lights, nothing works. Update was done....didn't help.

title: Solid Bose Opening Price-Point.  Classic Sound.
text:
I bought a pair of these in 2015, so re-purchasing based on experience with 1st pair. Solid sound and top 3 in this price-point as far as wireless. I just expected a bit more after a 4th-5th generation of the same product. However, the sound is great, fit is great. I would definitely recommend whether it's your 1st wireless pair or your 4th.

Pros: Ergonomic fit, great sound, ease of programming
Cons: Batttery life (7.5/10), noise cancelling, refining same series after 5 years.

Overall: Great ear-piece, comfortable, great bass. It would be an unbelievable value at $79. However, I am glad i re-purchased @ $100. My set that is 5 years old still sounds great, other than a few hardware diminishes. Top 3 in this price-point. I am keeping and not returning for another.

title: Prefer over BeatsX
text:
Tried the Beats X first and while I loved the W1 chip, it couldn't keep up with the bass it was trying to output (distortion mania) and the long cable drove me absolutely nuts and frankly looked weird.

At the same price as the Beats X, this runs circles around it in terms of sound quality. The bass is simply incredible on these things. Loud, punchy and no distortion, perfect for workout music. The rest of the soundstage sounds great to me. Audiophiles might scoff, but most consumers will rave about the sound it produces.

They're also more comfortable, despite being somewhat bulky. It's worth noting these aren't really "in-ear" earbuds as in they don't stick into your ear canal and do much to block the outside world. They have a similar seal as the Earpods/Airpods, just more comfortable and secure. I consider that a plus when walking around the city or going on runs. It also doesn't get that squishy suction cup effect when sweating at the gym. The wingtips

title: Great headphones
text: These are great headphones.  I wear them for hard workouts.  They sound great and stay in my ears.  I think the sound rivals any headphones I have owned, wireless or wires.  The battery life is solid.

title: Bought Second Pair
text:
I got my first pair as a gift, they last about 3 years. When the battery stopped keeping a charge and the blue tooth lost is connection all the time, I decided to buy the second pair.

I know that overall I gave it 5 but all my other stars are less than that, but this is why I gave it a 5:
- Adaptability - 5 stars - comes with 3 different sizes of ear pieces, so you should be able to find one that fits you
- Comfort - 5 stars - the ear pieces are soft and comfortable in the ear and don't come off very easily. I've had other ear buds where I have been afraid of the little tips coming off in my ear or the silicone is harder and hurts your ears. These don't at all! They extremely comfortable!

title: JUNK!!!
text: These are absolute junk.  Not at all the Bose standard.  They broke within 30days.  DO NOT BUY!

title: Exactly what I wanted
text: Excellent headset. With phone makers ditching the headphone jack, Bluetooth headsets are the best way to go for private sound. These are not noise cancelling, which, for me, is a good thing in this case. I have an over ear noise cancelling headset for when that is appropriate. These let just enough outside information pass through so that I'm not oblivious to my surroundings while still delivering that fine Bose sound.

title: I really love these headphones.  comfortable for my sensitive ears.
text: I had to order a new pair as my last ones got lost on a trip.  i loved them so much.

title: Best headphones

title: Great sound and very comfortable

title: GOOD ACCESSORY
text: Gave this as a gift to my brother.  He says he like it.

title: great sound

title: Battery issues resolved through a reset
text:
Great sound quality and I enjoyed them until a week ago when they stopped charging. It doesn't matter which charger I use, they charge all night and notify me of "low battery" as soon as I wear them. They lasted almost 3 months :/

2. Unplug the headphone from charging cable and power them on.
3. Enter the earbuds into the p

title: Update: Does not work anymore (Was: "They keep going even when wet")
text:
Update:
They only lasted a few months. They simply just don't work now. Wasted my money. Look elsewhere. It's a bit expensive, but Swimbuds have 3 different types of waterproof head phones for the type of water activity you are involved with. I am still happy with my Swimbuds.

Original Review:
I did really well with this. I kept accidentally going underwater with it during deep water exercise. Just below the surface, but the first time, the USB jack was not closed up and nothing went wrong. I should mention that I wasn't swimming laps with it. It did come off a few times, but not to the level of being annoying. Unfortunately, I've lost it. title: Very Impressed
text:

However the one I received had an issue. I sent the seller an email and I got a reply back in less than an hour. They certainly wanted to help and provided a suggestion on how to resolve the problem. The next day I tried, and it didn't work.

Sent another email, and again, less than an hour for a reply. They let me know another was on its way. In a few days I had a new working unit.

So it's great when the product works, but I think it says a lot more when the seller is so invested in making things right. 6 Stars.

I've had these headphones for a week now and have used them for over 15 hours of meetings in that time, paired with my laptop. The most important thing for me was whether my audio was good enough according to the other persons on our group calls, and they agreed that the sound coming through was very clear. I also could hear very well, so I am pleased with the end result and the quality overall.

Pairing with the iPhone was a snap; the Kindle Fire HDX was not as cooperative, but eventually it paired well and the sound is very good. My Windows 10 laptop is a bit more challenging to pair and manage, BUT that's not the fault of the headphones, it's just a bit tricky to figure out which setting controls the levels for my audio. Once I found that among the Windows settings, it was fine. As soon as I turn on the headphones, The ActiveBuds connect promptly to the laptop.

title: No one seems to make good-quality earbuds any more.
text:
My wife got these for me for Christmas because my old, $16 (like 25 year old) Sony ear hook buds finally fell apart. I've been writing a book and using Speechify for editing, using these earbuds. Initially, I liked that there were different sized silicone pieces to fit different size ear-holes. The sound quality seemed fine, and they were a bit bulky, a bit loose, but seemed not bad for $20. So now (1/10/21, or 17 days later) the charging port broke loose from the solder joints which affixed it to the little printed circuit board inside. Anyone who knows anything about wiring and electronics can tell you that a solder joint on a circuit board is inadequate to secure something that is going to have pressure put on it repeatedly due to plugging and unplugging. Obviously no one at Senso thought of this, or cared enough to put it into practice.

This is all part of a larger problem which is what is known as 'planned obsolescence' or put simply 'making things cheap so they break q

This is not worth your money except for a watch, when the screen doesn't go blank. No not worth it. I would not recommend it. Or buy it. It will be a hassle to replace. So I lose.

text:

title: Honestly, everything I was looking for
text:
I love these. I love these enough that I have a pair at work and a pair at home.

I have some mental illness and part of that for me is that outside noises, especially when I'm watching TV/Movies or listening to music at work, put me in fight or flight mode and I simply cannot sit there and deal with it. I NEED good noise cancelling and the volume has to get pretty high.

I went through Amazon and ordered something like 25 pairs of headphones - everything from $30 headphones to $600 headphones. I returned almost all of them, and was shocked at how little I liked the sound of the really expensive headphones.

Cut to these guys, and man, they had everything I wanted. I immediately bought a second pair so I could leave one at the office.

They are comfortable, first of all. I can have these on for hour at a time and be just fine with it. I don't find my ears getting sweaty and they don't agitate me in any way. The ANC is very, very, VERY good

title: Technical flaw in my  Soundcore Life Q20 pair (NC noise).
text: I bought these for $39.99. It is a cheap price for the quality of sound these earphones provide. BUT.... I am still debating whether I should return these. My pair of the  Soundcore Life Q20  earphones has a technical defect. You will hear unbearable screeching noise (your ears will burst) each time you try to go for a run wearing these earphones with NC on. The same thing happens if you ride in the car or a bus and the vehicle hits the rough surface. when you have  NC on... I took a long ride and was not able to use these earphones in the NC mode as I was getting so much static noise that made the whole user experience super annoying. These earphones work great if you want to use those to listen to the music sitting on the couch or in the office chair. Do not move or run or you will get static screeching noises. So disappointing.  If you do not move, the sound is excellent though. Alternatively, you need to turn the NC off, if you expect a lot of movement / vibration -- so the NC feat

title: Don't buy Anker Soundcore Life Q20 if you like your ears!

Apparently that is not the case for the second pair exhibited the same painful noise when touching the headphones for any reason with noise canceling was enabled. Hunted product review here, Amazon, and found others reporting the same issue. Too bad. Seemed like a nice headphone for $50 ($10 off coupon).

Noise cancelling cannot be disabled without turning it off every time you turn on the headphone. If it could, I would have kept the headphones for non-critical listening at home. For example watching television or Netflix on my laptop or tablet.

Advise not buying these and wait for the next generation which hopefully will not have the same issue.

title: Not durable at all. Earpiece connection to the headband is destined for failure.
text:
I bought two of these headphones for my teenage daughters about 6 months ago. They seemed to have decent sound when I listened to them, although a bit bass heavy for my liking. The noise cancellation was almost entirely ineffective, but that's to be expected at this price point. What's not expected is material failure in half a year....

One of my daughters came to me today to show me that the left earpiece on her headphones had completely broken off. The joint where the earpiece connects to the band is made entirely of extremely fragile, brittle plastic with one tiny metal pin. I looked at the right earpiece and see that is cracked as well around the same joint, and would likely fail soon too.

Sound quality - I am not an audiophile and usually listen to audiobooks. That said I have been very happy with what I hear using these.

The bad.

title: Work great! Can barely hear my children!
text:
In the days of shelter in place with a 4 and 6 year old, I was desperate for a little quiet. Even with my door shut and earbuds in, I could hear them arguing and their elephant feet stomping around the house.

These work great. They aren't so noise cancelling that I'm senseless of my surroundings, but they eliminate and muffle plenty. They eliminate nearly all road noise, and mute the kids and their movie in the car. I can clean the kitchen or work in peace, without overhearing all the "momma! momma!" they pretend whine when they play babies.

The first pair I had, one of the earphones had an odd pulsing. It wasn't a sound so much as a pressure. Like ears popping but softer. They helped me troubleshoot some options, but when those failed, they sent a new pair without any hassle. These soundcore headphone shave made 5 months home with my noisy children bearable!

Turns out that when the headphones are on my head, the way that they adjust causes the hinge to put undo pressure on the outside plastic and thus weakens it to the point of it breaking. I still have full functionality which works for me but makes me wonder if sometime soon the hin

title: High Quality Sound
text:
I'm a hardcore music lover. I don't cheap out when it comes to sound equipment. These headphones offer high quality sound and effective noise cancellation. You get 40 hours of wireless use. Bass tones are enhanced with the BassUp technology. A handy travel kit is included. It's easy to fold the headphones and toss them in any bag. They work with Bluetooth and a normal aux cable. A micro USB cable is also included. If you travel frequently or like to use headphones at work, these are perfect for reducing ambient sound. The ear cups are ultra soft and squishy. They're ideal for those with sensitive skin or for extended wear. These headphones are pretty awesome. If you're a stickler for great sound quality, I highly recommend them.

Thanks so much for taking the time to read this review. I hope the information that I've provided makes your decision a little easier.
