Pros: They’re built better than other headphones in their price range, and have great sound quality for the price. Swiveling ear cups make for a good fit.

Background voices and TV sounds are barely muted, and general ambient background noise is still noticeable, just dampened slightly. title: PS5 Owners Beware
text:
If this works with PS5, it’s not trivial to pair it. My guess is I need to spend more money on an adapter. Not sure if that will work.

**25-APR-2022 Update** I was contacted by customer support and they have been very helpful in offering to help and/or assist with a full refund. That said, I stand by my statement that anyone purchasing this to work wirelessly with a PS5; beware. I am in the process of exploring adapters to achieve this, but I do not believe this product will work (sound and voice) with a PS5 out of the box. title: Comfortable. Noise cancellation
text: The price was great. I don't have a good expensive ANC headphones to compare, but this ANC works. For example it made the dishwasher go away. Music playing in kitchen on a smart speaker was cancelled out. I could hear peoples voices, so doesn't take out everything. But it took away the machine noises. Music sounded good. They are very comfortable

title: good headphones
text: The noise cancellation works great, the sound is acceptable and the headphones are comfortable. I reduced the rating by one star because I find the connection to my computer is unstable. I can be in the middle of a zoom meeting and all of a study, I have no sound. In all fairness, this may have nothing to do with these headphones but may be a function of my computer or bluetooth. I will say that the noise cancellation is fantastic. My neighbor was using his generator within a few feet of my window and with noise cancellation on, I could barely hear his generator. The sound was loud and obnoxious and I was grateful for the effectiveness of the noise cancellation.

title: Comfortable
text: Good product, does as advertised.  Grandson enjoyed using them on plane and in motel.

title: Cheap Plastic
text: After a little over a month of daily wear, the plastics holding the headpieces on have failed. Entirely on one side and cracked on the other. Cheap materials, don't recommend.

title: Nice for the price.
text: Nice sound, and the battery life has been really good for me.  The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars, was because they are a little too big for my head.  :)

title: Good value and here's why
text:
My sound preferences:
I own the Bose Soundlink Mini, JayBird wireless ear buds and cheaper Anker ear buds-all excellent poducts.
My usage for Q20: travel on airplanes, at home for music or tv via streaming device.

The SoundCore Q20 are solidly built and comfortable. Sound quality is good and there's a bass boost button. Not as good as an equalizer app like JayBird but that's a cost trade-off. In listening to a variety of music I did not feel the Q20s made my listening experience bad. True, they don't have the articulation of Bose where you can hear every plink of an instrument, but overall the sound experience is good, satisfying and full.

NC is okay: they will lower the noise of a dishwasher, for example, to a mild hum instead of an annoyance.

There's no case, just a rubbery bag which has an unpleasant odor, so plan on an extra $13 for a case which is fine because you can choose your preferred configuration of folded or flat layout.

title: Great sound and comfort at great price
text: The sound quality is excellent.  I consider Bose among the best in audio and have owned several sets of Bose headphones.  I feel that the sound quality of these Soundcore Anker headphones is as good as any Bose that I have owned, and at a quarter of the price.  I don’t notice much of a difference between the sound cancellation and regular mode, however I think in large part because the cushions fit so well and do a good job at mechanical isolation.

title: Okay while they lasted (just over 9 months).
text:
These Anker 20's lasted a bit over 9 months. Given that they were not expensive, it's no big loss.

The defect: They stopped working for any usage. Dead, whether plugged in or Blue-toothed. Like the parrot in the Monty Python sketch.

Comfort: My wife has a small head (but big brains). She thought they fit fine. I bought them for me (head sized 7 5/8) and they were like an aural Inquisition. There's nothing plush about them - this isn't a Bose. I can wear most headphones okay - not these.

Sound quality: You get what you pay for. I have $20 JVCs - HAS650 - that sounded about as good. Definitely not in the middle class (Jabra 45) or better class (Sony MDRV6 Studio Monitor).

Noise-cancelling: Slightly detectable. I've thrown in the towel on cheap headphones and have a
Sony WH-1000XM4 coming in. I am hoping that they're worth it.

title: Stopped working
text: 10 months and they stopped working/connecting.

title: Not advisable for airplane travel
text: I read the reviews and head high expectations. I previously I owned Bose noise cancellation headphones. The old saying ‘you get what you pay for’ comes to mind. These headphones especially did not work for me on an airplane. They created an oscillating sound effect, which created pressure on my ear drums. Outside of using them on an airplane or high noise environments, they function OK.

title: Best wireless ANC headphones for the money
text:
I've been using these the last few weeks while traveling for work. They sound amazing with ANC turned off. With ANC turned on they sound slightly worse but still really good. There's an audible white noise with ANC on but you can only hear it when no music is playing. ANC works well but not as good as Bose or Sony. The Bass Up feature provides a crazy amount of bass. I love bass but can't listen to music with this enabled for very long. The headphones have plenty of bass with the feature disabled.

I compared these to the TaoTronics SoundSurge 85 and the Anker sound way better. I returned the TaoTronics.

I wish these had USB type C and they would be perfect.

For Bluetooth ANC headphones these are the best you can get for the money.

title: Worth the money
text:
The battery life is great, the NC works astoundingly well as my first NC phones. The quality in music is there or even for casual use. I weedwack wearing these and it blocks it out well enough for me to enjoy podcasts.

The only con for me was the adjustable head strap isn't firm. We've all had those headphones that grow weak over time and stop holding an adjusted setting that slides open. I'll be outside yard working and they'll come undone ever so slightly that will pinch if I take them off and rest them on my neck. This is hardly a problem for me because they're comfortable when they are on your head. I just figured this review would help people consider something more like 3M's line if they're outside moving around. I'll wear these all night on the computer or take them on a trip.

title: Follow up on Anker Customer Service, March 3, 2022
text:
Was under warranty and Customer Service (Justin) handled replacement ear cups.

Very pleased with product and service.

title: Works great for the $
text: Just as good as the 10yr old $150 Sony over-ear noise cancelling headphones it replaced. So what if it’s not the $500 Bose units, it’s good enough for me. Mainly use it on planes as it allows me to sleep. For those not schooled in noise cancelling it does not eliminate noise. It only cancels out noises that are constant like an airplanes engines. Does not cancel babies or talking. But if you connect these to a music/sound source it will mask the talking and babies.

title: Sound or lack thereof, these deliver
text: The sound quality of these headphones is terrific - one of the best I've heard.  The noise cancellation - or the amount of sound that interferes with your listening from outside sources - is very good.  In fact, the comfortable ear cushions that cover the outer ear work almost as well as when you turn on the NC feature.  I hardly even bother to turn on the NC since the amount of improvement is not substantial.  The only ding in these headphones armor is the fact that the right ear up and quit yesterday for no apparent reason.  I used them to cut the grass the other day and they worked just fine.  I put them on today and the right ear was silent.  Since they were still under warranty, I called the number provided and an Anker rep named Malcom was there to make things right.  He shipped out another pair right away.  That's some good customer relations and service!  I love the headphones and can't wait to have another pair to use soon.

title: Amazing
text: These things are a amazing, especially at this price. I bought these for my 5 hour weekly lawnmower ride, and the noise from the 60” mower almost disappears. In the past I tried using earbuds but the noise was just too much. I can understand those that may not like these because you can’t hear the mower (should something break), but the noise cancellation button allows you to turn that feature on or off. Even with the NC deactivated you can hear the music. They have decent bass, probably a little better then average, but I do wish the ear cups were a little bigger as my ears hurt after a day on the mower. I really couldn’t justify taking a star away in my review even though they’re not perfect. When reviewing a product I feel it important you consider what you paid. I gave these $40 headphones 5 stars, but had I paid $300 they would have gotten 3 stars. You really get what you pay for but for the money these are amazing and you just can’t go wrong.

title: Good Value
text:
If there is a loud noise nearby and noise canceling is enabled, the headphones get staticky. Other than that, there no problems so far with the headphones. They sound good and pair to my phone in under 2 seconds.

Update May 2021: The left ear has stopped working and they no longer pair to phones, laptops, etc. Am recycling. Overall a decent product but its a shame it did not last long. I would give more stars if they did not fall apart after less than 2 years.

title: Anker products have never failed me.
text:
I just received purchase yesterday, so 24 hour, WOW, noise canceling is AMAZING, sound is AWESOME, base everything is spot on. If I things change I will update. Value is great for total quality. I own many Anker products and I'm always happy.

Update: still loving these, travel with them wear them at home.

title: Best value for your money in noise cancelling headphones
text: These are very comfortable and do a good job at blocking out most noise. I had bought a pair of Sony NC headphones for $150 on sale and I was not happy with their overall sound, so I returned them. These have much better sound quality. Amazing battery life.

title: worth the low price for the quality
text: So far so good. The audio quality is good. The battery last a long time. This doesn't give a thumping base but I was looking for an above average bluetooth headset so I'd stop accidentally choking myself on wires and this is it. I've never tried fancy headsets like Bose or Beats so I can't say how those compare. I don't have the money and I don't think they're worth it. This sounds better than my previous wired earbuds, blocks out noise, allows me to walk away from my device, and has a great battery life. The functions on the headset are easy to control so that's also a plus. It serves me well. Worth the price for sure.

title: Excellent quality, creates a relaxing escape!
text: I love these headphones!  My 13 year-old loves them, too!  We are buying another pair for my husband.  First of all, they are very light and soft, making them very comfortable.  The noise canceling quality is excellent-especially considering the price point.  These are not 100% noise cancelling, but they cut out what I would say is 85-90% of surrounding noises.  I am impressed with how long the charge holds.  I use them over the course of days, with about an hour or so of use per day, and I don’t have to charge them daily, just every few days.  I love how they fold, and do suggest a hard case.  I bought the one offered on Amazon and it works perfectly.  The microphone is excellent, and my phone calls are clear and I am heard well.  I also use these to listen to music or the news while I cook and with the noise-cancelling ability, it feels as though I am all alone despite the kids, dogs and noise.  These provide a wonderful “escape” for me.  I love the Anker brand’s consistent high qual

title: Stupid long battery life
text: I initially bought these for a trip I took last December. The last time I charged these was on xmas day. Since then I use them on my lunch break at work to listen to podcasts ( 30 mins a day mon - fri) As of last friday 1/31 the battery life is still at 80%.

title: Great sound and noise cancellation, but plastic has already broken
text: These headphones are fairly comfortable and produce a good quality sound - deep bass and high treble, good active noise cancellation that cuts down on external low-frequencies.  My only problem with it is the plastic piece that is at the end of each side of the faux-leather headband part.  One of them broke off entirely, and the other is on it's way.  This doesn't really interfere with the functionality of the headphones, at least not at this time, but it does make me nervours that other parts of plastic will eventually break as well.  I'm not super gentle with my headphones, so if you are, this may never happen to you.  They've probably slipped off my bed a few times and fallen on the floor from a couple feet up, which is probably what caused this.  But for the plastic pieces I mention are either at a particular angle or are made with a particularly brittle type of plastic such that they would be the only parts of the headphone effected - at least so far.  If this is all that breaks,

title: just OK
text: Not the best but then they didn't cost a lot. Also not very comfortable. Better than another type i have by a long shot but still nothing like the Bose.

title: Will not charge
text: They sound really good but I  only  had them for a couple months and used them a few times.  The second time I tried to charge them they will not charge anymore.  I  bought them for wireless capabilities so they are useless to me.

title: amazing sound
text: first noise cancelling and amazing. cant beat the price. great for the gym or anywhere. I am stationed in the middle east and these are amazing for drowning out the testing of alarms and other construction noises.

title: They work great
text: They aren't super stylish, but I don't know why that's a thing now to have stylish giant headphones. They work really great, better than my Sony and Bose headphones for the noise cancelling and about the same in terms of audio quality. The Bluetooth works workout issue. They're comfortable to wear. The battery lasts a long time. I haven't tested the mic, but I don't really use that feature much on headphones and my Anker in ear Bluetooth headphones have a solid mic, so I don't know why these wouldn't too. They look a little plasticy, but I don't really care much what they look like because the work so well.  I also don't claim to be a huge audiophile, but to me, they sound good.

title: Wow - great sound and comfort
text: These exceeded expectations in every way.  The comfort is WAY better than my Bose set, and the sound is every bit as good.  I am super impressed with these - they are worth the investment.

title: Great sound, not so great construction (but worth the risk!)
text: The sound is great, connection is fine, noise canceling is very good, but with a few months of normal use (mostly watching TV or videos on my computer), the plastic housing that holds the band together cracked in several places and eventually fell apart, rendering otherwise good speakers useless. I don't have a particularly large head, and never used them outside the home, this was through normal day-to-day use. I know Anker makes good audio products, but this product needs a little more work to make it a little more durable. UPDATE: Anker has excellent customer service. They sent me a replacement, unfortunately the replacement died after one use. It just did not turn on, despite being fully charged, I have no idea why, so I'll just chalk that up to being a fluke. What did they do after that? They sent me another pair! (with the caveat that I send the non-working pair back so that they can have their QC people have a look-see). The third pair works like a charm, and just like above, th

title: Decent....
text:
....value for the money, good sound quality , acceptable noise cancellation.
Truly unacceptable, tough, is the fact that you need to provide an email address
(personal info!) to use the app!

title: Fine sound, too big though
text: The sound is fine for the price, but the headphones are so large they fall off my ears.  I think I have a normal sized head.

title: Kinda Blown Away
text:
First, I’m no audiophile. Second, this is my first pair of ANC earphones. That said, I’m very impressed and think this is an amazing set of earphones for the price.

The first thing I did when I got them (after pairing them) was try out the noise cancellation feature. I happened to have a lot of laundry running so I went in my laundry room (closet, really), closed the door, and tried them out. The noise in the room when from a loud rumble to an almost imperceptible hum. Really amazing. I turned off the ANC just to make sure it wasn’t just noise isolation and it wasn’t, though the noise isolation is good, the ANC is what really does the magic. I’ve tried these on plane trips as well, and they do a very good job; sitting at a window seat (where it’s loudest) the din of the plane went from a loud, low ruble to a higher pitched soft rumble - not quite a hiss but perhaps what one might get from a white noise maker. Not bad for noise cancellation alone, but when listening to or vie

title: Anker stands behind the warranty
text: I've bought Anker products for ages because of the quality and price. I have been really happy with these headphones but I am posting this review because of their great customer service. The headphones have an 18 month warranty and recently I noticed that the ear cup had split on one side after 16 months of use during the COVID-19 pandemic and sharing a home office space. Anker sent replacement ear cups in a matter of days.

title: Nice when it works!
text: Would not sync to bluetooth  on TV but playing music from phone great and they are well built and nice to look at only the tv problem which is the reason I bought them for would have keep.

title: They’re ok.
text: Good head phones but they fall off your head constantly so it gets irritating.

title: Never worked for me - Mac User
text:
Not sure why - but could never get these things working.
I have a PhD in engineering from UCLA. .. so it shouldn't be this hard.
probably a compatibility issue with the new MacBook Pros - lots of issues there.

title: Great set of headphones
text: All I can say is WOW! The sound on these headphones are excellent and has plenty of bass. The noise canceling also works very well. When it's on, I can't hear my wife. ;-) It allows you to fully enjoy what you're listening to. As for comfort, I can't say you forget you're wearing the, but you won't mind having these on for an extended period of time. I didn't suffer  the discomfort I felt with other brands after a while. I was able to use them all day, which also says something about the battery life. I highly recommend these headphones.

title: Great if you like pictures as instructions
text: How much extra is it for a company to print the instructions for use using words rather than pictures? Had to go to reviews and questions to find out answers such as why a red light for charging was not appearing.  This took so much time to scroll through Amazon’s Q&A’s and reviews I wouldn’t purchase again. Why did I learn to read if companies think pictures suffice?

title: Excellent Product
text: Excellent quality of noice cancellation and sound.  I was also very impressed with the life of the battery, lasting longer than any other wireless  product I’ve used. And for the price, compared to other wireless headphones, this is a no brainer.

title: Amazing Product, but Just "Good" Noise Cancelation
text:
The build quality, sound quality, wireless connection strength, and battery life of these headphones is incredible. I think that they are the best value headphones I've every bought. They sounds as good as my Senn HD280s.

The only downside is that the active noise cancellation doesn't work nearly as well as some systems I've tested (Here One Earbuds, Airpod Pro, Bose QC), but does cut out most consistent noise.

title: Good enough for me....I don't need Beats.
text: They work great.  I wish I didn't have to pay extra for a color, which I was too thrifty to do, but I would have liked.

title: This product doesn't last
text: I dont trust this product line of headphones! This is my second replacement due to poor materials and construction. First pair lasted a few months then the headphone covers came  off! The replacement broke apart at the swivel assembly while wearing around my neck! Very thin plastic parts and cheap design engineering. Sure, they sound good and Anker has an 18 month warranty, but im tiring of getting replacements that WILL break with daily use.

title: Love these headphones
text: To be fair, I haven’t used a “proper” set of noise canceling over-the-ear headphones before, so I don’t have a good comparison. I will say that when I put them on and then power them, the environmental noise is immediately and dramatically reduced. The sound quality is very nice. Again, I’m not a sound engineer, but I feel the range of tones is great. Nice full sound, noise suppression, and light weight. And it doesn’t make my ears sweat even after a few hours of wearing them.

title: Best in this price range !
text: For the price point- best all around— not perfect noise cancellation- but enuf to concentrate! Sound quality- crisp. Volume control easy to work. You can pay $300-400 elsewhere... but why?

title: Loves them
text: Bought as a gift for my teenager. He really likes them, sound quality is good, crisp. The noise cancellation isn't as good as others he's had. However, the quality is great for the price. Battery life is excellent! He wears these about 70% of his day.

title: The Best I've Used!
text:
Wow! I was impressed by these headphones. Not only do they have clear sound, but they are comfortable for longer listening times. They make listening to something easy even when the train in the valley is going by, or the trash trucks are at work.
I don't usually use the noise cancellation since the headphones cancel a lot of noise anyway, but turning on the noise cancellation was astonishing! It cut out so much noise that I didn't know when the AC kicked on and wondered why I suddenly felt my chair vibrate just a little. I can completely understand why there are cautions about using the noise cancellation at times because you really can't hear much!
I charged my headphones one week, and it is still fully charged a week later. Of course I don't listen all the time or for hours on end, but it's great that I don't have to charge them all the time.
One thing I really like about these, besides the comfort and noise blocking, is the fact that they are wireless. I like to be a

title: Great product except
text: Kinda starts hurting our ears after while.

title: Awesome sound.....
text: Awesome sound! I can actually hear all the instruments in the music 🎶🎵 now!

title: Great headphones for the price
text: Gift for granddaughter. Great quality for the price. Noise canceling for school with good sound quality for movies, music. Would buy again for me!

title: Great value
text: Great value.  Sounds good.  Bass sometimes is muddy but overall worth buying.  Maybe, buy two.

title: It's good enough for me
text: Unit takes a long time to recharge. I had it plugged into the usb port on my computer and after about 3 hours it still hadn't recharged.  I finally just plugged into a wall plug.  Took about 6 hours to charge.  I am not sure how the sound cancelling works, but I had construction going on in my house and I put the headphones on to reduce noise, but it didn't seem to work that well.  My cheapy sound suppressors that I use when working with my chain saw seem to work just as well.  I've listened to some videos on youtube and facebook and in that capacity the headphone works fine for me.

title: Bose are better
text: Ended up returning them because of the smell and cheap feel. Spend a little more to get Bose. These are just okay but it looks like it would break easily.

title: Awesome value for product
text: Awesome headphones! Noise cancellation works a bit too well for office use lol. Definitely recommend.

title: May have received a defective one
text: Prior headphones were wired Bose. Bought based on the positive reviews, but returned due to sound quality. Tried to improve via the iPhone sound adjustments (the Anker ap does not work with the Q20), but little improvement. Treble and mid range clarity/accuracy were poor. Noise cancellation better than expected. Comfort and build quality are very good.

title: Buy it
text: Great buy for the money. Light weight and perfect for long airplane trips.

title: Good headphones
text: Overall I am satisfied with these. They are comfortable and sound pretty good. The look nice. They fold flat for storage. They come with a bag for storage which is okay but will not protect them. They don't connect smoothly and powering them on is a little glitchy but not a major hindrance. Overall these are good. I have had better and I have had much worse.

title: Great
text: Great for price the noise cancelling is as good as any I'm sensitive

title: I get a lot of Popping sound when I walk
text: They are fine if you are sitting down.  But if you walk with them, the noise cancelling causes a lot of popping sounds...not sure why?

title: Much better than my old headphones!
text:
These headphones are great!

They feel fairly light, compared to my old, heavy pair, but not as light as my bose wired, non-bluetooth, non noise-canceling pair.

These have a feature that I really like: when I turn noise canceling (NC) on, the volume automatically goes down a bit. When I turn NC back off, the volume goes back up. This makes it so if I enter a loud area (such as a bus) and turn on NC, the headphones will block out a lot of the noise, but my eardrums are safe from spontaneous explosion (A bit of an exaggeration, I know, but I do have sensitive hearing, and having that sudden noise level difference can suck). I do have to adjust the volume a little, but that is not a big deal for me.

I also love that when I turn the headphones off, the voice telling me it's off is quiet enough that I can be wearing the headphones without having my ears blasted. With my old pair, I had to remember to take them off before turning them on/off, or when connectin

title: Drowns out gym clutter
text: I wear these at the gym, and don't realize how loud it is in there until I take these off at the end of my workout. Connects to my Samsung phone nicely and sounds great.

title: Surprisingly good for price!
text: These are great! I had modest expectations because of the cost, but these have far exceeded my expectations.

title: Too many issue for people trying to hear me
text:
I recently bought a few types of headphones for various different reasons. I was actually most excited about these. As someone who has a handful different Anker products I have never been let down by them..... until now.

These look amazing. They have a really nice build that is great fitting. Super adjustable. Fits all hat sizes from children to size 8 very comfortably. Nice ear pads that rotate, everything you could want in design.

I attached them to my computer via Bluetooth (super easy) and tried out videoconferencing. I can hear the people on the other end (we tested in the same house a hundred feet away) awesomely - but they hear cracking noises every few seconds so they can only make out every other word essentially. I go to switch places with them and yep, cracking noises. You can’t make out the person on the other end really.

Okay I think, let’s try using with the phone instead. I plug back in to charge (even though it was fully charged already)

title: Pleasantly surprised!
text: I’m very happy with these headphones. Sound great to my old ears and my first encounter with ANC; OMG, why haven’t I had these earlier!

title: Muffled
text: Sound quality is ok, but kind of muffled compared to other headphones. I have a Soundcore bluetooth speaker that has a similar quality.

title: Great lower cost option
text: Really good noise cancelling for the price.

title: Been looking for these!
text:
I've been looking for comfortable noise cancelling headphones without breaking the wallet and finally found it! I bought these on a sale for <$40. I have to say, one of the best investments. Even if the noise cancellation isn't that great, I can't complain but I got more than I bargained for. Cancellation is unbelievable and sound quality is crisp. Trebles are high and the bass rocks.

Buttons to on both sides work as expected and responsible. The padding is soft but if you leave them on for too long in warm weather, it's expected you may be a little sweaty. Battery life has been tested for 10 hours without any problems with noise cancellation while sleeping or needed to tune out sound. I have yet to run out of battery nor get an alert that I'm low.

Would've bought another as a gift if I knew...next time.

title: Not for me
text: So they are comfortable, and light weight, work wireless or wired. I have read you can't charge and use them but I have not tested that. I had borrowed a friends Bose QC25 for a day to test out noise cancelling vs my dog/kid. I didn't test those with these but the Bose noise cancelling were dead silent with nothing else going on these have like a low wind rustling sound to me, or like a highway far away. So I will be returning them. If you listen to music (and not have a book read to you like what I doing for school) they would be great. I will not be able to use these though. I do like the comfort, I did not test the blutooth because I don't have that function on the laptop which the books are on. I did not care for the sound quality of reading though in comparison.

title: Not enough noise cancellation
text: The sound quality is decent for the price. However, I wish I had used these a bit more before my return window ended, because the noise cancelling is not great. It softens sounds but you can still hear them.

title: Nice Headset - when it stays connected
text: This headset works well, however it has the hardest time staying connected after a couple months of owning and using it for gaming on the weekends. It's incredibly frustrating, and I worry that the second one we purchased after liking the first one so much will soon have the same problem. If you're willing to take a gamble on the bluetooth function, get this headset (it is fine otherwise) but steer clear if you want consistent results.

title: Fantastic!
text: Bought these mainly for airplane movie time. I wanted to eliminate background noise. Jet noise, kids, babies screaming, etc...These delivered big time. Superb sound including eyeball rattling bass. I couldn't be happier.

title: Justin with Customer services is great

title: Anger Q20 headphones
text: Great price, delivery and sound, noice cancellation is very good! Would recommend!

title: The best headphones I ever had.
text: These headphones are excellent, and the noise cancellation works well too. They last for a long time, like six hours, and I wear them comfortably for hours without discomfort.

title: Worth it
text: For the price you’re not gonna find much better. The whole family uses them even our 7 year old and we all love them.

title: Worked 2 days piece junk

title: Not what I was hoping for.
text: Easy to connect to Bluetooth. Sound quality is okay. I am returning to seller because of the fit. The ear piece flexes way out and it looks silly on. I think the quality of the earphones is crappy. Also had to contact amazon customer service because when I went to get a return label they were going to charge me 7dollars for shipping it back. amazon covered that for me, thankfully.

title: 10/10 Recommend if you wear glasses
text:
Very happy with these.

Pros:
- Sound quality is decent. No complaints from someone that listens to a lot of pop/edm
- Good at cancelling out noise (awesome office headphones)
- Comfy with glasses. Had to return my last pair of wireless headphones because they'd press against my glasses and cause pain. These don't do that, they're cushioned enough to be comfy for hours.
- The battery life is great - hours of charge (great for flights/work)
- Lovely rose gold/white color
- The fact that they fold down. It's prob not good for the headphones but you can fold them onto the strap of a carry-on bag while going through TSA.
- They come in a nice little hard carrying case, too.

Cons:
- As much as I love them, I do think they're more of a $20 item you'd prob get at TJMaxx or Ross, not close to $30 (but they've def been work $27 imo)
- White padding gets dirty easily
- Charging cable it comes with is super short (expect to us

title: My daughter loves these HD headphones!
text: My daughter loves these!  Comes with the case, charger, about 19” usb cord, Aux cord, instructions, warranty info., and a thank you card.  Wrapped securely and just perfect. Even a little clip on the case to clip on a gym bag or whatever.  I can’t stop rubbing the case!  Extremely durable and soft.  The sound is superb, maybe just me, however I tried these out and these sound like I am in a room with surround sound!  No joke!  Came with a slight charge to test and so glad I didn’t have to charge first then try these out before giving to my girl.  Can’t beat the price for the value of this product!  I actually just ordered myself one for the gym!  Thank you so much and this was a lightening deal and so glad that it was or I wouldn’t have been actually looking for headphones!  Can’t wait for mine!

title: Garbage audio and battery.
text: Bought these for the gym. The audio quality is terrible. Luckily I listen to mostly podcasts at the gym, for that the audio is great. For actual music listening these are garbage. Worked great for a month but after that will no longer hold a charge. Spend an extra $20 and get some better ones.

title: Very good headset for the money!
text: [[VIDEOID:6e528fbc75070223b16932e017da7010]] This headset is very comfortable and has great sound for the price. The Bluetooth was extremely easy to set up and use, no drops or interference at all. My only struggles have been the battery life and volume control on the aide of the war piece. I tend to bump the function button instead of volume control but as I said in my video that may be just my own struggle. 😊 The battery life really could be better, it lasts a full eight hour work day IF you are not on calls/in meetings all day. It seems to do best with about six hours of use. I recharge every night and have lost use a couple times in late afternoon meetings. Overall, absolutely worth the money and I would purchase again.

title: Excellent
text: I’ve gone through a few pairs of earbuds and finally decided to go for over the ear headphones. I am so pleased with these! They’re comfortable, they stay connected and the sound is GREAT! Amazing bass! They were comfy at the gym and laying in ed watching tv on my iPad! For $25 I’d highly recommend these for an inexpensive, quality sound headphone option. The only thing I’d say is that they are not completely noise cancellingn but they’re good enough for my use.

title: more about features than audio quality
text: These headphones have a surprising number of features, but the audio quality is not what I would consider acceptable.  The real issue is that the sound is missing mid to high end clarity, which is why my rating was 2 stars.  I purchased these mainly for the bluetooth functionality, and they connected to my computer with no issue.  It was also impressive being able to tune in FM radio stations with just a few clicks, and the reception was adequate, but not without some static.  There is also an SD card slot that I've not tested yet.  I appreciate the ambition in the feature set, but I would prefer a better sounding set of bluetooth headphones regardless of any additional features like an SD Card slot and FM radio.

title: Happy with these headphones!
text:
I got these for work - I like to listen to music while I work, and I was tired of running over the cord of my headphones, getting it tangled in my chair. A couple of times, I knocked over my iPad.

Excellent sound quality. Excellent noise reduction. Work at a fairly good distance from device. I walked out of my office suite, down the hall - still had clear music . When I got to the ladies room, it started curring in and out. That's a pretty good distance from my desk, so these give me good mobility around the office while still enjoying music.

title: You get what you pay for...sometimes
text:
I've had at least 6 pair of these Power Locus headphones now and some have been great, most have had issues. Three pair broke shortly after I got them but the others are holding up well. Some have had excellent sound quality and others have an annoying echo, especially with the on/off voice pre-programmed in. Still, for the money, these seem to be the best deal.

Oh yeah, sometimes some pairs have had a hard time maintaining connection and will play static mixed with the sound. I thought it might be my computer but they do it with my phone and TV as well. It doesn't happen all of the time, though. That makes me think they're getting interference from another device in, or near, the house.

text:

I've tried resetting the watch, unlinking it from the app, turning the watch off for prolonged periods...nothing works, it's just broken. Thankfully, I have the watch set to wrist sense, so it still functions, for now, but the on-screen buttons are easy to accidentally push and turn features on and off, and once I accidentally turn wrist sense off, the watch will no longer be functional.

This is a piece of junk, so look elsewhere for your smartwatch needs!

title: Good for an estimate of activity, but not accurate.  Also loses 2-3 mins a week if not synced.
text:
This is a basic watch that has some flaws.

Pros:

> Easy to read large numbers for the time
> Simple to swipe between screens to access information
> excellent battery life
> heart rate monitor appears accurate
> can notify you of incoming texts when linked to your phone (if you have a device that is supported)

Cons:

> Loses 2-3 minutes a week if not synced regularly with the app. It’s very annoying when the watch says it’s 9:57am when it is really 10:00am. It’s made me late to work a few times!
> Sometimes records sitting in a moving vehicle, especially in slow moving traffic, as exercise.

Summary:

If you want accuracy, this is probably not the watch for you. If you need a basic idea of how much you’re moving, don’t spend time in slow traffic, and can remember to sync it regularly to the app to keep the time accurate, then it’s an affordable alternative to name brand fitness watches.

title: Inconsistent and innacurate GPS
text:
I really wanted to like this watch. I trail run ultra marathon distances and have been looking for an inexpensive GPS watch to track my workouts and races. Comparisons were tested against Apple Watch Series 2, iPhone 7, and Garmin 935.

Pros:
- Battery life is legitimately great.
- Integration with iOS seemed great.
- Heart rate tracking seemed accurate, by comparison.
- Screen was always easy for me to read.

Cons:
- Inconsistent GPS tracking: Over several runs of the same 4 mile trail route my distance would be anywhere from .5 mile short to an entire mile too long.
-- On a 20 mile trail run my distance was .5 mile short, but elevation recorded was 2k ft more than reality.
- Inconsistent activity information synced between watch and iPhone app: On that same run my watch activity read 19.5 miles, but the MiFit app read 19 miles. ??!! When I tried to export to AmazTools the .gpx file only shows 12.7 miles. :/

Conclusion:

title: Way better than I expected.
text:
[Updates to the review in regards to Battery Life, Steps Counter and Sleep Tracker]
I have to admit that my expectations for the Amazfit Bip were quite low, especially after using a Gear S3 Frontier watch for the past six months. But I really wanted to escape having to charge my watch every three or four days, and if I forgot to charge my watch on Day 4, I could not make it all through the workday on Day 5. The Amazfit Bip's promise of 6 to 10 times the battery life of my previous smartwatch enticed me to give it a try.

The Amazfit Bip has almost everything the average user needs from a smartwatch, and very little of what they don't.

PROS:
* Screen: Yes, it isn't a flashy AMOLED screen, but it is always on and perfectly readable outdoors. There's also a good backlight for use in darker locations. I take functionality over appearance any day, and the Amazfit Bip delivers in that department.
* Lightweight: This watch doesn't feel like it's slowing you

title: Just what I've been looking for
text: Hello smart watch companies, THIS is what you should be making!  I can't understand today's smartwatches,; super expensive, hardly on at all unless you move your wrist 'just right' and you have to charge it every night.  This one has none of that. On all the time (reflective LCD), less than $100, and as I write this it's been 2 weeks since I charged it last and it's still at 78 percent charge.  Even has a compass mode!  Downsides?  Minor in my opinion but here goes.  Watch band is a little too short (and I have a small wrist), the reflective display is hard to see in the dark, when you activate the backlight it washes out the colors.  One thing about setup, be patient.  Took me a bit to get it going had to remove other Bluetooth connections on my phone, but put them back afterward and all is fine. Overall I couldn't be happier with this watch.  Oh and you'll need to download a couple apps to get other watchfaces.

title: Great for the price
text: No way to compare data collected, but sleep statistics seem reasonably accurate, steps are a bit off because I talk with hands, and GPS is a bit slow to engage. Had to download a watch face since provided ones are either too small and crowded to read in daylight, have too little information and force you to swipe for usable data, or are simply hard to read due to dainty graphics. Considering the discounted price I paid, I am satisfied. Had a competing, more expensive product performed in a similar way I would likely be far less satisfied. For the price it's fine.

title: The watch is great; the app is not.
text:
The watch is good, guys, but the app is not. It’s not user-friendly. If you do any kind of workouts other than running or cycling (indoor or outdoor), there is no way to add that to your app. Since the MiFit app isn’t compatible with ANY other app, this forces you to log everything in two different apps every time. Just save up the price of this watch for two months and buy a Fitbit or a Garmin. You’ll be MUCH happier.

UPDATE: after I returned my Bip and got my money back, I put it toward getting a Garmin Vivoactice 3, and friends, the Garmin app is SO much better than MiiFit. Just get a Garmin or a Fitbit. You won’t regret it. I’ve had my Garmin for about a week now, and I am so much happier and less frustrated with my fitness tracking 👌🏿

title: Not so impressed anymore
text:
UPDATE: Less than a year into it, the watch developed a glitch, where only parts of the screen are being redrawn. Not sure if it's a faulty hardware, or if a software update broke it, but it had become unusable. The support has not been helpful: suggested full reset, which I performed without any improvement. Dropping from 5 stars to 1.

After a few years with an Android Wear watch, I was skeptical of a $67 watch with a proprietary OS being any better. Yet, five days in, it had been rock solid. Notifications arrive immediately, battery, while not holding as much as advertised (I suspect because of a custom watch face I'm using) is still far superior to anything else out there. It's light and comfortable.

The only thing that I wish was customizable is the font for notifications.

title: Garmin Instinct - weekend review.  Replaces my Pebble 2 and Apple Watch Series 4

I used a GShock for years and loved it. I graduated to my first smart watch with the original Apple Watch. I had a love/hate relationship with it needing to be charged daily, the always-off screen, overall slowness, to name a few annoyances. It was just really cool all the “things” it could do.

I left it for a Pebble 2 and really loved that watch for about 18 months until the buttons crumbled. I started using a backup Pebble 2 but I knew these watches had a very short lifespan. I tried a Ticwatch and an Amazfit Bip. Both were dreadfully disappointing so I went to an Apple Watch Series 3. It was more of the original Apple Watch but faster and a more mature operating system. I just recently upgraded again to the Apple Watch Series 4, which was again more of the same thing but a faster

title: A GOOD BUY. This is a survival smartwatch in a traditional watch body. Really great.
text:
Summary:
I was looking for a Garmin watch to use with Garmin app workout group. I order several different models, including the vivoactive 3. The Garmin instinct is the one I chose. It doesn't look as fancy as the Fenix models, but the Instinct is so much better as a practical smartwatch for the price. The Vivoactive 3 is terrible compared to the functionality and durability of the Instinct. The Instinct does not have a fancy touch screen, but it makes up for it in terms of features. This is a survival smart watch. The GPS compass features are fantastic.
I have a smaller wrist 18 cm, but the watch looks fine. Although it is not as thin as the other Garmin models, it is light enough to work out with. In fact, it is surprisingly lightweight. It takes some time to navigate through the menus because it is not a touchscreen, but it is so much more of a durable feel because its not one. Although it is not a color screen, it has a high contrast backlight feature that can be changed.

title: Great watch - Garmin support is awesome
text:
The watch that I received from Amazon was the Asian version, not the US version. Each time I connected the watch to my laptop, it stopped syncing to my phone's Garmin Connect app and all data collection seemed to stop. I had to uninstall and reinstall the Garmin Connect app on my phone (and I had to restart the watch) in order for it to restart syncing.

Garmin Support
Garmin support is great! They quickly identified that it was the wrong model and promptly shipped a replacement (US version). I had no issues with the replacement watch. Support indicated that the Asian version takes longer to update and sync on US based phones (not sure why - but the US model certainly works better).

The Watch
I wanted an outdoor activity tracker without having to look at and learn yet another screen. The combination of this watch and the Garmin Connect app for my phone hits the mark. Compass, altimeter, barometer and GPS tracking all provide important data for outd

title: Simpler Garmin GPS Watch with Retro Looks!
text:
Other reviewers have covered the ins/outs of this watch in great detail.

I just would like to re-emphasize that this watch is essentially a simpler version of the higher end Garmin Fenix, Tactix, and other expensive Garmin GPS watches. As amazing as those higher end units are, any honest person will tell you they can also be quite overwhelming and "overkill" for the average person.

The Garmin Instinct is much simpler to use, and the screen works surprisingly well. That "Casio-retro" looking screen works perfectly outdoors when it's super bright outside. If you want a simple, good GPS watch with accurate sensors, the Instinct is the perfect option. Think of it as Garmin's version of what a Casio GShock on steroids should look like.

However, if you are a dedicated athlete or Army/USMC infantryman, etc., then the higher end units might be a better idea because you would benefit from the extra functions those expensive watches provide for those environme

2. I can easily monitor notifications and text messages at a glance. When a notification, text, or email comes in, the watch vibrates and I can easily see what it is at a glance. If I decide to read the full text, email, or whatever, it's easy to scroll down and read

title: Great Battery Life!

title: Great smart watch! Beware wrong region versions
text: I love this watch. I love the simplicity, great battery life, light weight and modesty of it. I can't really add anything to other positive reviews. One thing to be aware of though - if you notice when pairing that the device's default name is "Instinct APAC" then you have received the Asia-Pacific version of the watch, which isn't any different than the US version EXCEPT that it will not receive software updates nearly as quickly as the US version. The first Instinct I received was an APAC version and stuck on software 2.9, even though 4.0 had been released! It was even sold by and fulfilled by Amazon, so beware!

title: Really like some of it

title: The smartwatch for Casio
text: I have tried 4 watches. The tic watch, the powerwatch 1 & 2, and this watch. I returned all three except this one. Was looking for a workout watch and a hikers watch. I wanted a smart watch with the capabilities of a casio Pathfinder and this is it. Compass, run track, workout track, sleep tracker. Great app interface. Great battery life (haven't charged it for a week and it's at 75%. I have used the GPS for 3 days an hour a day. I also disabled the push notifications for emails and messages).

title: Well built solid device but with a steep learning curve.
text: I used this watch to replace a Fitbit that failed. And the Fitbit replaced a Samsung Gear that failed twice. All failuers were just after the warranty ran out. So to say that these trackers are cheaply made is an understatement. So far the Garmin has been solid. All of the functions work well, even the GPS tracking. The weak spot with the device has been the lack of clear DETAILED instructions how to navigate the functions to get to perform as designed. With 5 buttons on the device propper pushing in a specific order is critical. The documentation is vague and only gives general instructions on each buttons function. That leaves searching U tube for videos created by users that do poor job explaining the processes. So my biggest complaint is the steep learning curve of manipulation to achieve a desired goal. So the watch works well, if you have the time and patience to navigate the buttons and menus to make it work.

title: The Single Best Adventure/Smartwatch I've Ever Owned.  Period.
text:
This watch tells me everything I need. After learning how to navigate around the various screens (there's still stuff to learn) I've found it to be incredibly useful, tells me gobs of data, and encourages me to be more active/healthier. It also keeps me in touch as I need to since it ties well with my phone.

The display is nice, high contrast. The backlight is absolutely outstanding (and adjustable) so that I can look at it even in the middle of the night when I wake up and not ruin my night vision but still see very clearly. The 'flashlight' function is incredibly useful. The ability to customize each screen to tell you information that YOU want to see is just awesome.

These are minor things I *wish* but absolutely don't find as a deal breaker:

-The only way to charge it is with the proprietary charging cable instead of just a wireless charging stand. I've found some little USB adaptors so I can attach it to any USB-C cable anywhere, but it'd be

title: Now owned over one year
text:
Works good enough I guess. For the price I paid for it, I would like it to work better. I had a Garmin Vivasmart HR and it was spooky on it's accuracy. Then I through more money at Garmin and I get disappointed. Sleep tracking is below par, steps counts are all over the place.
I do like the battery life, the notifications from my phone. I've use the gps function a lot to and it does very well. As for the other tracking features, I have not used them. I'm old and not that active anymore.
Also, now issues with the band. If I feel a some soreness coming on under the band, I loosen one notch for a day or two, but that rarely happens. Also I use a permanent black marker to mark the hole I like to use so I'm not guessing where I like it.

title: Best Tactical Watch
text: This watch is amazing. The sleep tracking feature is incredible.

title: Progress toward the ideal, but a long way to go
text:
I bought this as a replacement for a Garmin Montana 650, whose waterproofing has failed. So it's a matter of time .... I didn't need a "big"-screen map, but I wanted an altimeter, which excludes much of the market. The Instinct seemed like the best choice. I wanted to log my activities, capture GPS tracks with time and elevation.

You cannot really use this device without the instruction manual. Read it once when you first get the device. Live with it a week or two, then read the manual again. Live with that for a month, read the manual again.

If you flex your wrist, you will probably press the Stop button and lose your logging. Set up auto-lock of the buttons.

If you set up auto-pause, the device will stop at random and unpredictable times, and (maybe) re-start at random and unpredictable times. This might, or might not, be acceptable, except that everything that happened during the auto-pause interval is lost forever. Disable auto-pause.

You

title: Essentially a Smartwatch G-Shock
text:
It’s basically a smart G-Shock. For active lifestyles this is the way to go. Few tips i have learned:
-turn backlight down as low as you can reasonably tolerate. Mines at 5% and i have pictures posted with it

-download the garmin connect app for all data related stuff

- watch YouTube videos on watch setup, i spent an hour or so the first day setting the watch up the way i like it. (Minimal smart phone notifications, battery saving options, watch face and “Hot keys”) for quick access for workouts

- i do running swimming lifting and hope to bike more so those activities are all easy and fast to get to

- this watch is not an Apple Watch. Its not fancy, it’s mot pretty. It is for active people that want that G-SHOCK look. The fitness and heart rate tracking is superb

-yes works with Apple Watch just fine

If i have cons I’ll link them here.

title: Good watch.
text:
Weather reading is stuck on 46 degrees. And why the he’ll do they bother using space on phone to state “NOW” before the weather reading.?? Of course it’s right now.

That’s the only issue. Def have to read owners manual to learn about watch before using etc.

title: Great watch with some issues.
text:
It does everything I need in a sports watch and very versatile/ programmable. Great battery life. GPS is fairly accurate. Heart rate pretty good most of the time but occasionally it quits reading (drops down to like 60s when it should be more like 120) in the middle of a workout! Nothing I try seems to cure the problem. It only happens occasionally though, otherwise it would make the watch useless as a sports watch as calories and workout intensity are based largely on heart rate. (To be fair, I've owned several other smart watches and they all have had this issue!).

Notifications work good. Nice to answer texts from watch from a list of short pre-installed responses, especially when I'm driving or working up on a ladder!

My biggest negative issue with this watch is that my buttons are getting to where they don't spring back and I can't tell if they are working when I press them. I use my watch every day for workouts, so the buttons get used a lot! I've only had t

title: Perfect smartwatch and GPS watch for me
text: I bought the original Apple Watch new then later upgraded to a series 3. There’s a lot to love about the Apple Watch but I don’t like the 2 day battery life and never used 90% of its capabilities. I like a quick glance to see if a text is important while I’m biking or running, tracking those activities, monitoring my heart rate, and the find my phone feature. The Garmin Instinct does all of that in a much more rugged package with much better battery life. I love the simple black and white screen that’s always on. It’s very light and comfortable. Probably my ONLY criticism is that you can’t turn off notifications by app, if they are on on my iPhone they are on on the watch. This is annoying with my Outlook work emails so I just turn those off most of the day and watch my computer or phone app a bit closer.

title: Love the watch poor positioning of the GPS button
text: I love the watch but there’s one flaw that I have noticed. I wear the watch on my left wrist and the location of the GPS button requires me to keep the watch locked or else the GPS will turn on any time I bend my wrist. Doesn’t seem like that big of a deal until you try to use the timer or the stopwatch and have to take the time to unlock the watch every time you wanna start or stop. Other than that I have no other complaints.

title: A Great Every Day Watch
text: I purchased this watch for the GPS functions and they work fairly good.  My biggest gripe is the menu buttons and selections on the watch.  It's hard to remember which button controls what because there is so many selections.  Other than the confusing menu selections I'm happy with the watch.

title: Not The GPS Watch I Expected
text: I've been using Garmin products since the mid 90's and monochrome screens on devices the size of large mangos. I was excited to get this watch for outdoor activities. I thought that I'd be able to track my location with a breadcrumb trail or at least be able to set a waypoint or two so that if I were hiking/backpacking, I could have some idea where my truck is parked or where the trailhead begins. Also, I haven't found a way to manually enter LAT and LONG, so basically all the GPS capabilities seem to be very limited. Navigation (no pun intended) through the actual menus and options of the watch is also needlessly complicated and counter intuitive. Sometimes a button does one thing, other times it does something else. None of it makes any sense-- and I've been using the watch a year.  I haven't used the watch much for sleep tracking because it's huge and I don't want to sleep with it on. Lastly, I'll say in my defense that I'm usually not so critical of watches, but this one was a huge

title: Bought one for my appliance tech
text:
I love this watch so much, I bought one for one of my alliance repair technicians. His watch broke and was going on a camping trip to Colorado.

This watch is rugged and can be used around appliances because there of no metal conductivity.

He was shocked when I surprised him with it a day before his trip.

He texts me the first couple days and when he got back on how cool all the features are. We even compared our stress levels and body battery depletion.

He also saved some of his stopping points. He did indicate the GPS did not work great without cell coverage and satellite could not pin point his location at times with the gps.

Other than that, he said it's the best watch he has ever had.

Buy one. You won't be disappointed!

title: Works well for windsurfing
text:
Bought it for tracking my windsurfing sessions. It does not have a special app for windsurfing like more expensive garmins, but I was able to add one manually using phone buttons and design my own watch face with current speed showing in largest part of screen, current time, total distance (all I needed). Can easily see speed when planing fast over waves (speeds ~25mph).
GPS works great, but I wish it would have mode that measures every second, in normal mode it skips some when it thinks speed is not changing much.
Pulse meter is pretty much useless when exercising, at least in my case. It works well if your hand is still, but when you shake it or just move around rate jumps by ~20 beats (checked with more reliable finger pulse meter). No matter how tight I have my watch. I guess it has some logic to filter out pulse signal, but since my normal resting pulse is lower than usual (just 45 bmp) perhaps it picks up noise. On the other hand I am not interested in this number at al

title: Sticky GPS key update
text: Still have problems with keys. They stick now and then. I have to hit softly side of watch and they start working again. Other then that great watch. They really have to work on those keys

title: meh...
text: I'll confess, I'm not really a watch person. I bought it mainly for tracking mileage and elevation when I ride my bike. It works fine for that. As far as how it functions for day to day watch use, I don't care for it. Firstly it's enormous. It gets in the way. but far more annoying than that, is when it goes into certain modes because you accidentally hit a button. Driving and it goes into trail running mode and I get notified with a beep and vibrating wrist every mile. I considered tossing it out the window more than once. It will also go into modes due to excessive noise. I play in a band. If it gets too loud the watch starts freaking out and beeping and vibrating. It also loses battery fast when you run it bright. and when you don't run it bright it's pretty impossible to read on the fly which I'm pretty sure is what having this device strapped to your wrist is supposed to be about. not impressed overall considering the brand.

title: Perfect for what I was looking for! Run, swim, dependability
text:
Update: gps open water tracking stopped working even after problem solving. Other options still working.

I wanted some very basic features but wanted them done well:
Track my runs
Track my open water swims
Be waterproof
Decent battery life
Be a regular size, somewhat inconspicuous and not like a Flava Flav clock on my wrist.
I am so happy that this watch delivered on all fronts!
For my runs and swims the GPS has been fast in locating me (20 seconds) although I’m sure this depends on your location. The routes have been accurate as well as I review on the app. So far the watch has held up fine for saltwater swims and tons of sweat from running.
I briefly used some of the other features but eventually turned them off. The heart rate tracker was big time delayed. Maybe 5 minutes after I start working out it registers an increased heart rate. Weird. The sleep tracker was probably fine but I don’t like wearing the watch when I sleep.
I

title: Love it but has pros and cons
text:
I do love this watch. But here's some pros and cons
Pros:
cool look, durable, comfortable to wear, excellent gps accuracy, great heart rate monitoring, love using buttons instead of crappy touchscreens, neat widgets for stress, barometric pressure, the tracking is sweet of youre into hiking and outdoors so you can set your point and record the path, lastly has tons of different activities available to track

Cons:
the sleep doesn't seem too accurate compared to previous Fitbit, I'm big into HIIT circuits, strength training, crossfit, etc. So, using this thing to track a circuit or strength training (i.e. reps, weight, rest time, etc..) is horrible. Very difficult to use and worst of all the booklet that comes with it gives you zero instruction for using this watch other than connecting it to your phone and the Garmin app.

Overall, glad I bought it and I like it a lot. Just wish Garmin made it a little more user friendly for programming/tracking wor

title: It's a keeper!
text: I truly like the Garmin Instinct.  The battery life is great the usability is just right EXCEPT for the fact that the silly thing sometimes loses the connection to my Iphone.  I have to fiddle with settings to get it to reconnect.  In truth, I don't know if the problem is the Garmin or the Iphone.  But I still heartily recommend the unit to anyone looking for a solid smart watch.

title: Great for active work sites.
text: Great watch. I work on industrial machinery, very hands on. Was looking at options to replace my garmin vivosmart hr. This watch is perfect. Very rugged and easy to clean. The display is well thought out layout.

title: A smartwatch that legitimately has two weeks worth of battery? Yes please!
text:
Do you want a smartwatch that has all the vital features of a smartwatch but don't want the hassle of charging it every single night? Then this watch is for you. I have the vibrating alarm set every day, constant heart rate monitoring on, storm alert on, I turned off the auto backlight feature (not really necessary) but I do have the backlight at 100% when pressed, I use the compass multiple times per day, and on average I've had to charge the watch once every two weeks. I've tested the GPS while walking the dog and it's quite accurate but have yet to use it enough to determine how much battery it consumes. I have the watch paired with a Galaxy S22 Ultra and the app is very easy to use and very intuitive.

In conclusion, if you can live without a bright and vibrant display, and you want a smartwatch that has literally every other feature PLUS a battery that lasts two weeks, then this is the watch you're looking for!

title: depends on what you need *****changed to  4 star as its dead after just 2 years and one month
text: I'm not doing any hard training or serious work outs.  Am not doing Geo Caching or wilderness exploring.  What I wanted was to keep track of my general activity AND have a watch that I could read easily without having to tap, press buttons, or anything else.  Am not sure how accurate this is in tracking heart rate or altitude and haven't yet figured out how to use the GPS feature or the exercise tracking features.  GPS interested me as I walk my dog in the early AM (this time of year in the dark with just a head lamp) and when we go off trail its easy to lose landmarks - so being able to figure out my trail would be easier than just wandering around blind through the undergrowth.  and I'll get there in figuring out how it works.  meanwhile, it is doing what I need.  Am finding sleeping with it to be no problem.  the battery is long lasting, but see that is a factor with how often you access the watch's features.  If you let it be and just use the phone app to track your data, it will l

title: Battery won't charge in less than a year!
text: I have had this less than a year, battery will not stay charged for more than an hour, tried contacting the company and they were not available by chat.  Gonna try again by phone.  Will update information if it ends up replaced by warranty.

title: Rugged and dependable but at a cost
text: I loved my first garmin watch. Lasted forever and didn’t really want to switch until this unit came along and I was hooked. Looks great, battery lasts forever, good functions but no touch screen. Worst of all, it would lose connection with my apple products. I’m sorry but I can’t have a product that doesn’t do what it says it will. I wound up getting rid of mine. Unfortunate. Not supposed to go backwards Garmin!

title: Great, except for swimming.
text: Instinct does a lot of cool stuff. I was looking forward to using it for running and swimming. It stinks at swimming.  No heart rate in water and only tracks laps with arm motions.  It won’t track kicking laps and it often misses laps with arm movement. Even breaststroke can mess up the counting.  For road biking it’s too small to use in rides.  I dare say it’s dangerous to look at on rides. But, for rides that you just want to tally but don’t Carr about stats, like to the train, it’s fine.  I bought this because of the Edge25 cycle tracker and all. The 135 blows this away, but having the heart rate on arm and 135 doing its thing you are really wired.  For the battery life, general look, cool gps apps and activity tracking it’s great.  That’s why I’m keeping it. Plus I look at my phone less as it woks very well with iPhone.

title: My grandson loves it!
text: Bought as a birthday present for my college student grandson.  He says it has all the features he needed and that he loves it.

title: very good

title: Great watch!
text: Love this watch... battery life is excellent... I think I might spend the money on the solar version...

title: A darn good watch!
text: This is a durable and very functional watch, but Garmin has to refine the HR monitor!  That part of the watch is just okay.  The rest is kick A@S and yes, I’d buy another one...I fact I am for my wife!

title: Accurate multifunctional sports watch with flaws
text: Works well in most cases, but no autotracking for walking, and voice prompts is in Romanian ( fortunately I understand it), but it is impossible to change the language even after talking with Garmin support. Issues are annoying rather than rendering watch useless.

title: This really does a lot.
text:
Super nice lite weight watch that really has a lot of functions. It has a few basic txt msg responses like ok, can't talk right now and 1 or 2 more. This one does have the ability to download golf courses. You can track your workouts like weight training and treadmill workouts for non GPS. Then you have stuff like run, hike, bike ect... for GPS tracked activities.
You can customize your watch face so you see what's most important to you like date, time, stress, heart rate and several other options.
It has weather, several alarm clocks, music controls, stop watch, GPS grid location save ability.

title: Great battery life. Works as it should.
text: If you want a basic health tracker function and ABC function and you want to charge it every week or two instead of every day or two this is the one for you. The display isn't too impressive but it does it's job well and can't be beat for the price if ABC and GPS is what you need. Not too fancy and built tough.

title: Like the Function, Hate the Color
text: I love everything about this watch, except for the “carbon” color. Three different tones of gray. One tone looks green with certain shirt colors. They need to make an all black version. I returned and forked out the extra for the “tactical” version. I won’t be executing HALOs anytime soon (never), so the additional functions are useless. I basically paid $50 more for all black.

title: My husband really wanted to love this...
text: ... But he cannot. Every day when he grabbed a drink or did any sort of wrist movement it would push the digital crown on the side. He had a 76 hour timer and never knew he started one. There would be days it would ding with notifications and days it didn't ding. It's a beautiful and rugged watch but the digital crown is both too big and in the wrong spot?

title: Great so far!
text: So far it’s been pretty simple to work and be set up. The touch screen feature works well. Looking forward to continuing see how it works, especially the swim feature and checking to see how well and how long it holds up to the water. I like the tightness of the band and the sleek feature of the watch’s face. I haven’t worn it long enough to check the life of the battery and how long it lasts whilst checking out all its additional features. I will be adding an update when I’ve had time to wear the watch a little longer. I’ve liked most of the reviews and based my purchase on what others wrote. Bonus is the extended one year warranty that comes in the package.

title: Great Semi-Smart Watch
text: My SmartWatch was too fragile and I needed something durable and waterproof that would work for phone calls and text messages and other notifications. I lead groups on trips tivities where I often get wet and there is a lot of background noise. I needed something that would notify me a phone calls or text messages from other adult staff or parents and that could handle the water and activities. This has been a very affordable lifesaver.

title: This watch is amazing for the price.
text:
This watch is fantastic for less than $30.00! I love how it has an alarm, water resistant, you can chime your phone from your watch, changing watch faces, get texts and see incoming calls. I am not able to answer the call from my phone but I am impressed with all it does.

I was going to get another type of watch like a fitbit however I saw pictures taken of people who had their wrist burned! Nothing like that with this watch for a 1/4 of the price. Very nice. I'm very happy with it and would highly recommend it.

title: Great Deal
text: I wanted this for the watch & step counter. Actually pretty impressive for the price.  Lots of options for the price. I've had it a few weeks with no issues.

title: Basic Smartwatch

I got this with intent to keep track of heart beats, body temperature, sleep quality, and activity level performance.

The app is great as being able to adjust what you give permissions for (I didn't want to get phone notifications but may have changed that option later), but unfortunately the step

title: Great watch!!
text: This watch has a long battery life, waterproof, easy to charge, fast charge, and overall great watch. Features are pictured and I haven't had any problems with this watch. I love it.

title: Don't waste your money it's really bad

title: Great headphones for average use, wires seem very sturdy & never get tangled
text:
These are great headphones for music and movies for the price. I have never owned a big-money pair of headphones & after buying cheap ones that I had to replace multiple times due to breakage, I decided to get these after a recommendation from a friend. After buying them for myself, I also bought them for each of my kids. They're great for average use... i've never been bothered by outside noise, they are comfortable, and the sound is of good quality. They also are much sturdier than the cheapie $10-$20 ones I've gotten before. (My kids haven't broken them, which is a minor miracle)

I do recommend getting a bag or carrying case for them (I use a Crown Royal bag:) it certainly helps protect them and they do seem vulnerable at the hinges where they collapse for storage.

The only thing my kid complains about is the wire is kind of short. I prefer it because the wire is nice and thick and never gets tangled. with my previous cheap ones, it was always the wires t

title: SERVICEABLE HEADPHONES FOR THE PRICE
text: I've had these headphones for 2 years now up to the date of this review. Inexpensive and adequate but obviously not for the audiophile! Light so they don't hurt your ears after long wear. Dynamic range is within it's price range. A reasonable value for a knock around set of headphones. The vinyl coating over the ear covers has worn off on both sides and the covering flakes off onto you when you wear them. I guess it might have been affected by how I must put them on and off. Still using them and they still provide adequate sound.

title: Terrific Budget Headphones
text:
I got these for $7.99 on a lightning deal. They are excellent for the price. I ordered two pair: one for my 3 year old and one as a travel pair of headphones. The sound quality is impressive for the price. These are on the ear headphones, not over the ear. So, passive noise isolation won't be as good. However, they are quite comfortable. I could easily wear these for a couple hours while watching a movie. The physical size of the headphones make them a good choice for children. Sure, they aren't brightly colored, but I'm willing to bet the sound quality and price are much better than you'd pay for a branded set of crappy headphones. Also, I was surprised at the quality of the cord. I was expecting a thin, cheap cord. It's actually fairly thick and tangle resistant. Only complaint I have -- I wish the cord connected at a single point, rather than running to each ear piece. But, again, for the price they're darn good quality.

Update: I've now used these headphones several time

title: Not meant for big heads
text:
Purchased a set of these a couple years back and used them on and off since then. Sound quality is pretty good, with decent bass. If I want to, I can crank the volume up to '11' on my phone without having the audio quality suffer as a result.

That said, these headphones are by no means noise canceling, or even noise muffeling. I'm currently using them in an office environment, and I can clearly hear conversations being held a couple doors down.
Biggest issue I have with these and the main reason I've finally decided to replace them is their size. While I am able to put them on, they begin to hurt from the constant pressure they apply over time. I do not have an abnormally large head, bit these things become uncomfortable after a while. I do not have this issue with other headphones I own. Only reason these have lasted as long as they have, is because I really wasn't using them for any extended period of time, until a couple weeks ago. The previous set of office headphon

title: Lots of bang for not much buck!!
text: These headphones are great!! I wanted a pair of "headphones" that were not "earbuds", and I did lots of looking before I purchased. These had really good ratings, and weren't over priced, so I took a chance on them. I must say I'm glad I did. These are stereo headphones, so they truly sound like a stereo in your ears. They are "on the ear" style, but actually fit more like "over the ear" on me. (I'm a mid-30's female.) The bass is really good, clear sound, comfortable, adjustable, and they're not ugly. Ha! The only thing I wish they had was a cord that could be removed. But I can totally live with that.

title: I wasn't expecting them to be this good!
text: I bought these because my friend recommended me to, and she has great taste! I wanted to buy them in pink but the black pair was cheapest and matched my laptop best, and I'm glad I bought the black pair now. They're very modern-looking and go with everything. The sound quality is incredible and the noise cancellation is shockingly good considering the price. I keep getting yelled at by my mom because I can never hear her when I have these on. It's great, because I don't really want to hear her most of the time LOL. I will definitely repurchase these when the time comes.

title: These headphones are light and comfortable with good sound quality
text:
This is exactly what I've been looking for. These headphones are light and comfortable with good sound quality. I can wear them for hours with no discomfort at all. They don't squeeze my head, make my ears sweat or do any of those unpleasant things other headphones have done. My only complaint is that people can't hear me very well through the microphone. I find I have to quickly pull the headphones out if someone calls me while I'm listening.

FYI The cable is about 3 feet long.

But as I bought these primary to listen to music I'm only taking one star off.

But you will have already opened the package and used the headphones. So it will be too bad for you. If you love the sensation of sharp plastic piercing you earlobes, this is the headset for you!
