title: Needed quick quality with little fuss and got a lot more for my money.
text:
I certainly didn't expect anything of audiophile quality or anything astounding. Knowing how much I'm spending is the context for the review.

It's a good pair of headphones.

They're lightweight, comfortable, the earpads stay reasonably cool, the cord is just long enough to listen to at a desk (and that's a good thing), and they sound awfully good for what I paid. They also look pretty okay. The fact that they fold flat is a nice little bonus but for a pair of utility headphones, I think these are a home run.

So as a sound engineer and a musician I would put it this way. They're not high fidelity headphones, nor do they work for performance. They won't work for reference/studio work at all. They're too flimsy to call a solid pair, but for casually listening to music while multi-tasking at home or for watching a streaming video, these do just great. The cord is a bit short, so I've had to use a extension cable. It's reasonable if you are working at the computer where it's plugged into, but I had these plugged into one computer, and would frequently work on other computers in my office.

title: Updated Review:  Seller/Manufacturer reached out about problem headphones
text:
Final Review Update, April 2020

The OneOdio CS team emailed me to request I increase my review star rating. To be fair, they'd sent me a new set of headphones to try out. But it's not even ONE YEAR since my purchase of the original headphones, and now TWO OneOdio headphones have failed. The replacement (thanks, OneOdio) crackles badly when using the line with the microphone, making videoconferencing impractical. And without even touching the mic button, my Itunes folder is constantly randomly opened--in the middle of conference calls for work. Extremely unprofessional.

I don't know what has happened to the headphone industry. We used to go through one headset every three to five years. Then a few years ago, while taking better care of our headsets--including wrapping the cord gently according to professional electronics instructional videos, gently placing our headsets in padded protective cases...--our headsets stopped lasting even a single year. title: Great headphones... Great price
text: Great cans for the price... I work in radio and have found when I don't spend $100 on headphones they last longer but I still want good quality... These come with a convenient carrying bag and two cords... One regular 3.5 mm cord that you can plug into your phone, or a 6.35 jack for studio/dj use. title: Not worth it
text:
Received today, tried them on and ...
They're huge, bulky and heavy to start with, so much that my head was actually hurting on top from the weight.
I turned them on, connected to my phone and tried them out; the "noise canceling" does not work whatsoever, I heard everything that was said to me by others and all other random noise, including my washer and dryer in my basement.
I do not know about how battery life is, but given my experience with them otherwise, I am not keeping them long enough to find out.
Picture included to show actual size, which is not the same as advertised.
The ear padding is comfortable enough and it did connect rather quickly to my device.

title: Great for a month
text:
UPDATE [9/21/2017]: After being contacted from customer support on this issue, I'm updating these to 4 stars. Customer support was very kind and helpful with this situation and issued me a replacement. Audio quality is spot on. I use these with 25% volume on my computer and adjust the media in the actual program and, in all instances, it was never blasting my eardrums or making me strain to hear the audio. It's crisp and clear and, especially for a work environment, blocks out a good amount of minor chatter and other noises. As long as you don't have your music blasting, you can still hear if someone is trying to get your attention.

I've had these for about a month now and I use them for work, so there is some pretty heavy usage 5 days of the week. While they were great while they lasted, the left cup is spotty now. title: They sound GREAT but don't last.
text:
Been a DJ for over 35 years, and I've gone through more than my share of headphones. Expensive ones, cheap ones, and ones that cost way too much.

The truth is, most "premium" headphones these days use pretty much the same components. Heavier magnets make for bigger better drivers, and thicker gauge wires, along with some smooth circuitry... Well, you can easily spend $500 or $50 and if you research hard enough, you'll find there's not much difference in sound quality - all things being equal.

I have tried 3 sets of these headphones, in their different levels of padding, etc. And first lets get this out of the way:

They sound GREAT
These are LOUD
They will deliver the BASS - which is impressive despite not requiring phantom power (like Beats, etc.)

The problem is their overall build quality. If you are a casual user, not caring about brand names, by all means, run, don't walk - to throw these in your cart. For less than $40 you'd be

OneOdio sent me a new set of phones. Wow...what a difference a properly functioning pair makes!
There is still the issue of the orientation of the earcups, THat's in the design. I can live with that. But the big difference is in the overall quality of the headphones.
The replacement set has a different cord. The previous one had a woven covering. They look like they would last forever. The new set has a vinyl insulating covering. I suspect this is to more easily accommodate the new built-in microphone. Yes, they have added a mic into the cord of the phones. A nice feature.
The very good news, though, is this set of phones has two working channels, so listening to stereo music is now possible. The sound quality is good. I would like a bit more bass response, but I really cannot complain about the sound, given the price of these phones. And with sound coming through both channels, I have no problem listening to music for an hour or more.

Also, the ear cushions are definitely not too big (if you have a small head /ears you'll be fine, I have a large head and gauges lol) and do a good job of isolating the sound to what you're listening to; comfortable and not hot. An audiophile friend rates the sound quality as incredible. To me, for 35 bucks, they are an awesome alternative to speakers. I got a second pair because they chain together easily with included cables (everyone listens at the same volume, right!) Yeah, man!

title: Good sound but too small
text:
I was wearing gaming headphone for work in conferences and felt silly. These looked more professional so I figured I would give them a try.

The sound quality is pretty decent for the price, but after hours of wearing them, they start to hurt my ears because they are too tight. The control button also gets hit accidently quite often and that's annoying. If you have a small head and don't mind clipping the control button to your clothes, then I suppose these are a great choice, but for me, I will see if I think I need another pair.

title: Fits my Work Needs
text: I am currently using this on a work laptop for my many online meetings, primarily though WebEx but also Skype for Business and GoToMeeting. My co-workers tell me my voice is clear, I can hear theirs very well, and the mute and volume buttons on the line are very useful. I find the on the ear design a little tight for me, and I would really say it's comfortable nor really uncomfortable. The padding really is okay for an on the ear style. It is definitely a set I take over pretty quickly. In comparison I use a Bose set for my phone, and a wireless set for my desktop for gaming. This was really just something I wanted to attach to the work machine all the time and readily available to just hook up.  For the price, it's actually pretty good for what I use it for... I just wouldn't ever choose something like this to wear for hours. However if a family member wanted something for under twenty and to put on for no more than an hour... this is a good start until they decide to upgrade.  This i

title: Works great but hurts my ears after a couple hours
text:
I got this headset to practice working with speech recognition software such as Dragon NaturallySpeaking. Overall, I've been surprised and incredibly impressed with the performance. The recognition is better than with the standard headset that came with the software, and the headset does everything I had hoped.

There's a volume control button on the cord, and the USB cord is about 8 feet long, which is plenty generous for connecting to my computer and doing speech recognition.

The only problem with this headset is that I want to use it for hours on end while narrating articles. I'm a freelance writer, and sometimes I either just don't feel like typing, have a sore wrist or fingers, or feel like I can narrate my thoughts faster than I can type them. After about two hours of using this headset, my ears start to ache. I haven't figured out any way of changing the tension on this headset so it doesn't press so hard on my ears.

While I would definitely recom

title: Addendum: Headband Broke, Weak USB Connection --
text: The usb connecter does not seem to stand up to repeated insertion and removal.

title: Very nice as you can mute yourself if you want to ...
text: Use these for gaming and to chat during dungeons/raids. Very nice as you can mute yourself if you want to just listen in. Crystal clear sound. I also use them for listening to movies and music if my husband is sleeping and I don't want to wake him. I love them. One thing I noticed though is after having them on for about 3 or 4 hours, my head feels like it's being "squished" and I have marks on my ears. No big deal to me, but others may not like that.

title: 2 functions I needed didn't work
text: I am looking for headphones with microphone because I work from home and do many video conference calls and record trainings.  I have been looking for microphone that blocks surrounding sound and this set claimed to do this.  I immediately tested and it did not successfully block any of the surrounding sound; it picked up someone walking by in socks on hardwood floor. Also, as another reviewer noted, the mute button didn't work at all.  Being able to mute is key as well when working from remote locations.  I was very disappointed because other reviews seemed to indicate these would work very well.  The quality of the sound it recorded was very nice, so gave 5 stars for that 1 component. I am returning these headphones.

title: Works ok, but ear pieces tend to hurt ears in short time.
text: I thought the head set ear pieces would be softer.  When using for a short time the ear pieces start to hurt you ears.  Moving them occasionally helps, but I think they could have been made softer.  The volume control is located about 24 inches from the head set, which is too long.  If you attach the volume control to your shirt there is a loop of cable between the headset and the volume control that can get in the way while writing or other movements of your arms or hands.  The clip on the volume control could be better as well.  It is fixed and can't be clipped on anything other than a thin shirt.  If you want to clip it to a thicker shirt collar you are out of luck.

title: Not worth the money. Already broken

title: No frills headphones that work great
text: As far as USB headphones go, this is a solid buy. Still, I will say that I'm buying them to replace my old pair. They lasted for about three years after being subjected to some admittedly rough usage. The sound quality is good, but nothing to rave about. The microphone though, works tremendously well with skype and other video chat programs. Your voice doesn't get distorted at all. It's especially nice for PC gamers who are tired of crummy mics with lots of feedback. My main reservation is  that, fully extended, they barely fit on my head, and I've got a pretty average sized cranium.

My favorite by far is the Logitech USB Clear Chat. I love it! It's really comfortable, doesn't slide around and is very accurate.

The only drawback to this microphone is that it dies on me once a year. That's probably because I do extensive dictation every day. I usually buy extended warranties but when I buy it on Amazon, I can't do that. That's my only complaint. Otherwise I'm delighted with this product.

text: They are not as nice or as comfortable as the Microsoft lx-6000.  My lx-6000 headset broke (common place), so in a pinch, I bought the Logitech ClearChat at Target. Also, the volume control on the headset actually controls the volume of the computer.  So to get the headset to an audible level that I can actually hear, I had to crank the volume up on the computer itself, so now all alerts and such are blaring loud.  It drives me crazy.  I personally wouldn't recommend this headset at all.

title: New One Every 6 Months!!
text: Avoid these like the plague. Unless you're ready to buy a new one every 6 months, either pay more money, get a different brand, use only once a week, use soup cans tied with string, etc. You get the idea. I've had two sets so far and am on my third. Why keep buying them? I have TMJ, and the adjustable headset is the only one I've found that doesn't give me a headache a minute after I put them on. What's wrong with them? First, after about 3 months the left ear/speaker starts going in and out. I've read this on other sites as well, so I know I'm not the only one with a problem. After about 6 months the wires above the mute button internally separate to the point that you spend minutes trying to get them held in the exact position, and then the least movement cuts you off again. This is embarassing during business calls, or all calls for that matter. The first time it happens, buy a new one then. The problem is only going to get worse. So, yes, I'm the idiot who keeps buying them, but on

title: Good for Skype or Google Video Chat ("Hangouts")
text:
This is an excellent headset for video conferencing. I bought three of them to use with family. I disagree with the reviews that suggest the headset is uncomfortable or not durable. All three people using the sets I bought find the headset comfortable, with clear sound, and the leatherette ear pads seem like they'll hold-up nicely over time.

The unit is truly plug-and-play. No installation of software or drivers needed. However, the only complaint I have is that there's a delay when you plug it in while the computer gets ready. Seems like that delay occurs each time (there is no permanent install so the computer needs to recognize when the USB plug is plugged in each time). It's a little frustrating if you want to use the headset quickly. I'd say the delay is about a minute. That doesn't seem like a long time, but it's an eternity when someone is calling you unexpectedly for a video or audio chat. So, I guess I can leave the unit plugged in all the time, or plan better when

For the price, this headset had superb performance - crisp clear microphone (everyone in the seminar could hear me clearly) and I could hear the content from other presenters also very clearly. title: Works fine
text: I bought one of these because we use the same one at work. With the pandemic, they have us in the office every other day, and working from home on the other days). I bought this so I don't have to carry my headset back and forth every day, now I have one for in-office and one for home-office. Works fine, USB connected to the computer, has an in-line mute and volume button, the microphone swivels up and down, and the headset is padded and adjustable to your head. The ear pieces are ON EAR, not over the ear, but they're decently padded. I don't use mine a ton, so I can't attest to how comfortable they are after hours of wear, but they work fine for when I occasionally have to talk on the phone to someone.

title: After 8 years as a happy customer, I cannot buy this model again.
text: I use a headset anywhere from 60% - 90% of my day. My co-workers frequently have problems with their wireless headsets that cost in the hundreds when switching between meeting platforms or other issues, but I never had problems with this headset model until recently. I found over the years that I had to replace them every few years but they are so inexpensive and were 100% reliable in all situations that I was okay with that. I just purchased a new pair and plugged it into the same usb port and according to the folks I talked to, the sound cut out about 50% of the time. Nothing I did in adjusting settings or plugging into another usb port solved the problem. I definitely don't have time for this and will not be purchasing this model again.

title: Great basic/entry-level conferencing headset
text:
I work remotely occasionally, and picked up this headset after some coworkers complained about the sound from the built-in microphone in my laptop.

My initial impression was that the headphones felt kind of cheap. Marginally better than the earphones you'd get with a tape player in the 90's. Although they feel cheap, they're also really light. So after wearing them for a few minutes, I stopped noticing I was wearing them. The sound through the headphones was pretty good, and a remote coworker said I sounded great on a video call the first time he heard the microphone.

So if you're looking for a basic conferencing headset with pretty good audio quality, I think these hit the mark. And although they seem a little cheap, they're also so light that they're pretty comfortable.

title: Fairly good headset
text:
I wear a headset for the better part of a day. I like to use headsets rather than the phone handset because it keeps my hands free.

These definitely do the job with noise cancelling and the volume control works very well. I've been told once or twice that people have trouble hearing me at times, but since I don't know if that's the headset or their phones, I deducted a single star.

My only complaint is the clip that puts the volume control and mute button together is very difficult to hook onto anything other than very thin fabric. I have to struggle to get it on and off my jacket during the day. My suggestion to the manufacturer, make the clip easier to slide fabric into or make it a clip that opens.

title: Great headphones

We'll see if it's durable.

Two issues - The set is somewhat bulky and does not fold, thus taking up a lot of space in my laptop bag. The other issue is that it sits a bit tight on the ears, making it uncomfortable after a couple of hours. It's a little easier on the ears if I move the cups slightly forward, but then that lets external sounds in. That said, it could be the shape of my head, and you might not have this issue.

title: Fits my small ears/head
text: I had the previous generation of this headset so I knew what I was getting. When I get glasses/hats I have to buy adolescent sizes (preteen-teen), so this headset fits me well at the smallest adjustment. text:

I love the function of being able to change the wallpaper on it! It seems like such a small thing, but sometimes being able to add just that little bit of customization makes a HUGE difference in morale.

The double secured straps on the band make this a great nod to those of us with tiny wrists. No floppy strap!

The only downside is mine did not come with the Sleep tracker function. I attempted to contact the company and have not gotten any replies yet... It is concerning, but the other aspects of the watch are great and work fine, so I can utilize it until I make contact.

title: Good Smart watch for not a lot of money.
text:
Had a problem with the Weather function on my watch. Contacted company and they immediately answered. They advised to download newest version of HeroBandIII on my Android phone...which I did. Now my watch is working perfectly and I’m pleased.
With their one yr warranty and their good price...it is a very good Smart watch. From what I have noticed, I believe the functions are pretty accurate. Now, if you want to spend hundreds of dollars for a Smart watch...then do it. But for me...this fits my needs pretty well.

The APP is simple to set up and a pleasure to use.  Apart from the problem with the sleep tracking , I would highly recommend this watch.

title: Awesome earbuds... not so awesome case
text:
So, first off, let me say that these cheap little earbuds are really great! It's always kinda a risky feeling when I buy cheap electronics, because the quality could go either way. But these are a really good find. Clear, crisp sound with good bass... awesome battery life... right size. Perfecto! Well, almost perfecto...

The only design flaw is in the case, which I'm seeing a lot of these bluetooth earbuds having these days. The case is so smooth & they only put a tiny finger grip strip on one side of the lid, so there's nothing to grip the other side of the case, making it a clumbsy open & you risk dropping the whole thing (which I usually did). Thankfully, I never lost an earbud before thinking of a simple hack (albeit, not the prettiest solution, but it works). Just add a little strip from a hot glue gun to the bottom side of the case and viola! Double grip and easy opening. Now it's perfecto! ;-)

title: Great for small ears
text: I have tiny ear holes and these were great. I bought these and another pair which were a tad bit smaller so my 11 y/o daughter took those and I kept these. Love that they charge in the case without being plugged into a wall. That was just a bonus. They last me for many hours and since I only use one at a time, I can get over a day's use out of them. They don't fall out easily (although sometimes I feel like they're barely hanging in there). I had the smallest size covers on. Quality is good and people don't have a problem hearing me on phone calls but I mostly use them to listen to audio books while I'm driving, walking and working around the house. I guess I did get a little ear fatigue at first but then you really do get use to them. I used to use a corded, single earbud but my new phone didn't have the port anymore so I had to upgrade finally to wireless. I had my bass set very high and too much will (understandably) cause these to buzz as the bass tops out.  But when you get it right, these will be very close to a nice pair of inexpensive earbuds.  But it's the comfort and freedom from wires that's the big change for me.  I can listen to these easily for their whole 5 hour charge time and not feel weary from either the sound or the weight of the earphones/headphones.  It's also incredible to me to have a pause function available by just tapping on one of the earphones. Have a conversation, then just tap to start up the music again. title: What more do you want?
text:
Let me preface by saying I decided long ago that smartwatches are basically notification engines that can also keep you from having to constantly take your phone out. Also, they do some health tracking. If you see a smartwatch as anything other than that, this review probably isn't going to make sense to you

I've owned, a AppleWatch, a TicWatch, a Galaxy Watch, an Amazfit BIP and this watch. I've used both iOS and Android. I do not understand why you would need anything more than what this watch offers. 14 (legit) day battery life. Bright, easily readable screen. Bluetooth notifications. Easy setup, and lots of watchfaces. What more do you want? Sure, the novelty of being able to take a call on my watch was neat (Galaxy Watch). It's just a gimmick/novelty. Yeah, being able to get emails on my watch while leaving my phone on a different floor in my office was neat. It just increased my anxiety about not being able to respond well (iWatch). I got this to replace the small oval one I got for Christmas last year of the same brand. The previous one was very uncomfortable to the point of causing pain. While this one is more expensive and more comfortable and doesn’t have to be charged as often it is lacking the functionality of the previous one. There is no option for bedtime to keep it from lighting up every time I move at night like the cheaper one. And it is very bright even on night mode. Also there is no option to choose what functions I want to show on the watch face like there was with the smaller cheaper one. And some watch face options only show time and date. And the strap either needs to be shorter or have more hol

title: App is unusable
text: If this is your first smartwatch, then congratulations... you will probably think this is a wonderful purchase. But if you've had a Fitbit or AppleWatch or Samsung Watch before, then you will immediately discover the lower quality of the Zepp app that you must install to use this Amazfit watch. I had a Fitbit Versa for years before trying out this Amazfit watch and I was impressed with the look and feel of the watch, but after using the Zepp app.... (it even had some Chinese in the app, and there was a weird (Chinese version?) name for Alexa). I couldn't take it and returned the watch.

title: Going To Make It Work
text: Purchased this watch inorder to receive call and text message notifications, mostly when I’m at work and away from my phone. It was easy to set up and connect to the Zepp app. Just have to read and follow instructions carefully. Call notifications hit without a problem but my text messages only hit every now and then. Not consistently. I have an Android (an LG Risio3 with an Android8 software). I emailed Amafit customer service and went back and forth since July to figure out what the issue was. It seems that my phone system isn’t compatible even though it’s usually compatible with Androids. Basically, I need a new phone. Amazfit customer service was incredible and followed through. Love my watch and will definately hunt for a new phone inorder for my text messages to consistently hit my watch.

title: Alexa what?
text: It says this has alexa built in, however the app on my watch cannot find the device. I have tried NUMEROUS times and it just can't see it. The watch tells me to go to my mobile app and allow it.....can't cause it can't find it!

title: Works well
text:
SLEEP TRACKING: - Seems to be accurate; but it does give a low sleep score if you sleep in and wake up couple of times in between. Compared to Fitbit, I didn't like this way of sleep tracking. Amazfit also give high sleep score even if your sleep is shorter than 7 or 8 hours, but certain ratios are met on deep sleep etc... personally doesn't seems to be that great method.

CALORIES COUNTED: seems to be accurate, but it only counts only the calories burned due to activity, where as a Fitbit seems to be counting even idle calorie burned, to me, Amazfit method seems to be more favorable.

BATTERY LIFE: There is always a disclaimer that whatever the maximum advertised life will not be achieved in practice. With some extra features enabled and mobile notifications enabled, I get 7 days give or take, which is better compared to the Fitbit HR I had which only gave 5 days

MOBILE NOTIFICATIONS: Seems to be working 99.9% of the time. This is an improvement I had

title: Great Watch
text:
I have been a loyal Fitbit user since the introduction of the Fitbit One. I wore out several and recommend and purchased several for friends over the years.
I started having sync problems. I put up with it. When the screen popped off the watch body, I decided I need a new watch. My Fitbit was less than 2 years old and paid over $200 when it first came out.
I am impressed with the new Amazfit GTS 2 Smartwatch. I am still learning how to use it. So many features.
The only complaint, the instruction manual is written for someone that has better than average eyes.
So far I highly recommend this Smart Watch.

title: Amazing!
text: [[VIDEOID:ef5a24d2cb113eafa1e92d082dc0e736]] Compare to Apple Watch/Fitbit. I get all my texts and it tracks my steps, the time, and my sleep. Typically lasts 9 days battery life. Love it.

title: Wonderful ! *UPDATED*
text:
I am really liking this watch! The last one I got was a bit cheaper but it was really awful so I decided to take it back. This watch works a lot smoother and has much better options for me ! I mostly need it as a timer, alarm and notifications and it does all these things ! I got a better strap for it as well :)

*UPDATED*

Now that I've owned this for several months, I have to say that the connection between the watch and my phone isn't very good. For no reason it wont show my notifications at times, I really wish this didn't happen, especially since I usually have important things pop up on my phone. I have to constantly every day go back to the app to reconnect it and sometimes that doesn't last very long (also it doesn't even let me know if its been disconnected..) this is really frustrating, I am not sure if something like this can be fixed on their side. Really disappointed. Had to change my rating because of this.

title: Great watch
text: It is a wonderful watch. It does a good job tracking my fitness parameters. Nevertheless, I would have expected it to be able to control the camera shutter on my smartphone.

title: Great battery life.
text:
Good watch which seems really accurate on stress, heart rate, sleep tracking. The app is easy to use.
Battery life is excellent. Lasts about a week before it needs recharged.
I would buy as a gift.
It has lots of watch faces, but I wish it had a wider selection of simple ones. Not fond of tons of info on the watch face. I can open the app if I want that.
Has Alexa on it. Doesn't talk to you but gives you written responses.
EDIT: Alerts are good, BUT they don't tell you who they are from. Tells you what app sent it, but not the person. Hope they fix that in an update.

title: Love
text: I don’t use this for sleep tracking, but I do love all the other features. The interface and app are user friendly. I love the music function for your phone.

title: One of the best options for a full-featured SmartWatch with decent battery life
text:
While I miss my four week battery life from the original BIP watch with the always on display, this watch does well on battery considering all the features that are turned on. I have almost all the features enabled and my average battery charging is about every 4 days. Overall, it's a very impressive feature set now with the heart rate monitoring and the blood oxygen level sensors.

The Zepp app continues to be the biggest weakness for all the amazfit watches. The new version is a little better but it often crashes when syncing with the watch.

title: Wouldn't sync to phone
text: I bought this as a gift for Father's Day and the watch wouldn't sync up to my dad's iPhone 12.  I thought he was just having trouble because he is not extremely technologically savvy, however it refused to sync up when I tried as well.

title: Not bad. Some days better than others
text:
I was originally going to give this 5 stars, as it seemed like it was tracking amazingly in the first few weeks, and I was DELIGHTED to find something so accurate at this price.
However, about 3 or 4 weeks in, the accuracy dropped. Some days it has been as much as 80 to 100 beats off when I am doing indoor cycling. Maybe 20 to 30 beats off at lower cardio levels when doing yoga, or warming up (I check manually)-- and then sometimes fairly close again. Could be a connection issue, but I have not been changing anything, so it feels like a roulette wheel. So, useful info, but know that if you are really depending on accurate numbers, you may not get them. Try to be more about consistent habits and awareness of your PRE.

title: I love this watch!!
text: Although it required more time for me to get it right, I am pleased with the results.  I can answer my mobile phone via the watch...but the phone cannot be to far away.  It keeps track of multiple body measurable functions.  I also set an alarm for certain events.  It has far more features that I have taken the time to use.

title: Feels like junk and cheaply made.
text: Why even bother buying additional bands when the bottom part of the watch where the bands attach is the color of the watch? So for example a green watch with a blue band . Or black band.. you're stuck with whatever color you bought the watch in.. meh meh meh. Less than basic of a smartwatch.

title: Heart rate monitoring not good for biking...
text: I really would have liked to keep this watch.  The screen is gorgeous, bright colors, lots of different watch faces (although a lot of them are a bit too busy for me).  It is light weight, comfortable to wear.  Great battery life (a few days when you use it for exercise (GPS) a lot).  The app isn't too bad either (although syncing is very slow sometimes).  It does easily sync to Strave (including heart rate data).  It is convenient to see text messages and WhatsApp pop up when I am biking.  The main reason I bought it for was the heart rate monitoring during my biking exercises.  Just walking or sitting it works fine.  But when biking (either road or mountain biking) it can be way off.  Sometimes it works fine, but sometimes it is much too low or much too high.  I think sometimes it is double counting or skipping ever other beat...  For example, going downhill on a MTB pretty relaxed it is apparently often the most strenuous according to the watch, with HR of 170!  Or I am huffing and

title: Love this watch
text: This is my third "smart" watch.  My first was a Samsung fitness watch, which was much more expensive and worked okay until I got my new Samsung phone.  Then it wouldn't stay connected to my new phone and I wasn't receiving most notifications.  I switched to a cheap Wyze watch.  That watch could track steps, but the notification function was poor and the texts could barely be read.  I'll give that watch to one of my grandchildren to use as a toy.  I bought this watch when Amazon offered a discount through their Treasure Truck.  Although setting up the watch took a while and lots of persistence on my part (it is not intuitive, some of the setting menus are hard to find), I finally got it all set up.  I've had the watch about 10 days now and I just love it.  It's very lightweight and unobtrusive. I don't know if the steps, heart rate, etc. are perfectly accurate, but they're good enough for my purposes (none of my watches or pedometers have ever been perfectly accurate).  I love the Zepp

title: Why does it need an app?
text:
I wanted a tracker with a good or hopefully better battery than every fitbit, pebble or other tracker I've tried yet. This was offered through the treasure truck and no where did I see that you needed to install an app for it to be useful. I hate when I want to buy a self sufficient product without having to install an app for it to be useful. I sent it back.

People don't pay attention to the permission you allow these apps to have, for instance the need for access to my contacts, no, not going to happen unless it's an app such as a messenger or something like that. Or access to my camera or my files, nope not happening.

title: Not worth the price
text: Does not connect to I phone easily. I could get the texts to connect at all. Most the features only work occasionally.

title: A good watch not perfect
text:
The watch in nice and it functions with many options.
I did not like that I need to lift my hand twice to see the hour and I did not like many erroneous continuous readings of my heart rate.

title: A good option
text: If you can't afford or won't pay the price of a more expensive watch. Great for workouts and other physical activity. The Sleep Tracker leaves much to be desired; for instance, it reports that I'm sleeping one hour per night when I'm actually getting a full 8 or 9 hours. The other functions seem to work fine. The battery life on this watch is incredible - one charge lasts for about 10 days of normal use.

title: FOR ONCE.  MORE FOR LESS MONEY
text: Wanted to wear it awhile before reviewing.  This is GREAT.  Wore it to Dr. Office.  All stats matched. Being able to use Alexa was a HUGE bonus.  The watch faces you can change to are really nice.

title: Excelente, pero..
text: Me encantó todo, tanto el GPS que es exacto en mi región como la duración de batería que no llega a lo que se dice.. pero 7 días para mi esta bien y el pero es que no me funciona Alexa en Ecuador

title: support
text: I like the watch but have several questions and tried several times to get someone to respond to my emails or phone calls. I have watched most of the YouTube videos but none have addressed my questions.  Very frustrated.Will be returning the watch

title: Great smart stylish watch at an affordable price!
text: This product was a gift and it was greatly appreciated! Love the style and strap. It's very lightweight and has fun interfaces.

title: The app is ok -Watch is Great
text: I only wish you could text back with the watch, but overall it has some great features. I love the ability to switch around what you use the most to totally customize it for you and your daily activity. The alarms are great. The watch faces are changeable. I had to get a cloth back since I am allergic to soft plastics. But it was a great buy. The battery last forever , at least 2-3 weeks

title: Lots of bang for the buck
text:
I have had this sports watch for only a week, but so far I am quite happy with
it. It has out preformed a cheaper $50 watch, and a more expensive Garmin watch.
Some of the features i really like.
1. Selectable watch face. There are dozens of watch faces to choose from. I chose a more utilitarian one that displays heart rate, steps, miles, Kcalories, date, time, day of the weeks. All easy to read in a bright crisp clear easy to read format.
2. Easy to navigate menu with touch screen and a sing push button.
3. Lots of sports activities, although I found the lack of indoor running and walking, which I do a lot of in the winter months, confusing. The swimming app really works well. I swam 66 laps, and it only missed one. My old watches would miscount several laps, especially if I did not pause between laps.
4. Activity screens are easy to personalize to display the information I want to view the most. It is easy to navigate between lists and graphs

title: Not bad at all for the price
text:
Let's be real here: Amazon is a giant company, but that doesn't make this any less of a cheap knockoff product. For instance, the strap doesn't stay secured all that well, and the automatic activity tracker insists that I have spent almost 48 hours skiing over the past week for some reason.

That being said, it does pretty much exactly what I was expecting it to given the price. If you don't have the funds for an on-brand smartwatch, this isn't a bad option.

title: NO burn, good.  Long battery.  Tracking steps not accurate. Love it but...
text: Versa 3 (two different ones) and Luxe sparked and burned my wrist when I swam.  Amazfit does not. Big plus. 9 day battery life!  Well made fit and finish.  Handsome.  I love this watch!  But I may return it because it does not track steps anywhere near correctly.  I will get up 3 times at night, each trip plus or minus around 22 steps.  Watch will often record 0 and, yes, band is correctly placed.  It records sleep patterns easily enough but not walking.  Seems to forget to count during the day too.  I've been tracking steps for 10 years and know this is wrong.  If I find something better, I will return it and  I do not want to.  Conflicted because I love it.

title: Not as described
text: There are only 3 sport activities. Running, cycling & walking. I purchased this specific watch for rowing. It says 70 sports but I only see 3 choices. Very disappointed.

title: Good Smartwatch
text: This was a gift for my grandson. He reports that he likes it as much as his Apple Watch! High praise for someone that writes apps for apple!!

title: Easy to use, amazing battery life!
text: I love how simple and easy this watch is to use. I wear it everyday and only need to charge it once a week. Battery is amazing. Highly recommend if you just want a basic smart watch

title: Wonderful Hardware Let Down By Poor Software
text:
This review would be 5 stars if not for one very bad flaw in these headphones. The moment you make a call, the noise canceling turns off and you (and whoever you're calling) will hear everything around you – amplified – while you're on the phone. It's so bad that I have to use my old QC25 wired headphones for calls (which work perfectly for this), and only use the QC35-S2's for wireless listening to music/podcasts/tv while doing other things. This is beyond ridiculous for headphones of this price.

As for their performance purely as listening devices, the noise canceling is slightly (but only slightly) better than the wired QC25's, but the tonal range is slightly worse in the base tones. Their ability to connect to multiple devices is great, but suffers from a tendency for them to want to connect to multiple devices AT ONCE (iphone + something else, for instance), which about half the time causes no audio to be played through them at all, even though the source is muted when

title: Technical issues
text: I get a lot of stutter. It seems like I am not the only one. It actually caused issues with a FFXV for PC. For some reason when the headphones were in 2 way communications mode the game froze every couple seconds. Weird but not a big deal. The thing that kills it is the fact that the sound stutters when I am just listening to YouTube videos. I am right next to the Bluetooth receiver with updated drivers and I still get the stutter. I had the MDX1000 until they cracked and had no issues with the sound or connection. Its disappointing that Sony can figure it out but Bose cant... I guess you have to choose between poor manufacturing quality (Sony) and poor Bluetooth implementation.

title: Unpleasant glitches and early wear off
text:
I have a very unpleasant experience with this equipment. There are constant small glitches when playing from a laptop. While it works fine when playing from a smartphone, the limitation is annoying. There is a thread with more than 26 pages in Bose's support community discussing the issue.
Removing from the laptop and reinstalling worked for a while. Now it doesn't. I updated the firmware and followed all recommendations, but the problem persists.
For the very high price it charges you, Bose should provide a better support.
I do not recommend the purchase, with so many other similar options in the market.

Update:
I could make the headphones to work without glitches by disabling a couple of Bluetooth Services, namely ARfcomm, AudioWear, AudioWear Voice and Handsfree Telephony. It still presents glitches, but now it is less frequent.
However, now I have another issue. The earpads began to wear off. It is way too early for this to happen (I bought about

title: Bose is Riding Their Reputation
text:
I left a lengthy review comparing the Jabra Evolve2 85 (E285) vs Bose Quiet Comfort 35 II (QC35II) vs Bose Noise Cancelling 700 (B700) vs Sony WH-1000XM4 (SX4) (in order of when I received them) for the E285. This is a shorter version of that review, mostly focusing on QC35II and E285. This was almost entirely written up during the week I had all four headsets for direct comparisons.

Microphone and Playback
QC35II recordings were consistently louder than the E285. But the E285 did a far better job of removing background noise, as evidenced by my recordings while drumming. Muting can be done with the QC35II but it was much more difficult than just raising/lowering the boom arm of the E285.

Audio Output - DISCLAIMER - I'm not an audiophile
E285 and SX4 audio sound better than the QC35II but primarily because I can use an equalizer and increase bass (obviously this is the most important factor). Even if the QC35II had better hardware, it’s not as accessible

title: Very, very disappointed
text:
Very, very disappointed. I really wanted to love these - as I had wanted to when I purchased a pair of original QC35s. I love Bose speakers, esp. bluetooth speakers, and have Bose stereo systems in several cars I currently / recently owned (currently two Mercedes and one Audi), all of which are excellent systems.

These are the most comfortable headphones I've tried - which includes, for reference, Sennheiser HD1, Bang & Olufsen H9 (my personal favorite of all headphones), Bowers & Wilkins P7s, Bluedio, AudioMX, etc. etc. These ARE comfortable.

I do think these have the best noise-canceling in the industry, including the Sony MDR-1000X (or current WH-1000XM2). Much of that has to do with the softness and plushness of the ear pads; you'll notice that about 2/3 of the noise canceling is accomplished simply by WEARING the headphones, even while off.

I like the addition of the multifunction button that toggles between noise canceling high / low / off. Very g

title: Very good sound
text: Very good sound quality and I am pleased with the comfort, but only four stars for three reasons: no charging adapter included - seriously at this price point? The "customizable" button can only be assigned to a few things. The last reason is the charging seems slow and then the power drops down to 70 percent very soon after you start using it. It is too soon to tell if it has a defective battery.

title: Amazing headphones and really a value (believe it or not) compared to another brand.
text:
I was not sure what direction I wanted to go with travel headphones so I tried a couple of different ones. I tried these Bose QuietComfort 35 II, the QuietComfort 20 in ear, and the Sennheiser HD1. The in-ear are not for me just because of the shape and size of my ears. :( I liked them and for what they were the noise cancellation was pretty good. With the HD1 and the QC 35 the biggest difference is price. I would say the HD1 was a bit louder and I could hear some "cooler" things that I couldn't hear with any other headset, but the biggest problem with it was there was an obvious defect in the unit I received. I sent them back. But to the point of this review.

The QuietComfort 35 are scary good. The sound quality is amazing. Even the bass was great given that Bose doesn't have the biggest reputation for bass in their headphones. The noise cancellation is especially scary. It is amazing the technology that is able to allow for so much around a person to be blocked. I

title: Does not actually cancel most noise
text:
I am so disappointed. When I received this package today, I thought I was finally going to have the chance to get some sleep. I live in a room right next to a noisy person (and rude - phone calls right next to me at 2 a.m. kind of deal), and have neighbors with yappy dogs that they leave out for hours on end at times. I've tried various ear plugs, but those haven't been effective enough for blocking the noises, so I figured I'd try active noise-cancelling headphones. These do not work for that purpose. I have the noise cancelling function set on "high," but I can still hear the TV downstairs, footsteps nearby and chatting a few rooms away. It blocks has blocked out a very limited range of background road noise (noises like revving engines are still heard), but simply isn't doing what it claims to be able to do: cancel noise.

I'm desperate here - does anyone happen to have suggestions for headphones that actually cancel noise? I've been playing brown noise in the background w

title: Not an original Bose product!
text: Buttons on the side were not working and the quality of the sound was very poor. I went to the Bose store in NYC to see if my headphones were defective or if I had set up the bluetooth connection improperly. To my surprise,  I was informed by the representative that this was not an original Bose product.

title: Overpriced garbage.
text:
The sound quality and comfort of this set of headphones are pretty good even considering its relatively steep price. While it might be a bit awkward putting these on if you've never used a pair of noise-cancelling cans before (it feels a little like your ears have to pop), you quickly get used to it. The sound quality is phenomenal; I use these mostly for playing video games in addition to music, and they're simply unmatched by any other set of headphones I have, including my pair of Audio Technica studio monitors, and a pair of Sennheiser "beater" phones that I use while traveling and such. Though this is to be expected considering the price of the Bose is far more than those other pairs. Zero complaints, but I'm no audiophile, so if you're looking for an in-depth review you'll have to look elsewhere.

Being a gamer, I spend quite a few hours-long sessions with these on. My Audio Technicas had a nasty habit of really hurting my left ear, leaving soreness and a red mark if I

title: Just threw them in the trash
text: Worthless garbage.  Uncomfortable.  After 6 months, they couldn't even maintain a connection to my iPad for more than 3 minutes.  Sound quality was good when they worked.  Battery life was fine.  Quality was apparently sub-par.  Price was ridiculous.  I'm done with Bose overpriced junk.

title: These are the headphones
text: Typically my head gets sweaty with headphones, but these are too comfortable. These headphones are perfect. They really block out most sounds when you have a little song playing. It can be a little spooky at times. These headphones are perfect sound quality too, as bose does. The bass is normal, not heavy like other headphones. I enjoy that, as bass can wear out the ears over time. The app is a little minimalistic and leaves a lot to be desired. I also love how the Google Assistant on my phone functions with the button on my headphones. It is really amazing to see it work. It is also perfect with calls. I love these headphones.

title: Witchcraft Involved in Noise Cancellation?
text: I am forced to the conclusion that witchcraft must have been employed in the creation of the noise cancellation in these headphones. Just now I had nothing playing on them while lying in bed and out of curiosity I turned off the noise cancellation. I was confused when I suddenly heard a loud rumbling. I took off the headphones and realized it was my white noise machine on my bedside table. I keep it pretty loud to drown out noises from outside the house. I turned the noise cancellation back on and the sound completely disappeared. How in the world can it do that? I mean, maybe white noise is something particularly easy for them to cancel out, but that is still incredible. Is someone playing a prank on me and just turning the noise machine off as soon as I hit the noise cancellation button? I might think so, but if I lean in really close to it, a bit of the sound gets through. A seemingly unrealistically small amount... and yeah, yeah the headphones sound amazing when you play stuff in

title: Amazing comfort, crisp clear sound, and absolutely no background noise.  Superb!
text:
Bose more or less invented (with other companies working in parallel) noise cancelling headphones, and while there is an emphasis on style, marketing, and hype--these are legit. No weird sound, eerily free of background noise, and supremely comfortable. If you want the best noise cancelling headphones--these or the original QC35 are it.

The box was surprisingly small--so much so, I wondered if these weren't full sized over the ear headphones. They are, albeit with a very justified focus on folding compactly. The box opens to a minimal experience with the nearly fully charged headphones in the included case. Accessories are the case, a bizarrely shorty micro USB cable, and an analog cable. The included instructions are absolutely minimal (unless I missed the larger one I found online) and basically direct you to the app. This created a modest issue, as I was diligently charging the headphones, and lo and behold, they cannot be paired when charging. They cannot do anyt

title: Defective Firmware
text:
I am a fan of Bose headphones. They are expensive, but up until this purchase, I felt there was still a value there. The pair I got has a noisy left ear. I downloaded the firmware update. That software is junk, but I finally got it to start up. It states that I am at the current level of software.

This is a huge disappointment.

Update: I called Bose Customer Support. They asked if I had tried it on two devices. I had. That was the end of the diagnostics. They were going to send me a replacement product. There wasn't even a mention of trying a firmware update. A person on the Bose Community board had this problem over a year ago and she received a second defective product. Apparently Bose isn't the company they used to be, if they can't fix a manufacturing or QA issue in over a year.

title: They are ok
text:
Bought 1 pair and didn't care for them so I gave to my husband. Bought a 2nd pair because of crying kids and loud excited old ladies on train at christmas and didn't want to listen to them on trip home on train. My head might be a tad too small. Wish they made them in a smaller size.
They do not block out sound at all, not even close to it. It just muffleds sound. Makes sound less sharp and shrill.

title: THESE HEADPHONES SAVED MY MARRIAGE
text: Ok, I’m a HUGE Bose fan in general, but these noise canceling headphones quickly became the one thing I would never be able to live without. Don’t judge, you haven’t experienced my husband’s sleep apnea and resulting snoring (Oh. My. GOD). If I was going to a deserted island (and my husband was joining me), the one thing I couldn’t live without out while on my relaxing island getaway would be these headphones. I literally cannot sleep without them due to my husband’s snoring. I tried countless other earplugs and noise canceling buds and headphones (sometimes wearing silicone earplugs WITH headphones to no avail… I tried everything), but none of them worked; they didn’t cancel noise effectively and/or they were just stupid uncomfortable, like so uncomfortable I ended up throwing them out of my bed. I sleep in these every single night, falling asleep to the sound of raindrops. I literally cannot hear my husband come to bed or my five dogs barking when I’m wearing these. They are surprisi

title: Only works with music app, upgrade bricked it
text: These things only work with the music app. The pause feature will always revert back to that, no matter what app you were actually using - YouTube for instance. Then it took forever to download and install a firmware upgrade. After that, it won’t connect to anything. It’s a brick now. I didn’t buy them here, but at the Apple store. Hopefully I can return them. I am done with Bose.

title: These sound like crap
text: I'm a happy Bose QC 15 owner looking to replace due to a bad headphone jack connection.  Unfortunately, these sound much worse.  The high end is limited and garbled; the bass is a clear departure from the warm, powerful bass of the 15s.  These just sound weak and messy.  Very surprised.

title: A bit expensive for sound quality
text: It blocks out low frequency noise like a plane engine but I could still hear a baby crying in the background. The sound is good for music when listening via Bluetooth but the sound is only ok for movies and shows with the cable. With the rumble of the plane engine, it almost wasn't loud enough to hear the dialogue. I had to turn the volume on my tablet up all the way.

title: NO USER MANUAL IS AVAILABLE FOR THIS PRODUCT!
text: Everybody should be aware that this is a complex, sophisticated product which is shipped completely without instructions. Also, it is shipped without a charger, and nowhere mentions that the battery needs to be charged. Is it in fact rechargeable? - I am guessing so but there is no information about battery care.  The only clue is a link to the Bose website, whereupon under this product it says "There is no user manual available for this product".  By the way, I am a Ph.D in electronics of the kind who builds his own computers.  The dissatisfied, desperate purchaser of this product can download snippets of information on how to achieve various objectives. For example there is quite a useful one which tells you which control button is which.  But for $349 Bose owes the purchaser some instructions instead of leaving him/her to flail around trying to find by experiment and trial and error how to use it. I feel insulted! I wanted a product, not a research project - I have better th

title: ... that expired and now you can do a lot better. I bought 2 sets from them and their ...
text: Boise had a patent that expired and now you can do a lot better.  I bought 2 sets from them and their life was short. $600 down the toilet.  The sound is great, if not the best , their service and quality is notoriously bad.  I see from the reviews I'm not the Lone Ranger here. Grrrr.  Some companies need to die and this is one of them. Beware!

title: The incomparable Bose does it again!
text:
Bose has been and remains the best headphone maker. I’ve tried at least a dozen different products on amazon, with perhaps a dozen more in my life, and in all those experiences, the Bose name always stood far ahead of the crowd.

First, their noise cancelling technology remains the best. Eerily effective, especially on airplanes and in cars, trains, or buses, you put them on, and the world becomes quieter, making you realize how terrible the ambient noise was! These have the added benefit of adjustable noise cancellation, unlike previous models.

In comfort, these are built for long-term use, remaining wearable for hours. Most other headphones I’ve tried tend to make your ears sore, but not these! They feel soft and lovely everywhere they land on your head.

With or without Bluetooth, these offer exceptional clarity of sound, powerful bass, and clear crisp treble, none of which turn to distorted noise, even at high volumes. Yes, these are very powerful! An

title: What kind of idiot would pay $300 dollars for headphones? Me.
text:
Back when I used to spend my days fumbling with earbud wires, I wondered "What kind of idiot would pay $300 dollars for headphones, when you can hear your sounds perfectly fine for less than $10?". Even as these products rose more and more to popularity, I scoffed and resisted.

Those days are long gone. After I borrowed them from my friend for a day and tried them myself, I knew why. After getting them, these quickly became a non-negotiable part of my life. I use them to:

- take and receive meetings
- freely go pee, refill coffee, and wash my dishes while listening to a meeting
- go on runs while listening to music
- ignore noisy people around me
- get myself into a state of focus
- calm my anxiety on airplanes
- show people I am not interested to talk to them
- as a fashion statement

Seriously, I wear these so much I think I am developing a bald spot. Maybe that's why all the engineers are bald. The sound quality is s

title: Just Cancels a Few Noises - Rarely connects via bluetooth
text:
I live in a loud neighborhood and was hoping this would at least muffle some of the sounds. It doesn't. You can hear motorcycles, boom cars, sports cars, and car horns when you have them on.

These noise 'cancelling' headphones only cancel out some noises, like general ambient noise. I guess it's just used for people on airplanes.

And the bluetooth connectivity is awful with Apple products. Be ready to constantly remove/add/update bluetooth devices with this hot mess of bluetooth technology.

title: Disposable!!!
text:
Bose used to make their noise cancelling headphones so that they could accommodate a standard AAA battery. The QC 2, QC 15, and QC 25 all used a single AAA battery and would last for 15+ hours. When I went on a flight, I'd carry two Eneloop rechargeable batteries with me, and if one died, I could just remove the dead one and put a fresh one in.

Now, they use an internal, non-replaceable lithium ion battery. So, when the battery reaches the end of its usable lifespan, you must throw the entire product away. What a waste! Even worse, if it happens while you're on a flight, that's just too bad. No noise cancelling for you!

Oh, and let's not forget that lithium ion batteries occasionally catch fire, and sometimes even explode. It's definitely not a good idea to have one next to your head, or on an airplane for that matter.

title: Overall a reliably good headphone
text:
Sound quality is well rounded - though I usually prefer something a bit bass-heavy, this hasn't been unusable by any means.
They are pretty comfortable to wear all day - not totally pain free, and a reminder that wearing glasses with any headphones is usually uncomfortable at the end of the day because of the cuffs pressing your frames into your head.
The battery life has been totally sufficient between transitioning from student to full time employee on the 9-5 grind. I charge every other day or so.
The noise cancellation is great for sitting in a crowded noisy place like a study area or airplane or coffee shop.

They don't look that bad - I thought I would hate the bulkiness more, but it's not very bad. I have a pretty small head and am pretty petite overall.

Second, the sound quality is good only when compared to the "average" headphones/earbuds such as the one that comes with iPhone. It sounds like a disaster even when compared with a JBL Everest 300: flat resolution, messed-up bass. and tasteless overall performance.

title: Be Careful bad customer service
text: They broke in 4 months, called Bose and they wouldn't replace them. I would stay away.

title: Great Product, Horrible Support
text:
Quality of sound and comfort is great- but the warranty is not. I first broke these headphones by stepping on them and snapping the band. Bose said they'd discount me for a new pair- but I still had to shell out another $200 for a new pair. Two years after buying that new pair, and now suddenly the right ear is not working any more. I didn't do anything wrong, the audio just got quieter and quieter, and now it hardly works at all.

The customer service support system is awful. They keep directing me to the "discounted" price, and not actually doing anything to provide support because of a one-year warranty. I'm sorry, but your $300 headphones should last longer than two years without breaking on their own accord. I am absolutely disappointed because I really love these headphones, but I don't want to shell out another small fortune just for them to break like they're made of glass.

title: Great hardware & sound; pitiful software and app makes all wireless features useless
text:
UPDATE February 2019: The latest software update eliminated most of the problems with hijacking my hearing aids and linking with my iPhone is now more reliable. Unfortunately, it is now virtually impossible to connect to my MacBookPro unless I do a hard reboot of both laptop and headphones. That makes the Bose phones useless for videoconferencing, listening to music via laptop, or pretty much anything headphones are routinely used for. It appears that the software engineering at Bose continues to be second rate, and if problems aren't fixed a year after the product was sold - I fear that they never will. This is the last Bose product I will consider...

UPDATE December 2018: None of the problems from my original review have been addressed by Bose despite multiple app updates. title: Great on Sound, Bad on Size
text:
These headphones are great on sound and noise reduction (average user, main use is in a open office space)-- HOWEVER I had to return them.

I have a small head (like wear a child's size large/x-large in helmets), size 7 in hats, and these headphones on the smallest setting "hang" on the top of my head to stay on, making it uncomfortable. They don't clamp in enough because they are too big, and slip out of place any time I bend over or look up. I'm so sad since I love Bose and have Bose ear buds since they are some of the most secure ear buds out there for small little ears like mine.

They fail in fitting petite heads, wish they were adjustable down to a smaller size of just 1/2-1 inch more and they would be perfect. So sad these didn't work out.

title: Saved a Father Daughter Relationship
text: I got these for my father, who is hard of hearing, and loves to listen to LOUD podcasts on the front porch :).  He claimed to "hate headphones" and claimed he "couldn't stand wearing those dammed things."  I researched best headphones for people with sensitive ears and hearing loss.  These came up and had excellent reviews.  I left them laying around the house for a few days and he took the bait.  He can't resist trying a new gadget (he's 80).  He tried them a couple of times and promptly ordered his own pair (which ironically had gone down in price $60 since I bought them the week previously).  It's a dream come true for all who live with him.  We no longer have to hear podcasts blaring all over the house, and he claims he can no longer hear the traffic go by or the airplanes over head.  We are a happy household now.  They were worth EVERY penny (and yes...I kept my much more expensive, identical set).

Tip: do not update the firmware (that means don't pair the headset to the Bose app) no matter what Bose says. Don't even download the app.

title: Never knew what I was missing
text:
These are phenomenal headphones. I didn't realize the kinds of ambient noise I had around me until after I used this for a while. I bought other fancy headphones in the past, which usually had cables and ended up broken when I tripped across it. These are wireless, with a volume control built in. (You can still plug them in if you like, but I never do that.)

When I got them in the mail, they worked perfectly. Then, after an update, for a while, the software was a bit glitchy and would randomly beep. But at some point they must've fixed that in another update, after a year or so. Because they work perfectly again.

One downside is that the microphone isn't really worth having on it. In order to use the microphone, you have to put the headphones into "headset" "communications device" mode, which doesn't properly do sound cancelling. Worse, it sounds like someone is playing back the audio from outside the headphones. I never use that mode, because I can't stand it.

title: Infinitely better software than Sony
text:
Bought and returned a Sony 1000XM3 after discovering how terrible the mic is, and how incredibly frustrating the bluetooth connections are with every device I've tried.

Got one of these instead. Immediately happier with it in every way, except that I think Sony's noise canceling is slightly better (though this is still excellent). It installs updates in less than an hour (seriously!), connects to two devices reliably, can switch devices without forcing you to disconnect manually or sometimes forget a connection to stop re-connection, and you can do a fair bit from the app that's handy (e.g. change the second device it's connected to, quickly and easily).

Sound quality is roughly on par with the 1000XM3, but mic and everything to do with bluetooth are incomparably better.

title: I will NEVER buy Bose again.
text: This product must be meant for indoor typing folks who just can't stand the hum of a fan or a fly that keeps swarming because that's about all they are good for. Yes they cancel some light noises, but very very little. They are not for anyone who really wants to get hearing protection. Also, if you work outside, DO NOT BUY THESE!!!!! You can hear the wind in your ear magnified by the "noise cancellation" of the headphones. I was so sad because I hoped they would help me retain my hearing as a farmer, but they are the pits. I put some $10 ear buds in and put some $10 headphones over and I had 6 times better "noise cancellation" and no wind noise. They are comfy and shinny, so that's a nice perk for you people who care about those things, but other than that you'd be better off with some Sony earbuds from Meijer.

title: The BEST value! Couldn’t live without them. (6 month review)
text:
I’ve been using these for 6 months and they are invaluable to me. They are quiet enough for doing loud chores like mowing the lawn or vacuuming.
I also use them while I commute on the train. Even with the noise of the train cars and other people around me I’m still able to make phone calls while listening and speaking clearly with these headphones.
I love music and they are also loud enough for my taste (I’m moderately picky about audio).
Battery life is great it’ll last me all day listening to podcasts, music, and making calls.
They seem to charge back to %100 very quickly
The buttons are easy to use. They make answering calls or pausing/skipping songs easy.
They also come with a very nice case that I use often.
Overall these headphones are great and well worth the money in my option.
I would absolutely recommend these to a friend.

title: Bought 2nd Bose QC 35 Headphones, excellent sound
text: This is a 2nd Headphones. Now my wife and I both have the headphones. The sound is good, wears comfortably on the head. Battery Life is good.Pairs with other Bluetooth devices easily.

title: Ok but a little over rated
text:
I feel like they're a little over rated, yes they cancel out noise better than most of the competition but the sound hasn't exactly blown me away.

Also, im always Taking public transportation to get to work, the bus goes through the freeway and as soon as the road gets bumpy I start to hear stutters of air which ruins the sound and cuts it off.

title: Loved them Immediately
text:
Very sleek design and the comfort is amazing.

Noise canceling is so great that I wouldn’t use option while walking.

title: Nice headset but broke after 7 months
text: Worked great for about 6 months. Then one dat I was in a meeting and everyone told me they can hear everything around me. Just stopped working. I would not purchase again.

title: The sound reproduction is EXCELLENT.  They always connect, very comfortable on the ears, not hot or heavy.
text: WOW... I use them to listen as I watch movies on my Microsoft Surface and the sound reproduction is EXCELLENT. Plus they always connect, very comfortable on the ears, not hot, not heavy and wow, they really eliminate the outside ambient noise. I'm pretty impressed by how capable or effective the noise cancelling feature is. I choose the silver and no one plays with them. My guess is they don't look impressive or high tech enough. Works for me....

title: Not good for Noise cancellation.
text: This is not a good headphone for Noise cancellation. I can hear other people talk. I was expecting silence. But I like the sound quality.

title: My hands down favorites, and I've tried a lot
text:
I have a war torn battlefield of bluetooth headsets on my desk and scattered around. I have $20 ones, I have $200 ones. I have ones I like, ones I hate, most in the middle.

This is the first set I loved.

The noise cancellation is great. The sound is excellent. They're comfortable for all day wear (6-8+ hours). They connect seamlessly to everything (you'd think this was a given...trust me, it is not). The range is fabulous. They connect to multiple devices easily, meaning I can be wearing them listening to music on my computer, keep them on as I walk out of the house and start up a podcast, and not touch them once. I just love love love these headphones. I wish I could go back in time and tell myself not to bother with any others. These are the ones.

title: Comfortable but very flawed
text:
The good - very comfortable for long periods of listening, battery lasts a good amount of time (I use these for lots of plane travel), great noise cancellation

The bad - the bluetooth pairing is so frustrating. it randomly picks devices sometimes if you have paired to more than 2 devices. it only connects with 2 devices at a time but even then it doesn't always work - if you pair with your PC and iPhone, it will never play music from your iPhone. it's so frustrating because you can only control what it pairs to using the app, which is useless when I want to listen to other devices unless I'm also paired with my phone. Additionally, if I deselect a device on the app, it will still connect to it when I turn the headphones back on.

TLDR
Get these if you only want to pair with a single device. Otherwise, the pairing controls and app are a complete, unstable nightmare.

title: High quality product
text: Absolutely love them. I’ve had them for a week so far. The battery life is about a full day with the noise canceling on high. I enjoy the sound quality and the noise canceling is fantastic; i can still hear a voice if they are right next to me, but in general it blocks out a significant portion of the office noise around me. The build is sturdy and the case is just the right size

title: DO NOT BUY THESE - unless you like disappointment.
text:
I received my first pair of these headphones last November. I'd never spent this kind of money on myself for such a luxury item. When they arrived I was over the moon because the sound quality was amazing. About six weeks later, I took them off my head and place them on a side table. And I am very careful with the things I own. I never had much money growing up, so you learn to take care of your possessions. Anyways when I went to use them again, the left ear had broken off of the head band and was hanging from the wires. I contacted Sony who's response was, we don't cover damage. WHAT? I argued with them for quite a while and a few days later they agreed to replace them.

Fast forward a few months later. Now I am EXTRA carful with my second pair because of the problems with the first pair. I am literally removing the headphones from my ears when the same exact thing happens again. There was NO WAY that I broke them. I have all the parts (see image) and the plast

title: Very Solid For Out-of-Office
text:
