title: Standard earbuds — wired
text: You’ll need to plug these into your phone with the audio jack. This is NOT a lightning connector  and it is NOT wireless but if you can still use earbuds that plug in these fit fine and the sound is decent. I don’t think they are stable enough for a lot of moving around but if you are more or less stable, these will do the trick. Earbuds have enough options and I found them to be very comfortable.

title: Like these so much I bought 3 pairs
text:
These are the only earbuds that actually fit in my ear comfortably and don't fall out when I move my head.
I still like the kind that have a behind the ear clip if I run but it isn't convenient if you are taking them on and off alot. These are perfect inexpensive earbuds for everyday use. Fun colors too!

title: Cheap and "OK"
text: These are kind of fragile, but work. Bass isn't good, not the greatest sound replication, but for the price there's no complaining. Except I also bought a pair off Amazon that cost a whopping $3 and they work the same. Same sound, same quality. Skullcandy is the one to beat, but I don't like using those where they might get wet or destroyed.

title: Quality of sound is stunning!
text: Works just like everyone else said. Since I am 6'2", it is a tad bit on the short side (the cable length) but besides that tiny issue, its great! Sounds are balanced but do not skimp on the bass.

title: worked great :)
text: Hubby bought me a galaxy x tablet and I love it but the volume is very low.... These took care of the problem... Thanks :) they came VERY quickly!! Great customer service!!

title: Tinny with no Bass
text:
These sound better than the buds that came with my mp3 player. The smallest earpad is too small for adults and will probably fit children. The other two are adult sized. The design of these is a little odd as the earpad is offset a little bit from the bud. This unconventional design may bother you if you have very sensative ears. Jan 2012.

Update Sep 2012.
Panasonic must have changed suppliers. I purchased a second pair, and while they looked the same they sound horrible. About the only good thing is that they don't fall out of my ear. I cannot recommend this product.

title: Good value
text: They fit in the ear great and don't fall out. My only complaint would be that the cord is a little bit short

title: Great Headphones
text: Great pair of headphones for the prices. Cheap enough to replace when broken. I have 3 of them and typically keep one in my backpack, on connected to my laptop and one just for generally use. Pretty good sounding music and feels comfortable going into your ear. just brought another pair as of today for a friend.

title: Earwax
text: Creates a wet inner suctioning and buildup of earwax

title: Great Value
text: These earbuds have great sound, they fit in my ear comfortably and don't come out unless I want them to.  Great value for the price I paid.  I have paid over twice as much for similar earbuds and these sound way better.

title: Good product
text: This ear phone is awesome. Worth the money!

title: Good value.
text: Good headphones for what I need them for, which is the gym. Reasonably priced too.

title: So so
text: I gave these a try, but the bass isn't that great. My cheap Coby's are much better & that is what I'll stick with.

title: Nice quality
text: I use these with my phone for music or movies.  I don't bother anyone that way.

title: Not as comfy as I thought
text: Just got em today, so can't speak of their durability.  DEFINITELY NOT the bass capacity, but they're lower end. Not as comfy as I thought, even though my ears are small.  But my ear canals are sensitive.  I've had these types of ear buds before, but the other ones fit much better--but then again:  These are low-end ones, so I shouldn't complain.  Perhaps I'll try changing the size of the rubber ear plugs on them, that may help.  I'll have to take off one star for less comfort.

title: Great for the price
text: My $100 Bose earbuds finally died, so I bought these as a cheap replacement. The sound is pretty good. I certainly couldn't expect the bass and sound quality from the old ones, but these are definitely decent.

title: They work great
text: I got these are my on the gone headphones. They work great

title: These are fantastic. Im kind of an earphone snob
text: These are fantastic. Im kind of an earphone snob, but I dont have a lot of money. These worked better then most the super expensive ones I tried. Loud, good bass, and great at cancelling out noise. Highly recommend

title: New favorite headphones!
text: New favorite headphones!  Absolutely love them.. fit in ears great. Best headphones I've bought in the last year. No more searching!

title: Outstanding
text: Sound is loud and clear, price was great. What more could you ask for?

title: Good headphones for my tween son
text: Good headphones for my tween son. He likes them. Says they fit well and are comfortable to wear. Now if he can just keep up with them!

title: Love Them!!!
text: They last me for about 9 to 10 months until I have to get new ones. For $6 to $7 that really good.

title: Good buy, but my ears deserve better!
text:
Pros:
Nice length
Fit:
Stays in ear while exercising
Design:
Very lightweight. Comes in many color choices.

Cons:
Sound is is "just" ok for $10
Hurts inside of my ears after 15-20 minutes
Cord is very thin

Overall, its just good for emergency back ups but doesn't have the quality of the better headphones out there.

title: Too Big for 12 year Old's Ears.
text: Good headphones but still too large for my 12 year old's ears.  I wish they'd make them more kid sized.

title: RETURNING/DEFECTIVE/MISSING PARTS.
text: There were no adjustable ear pieces in with the packaging. The sound was broken/intermittent. Returning these ASAP!

title: Last long
text: These earbuds last for pretty long. I am pretty impressed. I think the price is reasonable for the quality

title: Pretty sweet sound and price
text: Bought these for my wife to use with her iPad and she likes them.  Got them on a sale price of around $7 with free shipping, and its hard to beat that deal.

title: Good Value
text: Use these at work which cancel the noise yet can still hear the business around you.  Good cord length from my computer but would prefer a wrap over ear style to ensure they don't come loose.  Comes with extra ear pieces for extended use.  Great product

title: Five Stars
text: Great for children and not a bad price.

title: Five Stars
text: Durable, affordable. Can't go wrong with these if you are on a budget.

title: Impressive upgrade for a fitness user from Gear S2 Sport (3G)
text:
9/18/21: Reducing review to 1 star. Watch began bootlooping last week and I wasn't able to revive it. I opened the watch this morning to find it full of water. Ive only used the watch as directed, and it doesn't seem to be up to the task. I love the watch functionally, but ultimately if it can't meet its own product description, it is useless. After researching the bootloop issue, it seems that water ingress is a common concern amongst owners.

Skip to the "REVIEW" heading if you don't care about the factors at play that influenced my purchase.

Update #3: Just about 7 months in of daily use by a very active user. I probably use watch GPS for 10 hours a week on average, and the watch has spent probably a hundred hours in the pool. My battery is at 22% with 4 days and 10 hours on the battery. My opinion of the watch has only improved. Despite daily use in rough environments (cramped car engine bays, chlorinated pools, etc) the watch still looks brand new. Ther

title: Not Compatible with Android 10
text: The "Samsung Galaxy Wearable" app, which is required to pair and manage this device, is not compatible with Android 10.  Worthless purchase for me.

title: UPDATED: I like better than Apple
text:
20 Mar 20 Update, still like the function of the watch other than the fact after only 4 months of use the battery life has gone from about 2 days before charges to only about 6 hours with minimal use. Knowing this, I would not have bought it.
***********
I've been a huge iPhone fan for years and also had the Apple watch 3 with dedicated line to go with my iphone 7. Due to frustration with not getting call and texts on both my phone and watch I decided to take the leap to android and traded in my iPhone 7 for a Galaxy Note 10+ and purchased the Galaxy Active 2 smart watch.

1st the Pros for the watch: I found it to be a mutch brighter and easier to read display. I love how easy it is to customize the display and the huge range of display options. The battery life is unbelievable. I charge it about every 2 days which is longer than they advertise. The round shape I like better than the square apple watch in which I'd always accidentally hit the side buttons if I bent

title: Great... AS LONG AS YOU DONT WANT EKG
text: Item was returned. It was advertised by Samsung as having EKG ability and so sense my Apple watch did it very well I expected the Samsung watch would also. Upon receiving the watch I came to understand that Samsung still has not had the EKG function cleared through the FDA and therefore it is not functional. That was the whole reason for upgrading so I returned it because it did not do what they promised that would.

title: Horrible. Do not buy!
text:
Amazing quality! I am so happy with this watch. I can control my music right from my wrist. I am in love!

UPDATE!!!
I bought this less than a year ago. I bought it in November of 2020. It's now September of 2021. A few months ago, the watch kept saying "Overheating. Powering down", or something similar. Well, an update fixed that. But last month, I couldn't get the watch to power on. I tried everything possible. I contacted Samsung, and they paid to have it shipped in, and fixed (supposedly). Well, now less than a month later, it won't turn on again. It is showing an icon that I can only guess means that it is too hot. The room it's in has an AC less than 5 feet away set at 72 degrees Fahrenheit. Also, it is completely cool to the touch. I have tried everything again. It will not turn on. It has been on the charger for over 24 hours. This time, the screen comes on, then it shuts right back off and has this logo. If I can't get my money back, then I will never buy one of

title: Waranty does not cover water damage for a watch that they claim you can swim with
text:
Device didn't last even a year. Warranty doesn't cover liquid damage for a watch that they say you can swim with. I never swam with it, it got wet from sitting on my counter in the bathroom. They quoted me a price of $33 MORE than I paid for the device new to repair it. While the support people were kind, they were unable to help me in any way and refused to do anything to adjust the cost of the repair.

DO NOT BUY THIS OR ANY OTHER SAMSUNG SMARTWATCH.
THEY ALL SEEM TO HAVE THE SAME WATER CLAUSE IN THE WARRANTY FOR ALL OF THEIR WEARABLES

title: Great for monitoring your heart health!
text: No more reaching in your pocket to read texts or take calls. Very convenient to answer calls while driving, or see who is calling and skip. Great for using google maps, directions show on watch! Great way to track heart bpm, and can set high/low alerts. Afib detection coming soon in an update! Battery life excellent even with constant heart bpm monitoring which causes a constant green flashing light underneath on your wrist. I recently have had palpitations and went into afib while simply relaxing at home, which prompted my purchase. I am very pleased so far!

title: Great watch, short battery life
text: I love my watch. My only complaint is the battery life is about 24 hours and that's with just general use. I don't sync my emails. I use it as a Walk tracker and get my text messages.

title: Don't waste your hard earned money on this!
text: I bought this for my hub for Xmas.  He was excited to receive it and got it up and running around Jan 15th, once things settled down.  Worked great for about 2 wks then would no longer sync w phone which is a Samsung.  Tried resetting and nothing.  About 2 days ago after normal recharging, the watch got really hot and does not work!  What a royal disappointment not to mention waste of money that I don't have.  And of course timed perfectly for the 30 day return to have passed. Guess that's what you get when you're loyal to Samsung!

title: Good watch for someone who doesn't wear watches.
text: Being in heavy construction, didn't quite hold up. That's why I shouldn't wear watches. Big scratch on face second day, even with screen protection. Give away freedom with sleep monitoring, but love it. Can see sleep patterns and 02 saturation. Voice recognition could use work, or maybe I need to define my words better.

title: What a difference money makes
text: Boy what a difference money makes purchase a cheap smartwatch off of Amazon had to send it back it didn’t work so decided to save up and get hubby the Samsung watch ....much better he loves it u can’t tell him nothing it works great fast even and no freezing or shutting off came in the original Samsung box with charger of course u have to get the piece that goes into the wall but who cares it works great and he is in love that u can charge it on the back of his phone

title: Owned it for 2 years and satisfied
text: Overall, I am happy and satisfied with this watch and have had no issues at all after using it for 2 years. I now moved on to Galaxy Watch 3 but this watch is still one of my favorite smart watch.

title: lot of features in a tiny package
text:
I bought this for the heart rate monitor, and the ultimate selling point..the ambient light sensor.
being able to see the display outdoors in the sun is VERY helpful.

pros:
way too many to list them all, but I'll call out a few.
ability to answer calls when hooked up to one's phone via bluetooth.
ability to use the watch as a speakerphone when answering said phonecalls.
ability to control music playback..no it does NOT matter what app one uses for playback.
so many watch faces and customizable ones at that, that its seriously not funny.
expandable capabilities, simply add the requisite app to the watch.

cons:
the wrist band has a tendency to get slick with sweat and feel all scuzzy and slimy in humid weather, a more breathable band would have been a better choice than this tpu polymer band.

observations:
depending on how often/long you have the display active and what features you enable, the battery will eit

title: love it!
text: I love my watch. Ive always been a fitbit user.. but this is a game changer. It's literally like have a littlw phone on your wrist plus all the perks of a fitbit!

title: Don't Purchase - Back Plate Fell off in 4 months
text: After owning for approximately 4 months, worn in a casual environment, completing 3-4 cardio works outs per week. NEVER got wet, except for some body sweat (it is a fitness watch so this should be expected). The Heart Monitor Plate / Back Plate Came off ( literally stuck to my wrist).  This was not covered by warranty and Samsung indicated it would cost approximately $150 to repair.  The battery stopped working shortly after, would not charge.  My last Samsung watch was also well taken care of and the battery began dying rapidly (2-3 hr charge max) after 1 yr.  I love Samsung phones but I will never purchase a Samsung watch in the future. Cheap construction/assembly with a functional phone app.  Don't purchase the Samsung Galaxy Active 2 Watch unless you want to throw your money away.

title: Pretty good watch
text: I really like the watch I got it on sale plus 20 bucks off. It has so many features and being able to customize everything is amazing. The battery life is ok im getting about 3 days out of it which is understandable for all it does but cheaper watch ive had lasts atleast 10. Good for fitness and notifications. Also calls are clear from the watch and so many ways to text from it. (Voice, quick reply, draw, type)

title: Fantastic Watch
text: This is an incredible watch. The accuracy is great when using to work out. Very happy with my purchase

title: Samsung doesnt stand behind their products
text: I bought their watch and its been a bane on my life. Biggest rip off ever. Wait another decade before these things are useful

title: Not a good fitness tracker!
text: I have the stainless steel one and I have to say thebbame active is just not warranted the GPS has a software problem where its take more then 10 minutes to start tracking. This is a known issue by Samsung and instead of sending put an update they release a new set of watches with the new software. Other issues includ rais to wake function is hit or miss you have to realy aggressively rais your wrist for it to work. Also the auto pause on the workout app is terrible, if you stope in the middle of a workout it will pause then play then pause and play again till you get moving again. I wouldn't recommend this watch at least not qt this price point.

title: Liked the watch until the 6 month mark
text: I do like this watch, though not as much as my previous Fitbits or Garmins. The watch worked great for the first 6 months, but now the battery seems flaky. The watch will be fully charged and show for example 18 hours of battery life. Even on a non-exercise day by 4 PM it will ask me if I want to go to power saver mode as it is down below 10% power left. Also if I click on run or walk now whereas it used to go to the countdown almost immediately it can now take 1 - 2 minutes. Also if I go into the pool, just standing in the pool gets picked up as swimming. I need to take the watch off. The app is okay but nowhere as good as Garmins or Fitbits, plus Samsung has no website, so is a big loser there compared to other fitness trackers.

title: Battery Life is Terrible
text: My Samsung watch has a terrible battery life. I have to recharge it at least every third day or it goes dead. I think a watch this expensive should have a battery that lasts at least a couple weeks. Three days is terrible. Fortunately, I received this free as a promotion with my new Galaxy phone. If I had paid for it, I would have sent it back. Samsung products have generally been very good, but this is one that I can not recommend.

title: Love this watch
text: Just got this watch absolutely love it. I got the 44mm  I thought that I might be too big at 1st because I have a very small wrist  But it fits perfectly Good battery life so far Have had it on for 12 hours and is at 76% still.  Definitely worth the money I have it paired with a Samsung Galaxy S20 plus. Does everything you want it to and more. I do also have the case that also covers the screen on it that I bought on here for like 5 bucks

title: Not Durable
text: Had the watch for less than a year and it just stopped working. The screen just started to show lines. It was not dropped or bumped. Worse I had it in a protective covering. It wont shut down for a restart.

title: Stopped working in 1 year 4 months
text: The watch stopped working in 1 years 4 months.  It's too much money to spend on something that won't last.  I sent it to repair center.  They requested $150 to repair the watch. I think it's ridiculous to pay so much money.  Shame on you,  Samsung.

title: Stylish,  well made,  extremely functional
text: Bought for my grown son, for Christmas.  He loves it!  Excellent choice & considering how hard he is to buy for, I made a great purchase.  It receives calls, text messages, reminders.  It tracks his sleep, wake times, & heart rate.  So far, so good.  Nice look & couldn't be happier with our purchase!

title: Horrible heart rate tracking, everything else decent.
text:
Compared real time heart rate to pulse oximeter and old fashioned finger and wall clock pulse count and this thing is horrible. Also while not accurate, it's also jumpy, going from rest rate to like high 100s for no reason.
FORGET about this ever doing the more advanced heart functions that Samsung promised like EKG... no way in hell this hardware gets there.

Otherwise the display is a delight, the sleep tracking seems decent enough in catching the real go to sleep time and wake up time... as far as LIght vs Deep vs REM, who knows how accurate it is, but total time of rest seems reasonably accurate.

title: I have had a lot of issues with mine
text:
there are a lot of software issues with this watch. When it works it's great. But the GPS has tracking issues and the auto pause is useless. I'm pretty sure that the sleep tracking is just guessing about the state of sleep you are in.

I've hard reset mine probably 7 to 8 times because something would drain the battery in like an hour then take 10 to 12 to charge back up.

I finally sent mine in on a warranty issue and haven't had any hardware issues since buy my wife's is starting to do the same things.

I really wish this watch did the things its capable of doing

One last note. Samsung costumer service is top notch and they worked really hard to get my watch fixed under warranty even though the warranty had expired.

title: For the price, this is a terrific smart watch!
text:
The first thing to wrap around your head is that this watch doesn't sync up with a lot of Western apps, such as MyFitnessPal. I hope that changes in the future, because this is a terrific piece of gear for a very competitive price.

The other thing is that your customer support (and much of the manual/app data) is translated from Chinese, so it comes across as odd. (For instance, after stopping the exercise timer, you'll get a message stating "Does it complete the exercise?" It just sounds weird out loud.) However, when I contacted them with a question, I received a very detailed answer within 24 hours.

There are the standard problems with the pedometer, just as with any smart watch, in that it tracks any arm movement as a step. The sleep tracker is pretty accurate and monitors your heart rate to track normal sleep and deep sleep patterns. The battery life is also terrific, although I'll admit that I don't have a constant heart rate monitor going, which I assume wo

title: Needs the App more than I thought it would.
text:
It's a big display. On the app you choose different layouts or designs.
It's a good watch.
I am not a big fitness person, but it tracks my steps, heart rate.
Easy to charge-magnetic connection, easy break away without damaging watch or charger.
I turned off getting texts to the watch. Aside from the print was too small to read, it was annoying and a battery drain. I am connected enough.
I was disappointed that the only way to start/stop BP is with the phone app. I hoped it could be accomplished on the watch itself.
I don't wear it to bed, so I don't track sleep. I find it comfortable during the day, but to bulky for sleep. The app is OK, not complicated, but I am not using it to its full advantage.
I wanted a reliable watch, large display, that gave me some fitness tracking and was waterproof. I got that. If you are a very serious and active fitness person, you may want to invest in something higher end.

title: OK as a basic activity tracker/watch.
text:
Update - 12/8/22

I do have a couple of updates for my review. I went from 4 to 3 stars, mostly due to durability issues. Over the 5 months I have had this watch, it has gotten scratched very easily. Granted, this was typically on the type of thing that would also scratch anything else made of glass, such as taping a painted brick wall, but the scratches also lead to a crack in the watch face. The crack is currently about 2/3rds of the way down the screen diagonally. The band also tore off. Replacement bands are easy to find and cheap, and I do wear the watch in the gym and other more rigorous activities, but still, I'm not running triathlons or anything like that. Also, and I'm not sure if this is related, but the sleep tracking doesn't always work. I can't really say why. For sure, it doesn't record naps, but also, it will decide I'm awake and then stop tracking sleep. It will say that I slept for 2 hours when I really was asleep for 7-8 hours. I would say it's good for abo

title: Great Watch for Someone on a Tight Budget
text: This is a great starter watch and I love the different face options. I have found the heart rate, blood pressure and oxygen levels to be pretty accurate. I visit the doctor's office often and I always compare my results with theirs and the the results are pretty much on point. I feel that there needs to be more options for exercise because some of us can't do traditional exercise for various reasons some some additional options such as chores around the house or a general exercise category would be great. I am a teacher and I am still moving boxes from my storage unit to my classroom and from my home to the classroom but there is not a category for that type of work. I am also doing work in my house which would qualify as exercise but there is not a category for that either. One of my main issues with the watch is that it does it consistently keep track of my sleep which is something that is very important to me. It was great in te beginning but now it does not track my sleep every nig

title: Inaccurate Junk
text: Every little arm movement triggers multiple steps. I apparently walked nearly a whole lap just drinking coffee while working. Also, the heart rate monitor is wildly off. I calibrated it with a stopwatch, and instead of the 65 bpm I was counting, it regularly shows my around 100 bpm. Don't make my same mistake. Save your money for a good watch. Expect to pay over 100 USD for a usable watch.

title: Very nice smart watch
text: I really wanted a smart watch and this one was recommended by a colleague. I decided this try it. It was the perfect size for me. Fairly easy to set up and pair with my smart phone using the app. It has a BP function but not sure how accurate it is but the other functions are acceptable. I have not had It long or wore it as yet so not sure how long the battery lasts.

title: Best watch ever
text: I was in the market for a watch that tracked my steps. This watch was way more than that, I am inpressed. I can't believe all the features you get for a watch that cost 40bucks. Why do people buy all those expensive ones from Samsung and Apple. This watch does it all. Super comfy with the silicone band. Battery life is awesome. Super excited about this watch.

title: Not that accurate in measuring BP and your steps
text:
It is ok for basic functions. But is is wildly inaccurate with measuring blood pressure. I tested it in the doctors office and it was about 15 or 20 points different from I the doctors device. Even when I test BP in the watch within a couple of minutes, the readings are off by five to ten points. My blood pressure certainly doesn’t change that much within a two minute span.

Also, measuring the steps is inaccurate. It tells me that I am walking steps even while sitting at my desk for hours. For example, it sent me an alert telling me “You reached your goal “. This being despite sitting at my desk most of the day.

So, it tells time fine and heart rate seems accurate but I would not purchase this watch if I were you. I got it because I did not want to pay $200 to $300 for an Apple watch. Don’t get this if you want accurate monitoring of your steps and blood pressure.

title: It's 99% accurate if worn on right hand
text:
I have had this product for a few months now,
Pros- tracks steps, bp, step counter, heart rate, workout sessions, and app is easy to download and easy to set up and use. Also, battery life lasts 4 days
Cons- heart rate accurate, BP is not even close, workout tracker could have a few extra options.

Every fit bit I have bought the step tracker works and if you move your arms alot it tracks those as steps too.
Left wrist- well it don't track all the steps because my left wrist doesn't swing as much as my right
Right wrist- it is my dominant hand but I wear the watch on this wrist because the left wrist doesn't seem as accurate. All fit bits are this way. But you only need to worry about the fact that the watch has great qualities to it needed I do recommend this watch for all features!
Blood pressure (bp) tracker is the only thing not as accurate the rest is as accurate as a watch can be. Also you can try using Google fit it's free don't require a watch

title: Super short battery life just one to two days
text: Battrey only last one to two day even if you turn all the smart features off.  Get a different on!!

title: Nice purchase for the price!
text:
I received my watch the same day I ordered it. So great. And the watch is very nice for the price. I gave 4 stars because the watch does jump around too quickly at times from screen to screen.
The feature I like is text messages have alot of space. Also many cool clock faces to choose from. Donerton nailed it!
Buy it you won't be disappointed

title: Honest review as of 9/28/2022... You get what you pay for
text:
Had the watch since November of 2021.
Pros:
- Accurate time
- the ability to Customize watch faces... even with a picture!
- Could be mistaken for an apple watch at quick glance
- Long battery life. I charge this watch like once every 4-5 days. And wear it everyday

Cons:
-ALL of the SENSORS are INACCURATE. HEART RATE, OXYGEN LEVEL,BLOOD PRESSURE, SLEEP and STEPS are all INACCURATE. If you're expecting the accuracy of a name brand smart watch, this $30 watch doesn't do it.
- Weather doesn't update
- Only 3 Notifications are allowed. If the text is too long, you can't read it all.
- basic interface
- the app [heroband iii] sucks
- Camera function is stupid. There's no camera on the watch itself. So all the camera function does is act as a shutter button for your phone. Kind of a pointless feature

It's okay if you're getting this watch as a placeholder or starter watch before getting something better

title: Nice watch, but
text: Nice watch. But, hard to I stall.

title: Set up
text: Havent had it long enough to test everything out but the set up is super easy. Go to google play store get heroband111 app and your on your way. If this old lady can do it anyone can.

title: Selling Customer Info?
text: The app required to link the watch to the phone required my credit card information, although the watch was paid  for and the app was "free".

title: Don’t too small
text: We purchased this based on the reviews. I have an inexpensive semi-smart watch/fitness tracker, my husband wanted one that was Rectangular. Based on the stellar reviews we ordered this one. It has two flaws; one when you turn it up so you can see the watch face it lights up and then within two seconds the screen shrinks down to a smaller watch face that’s hard to read the other problem is that the text messages do come through on it but the font is so tiny it’s unreadable. Maybe if you’re 20 years old this will work fine. We are 50 & 60 and the font was just not large enough for us.

title: Make Sure it is Compatible with your Phone!
text: Unfortunately, this was not compatible with my sons phone so we had to return it. He has an AT&T Calypso.

title: Easy to set up and use.  Like knowing my steps count to better health.
text: It was easy to download the program to my Android  phone ti get started.  Love knowing my everyday steps count towards my progress to my overall better heakth.

title: Fitness tracker
text: Not a bad smart watch per say its good for the price u can get a host of notifications and track lots of health features/ stats.

title: (EDIT: 2+ YEARS LATER) Extremely Durable & Reliable
text:
While I do have wireless earbuds, I usually only use them for exercising just because always worrying about a charge is a task and I tend to overlook that. That said, I just wanted something that was wired that I could use at a moment's notice and have great sound with a very good price. These 100% do the trick. I'm not some kind of cork-sniffing obsessive compulsive audiophile, but I like to have great sound with a good balance of lows, highs, and mids when I'm using the audio player on my phone without having to EQ anything drastic. With these, I can run the EQ flat and still get reliably superior sound. If the audio is not good, it's more than likely coming from the sound file and not these (garbage in, garbage out).

These earbuds are extremely sturdy and well built, and the wiring is a bit more heavy duty than most wiring that you'd see on any other earbuds. The controls work well and I have had zero issues or complaints. I really like the mesh bag and tin (like an Altoi

title: It's a keeper! (Update: Okay at best)
text:
Love the color options. Cord feels sturdy, not too much noise when I shake it. Has volume and pause control. Comes with foam ear pieces, but I prefer the softer synthetic ones. Has size L, M & S. Sound quality is pretty good. Bass seems louder compared to other brands, can't tell if it's because it's a slightly more muddled sound or because of the noise cancellation. I love how it has a pouch and metal case to store it. Overall like everything about it so I'm keeping it as 1 of 2 earphone sets.

Update: Big and chunky for an earpiece, kept falling out of my ears and I was just lying there watching on laptop. Forget about working out with these, they don't even stay in without movement. I only kept them because I could get a "free" one for a review. Which I already do whether I keep or return an item.

title: Great Earpods!!
text: I like these earpods alot. The sound comes in clear and the volume controls are easy to use. However, I'm not fond of the noise cancelling much. Other than that there great earpods. I would buy again.

title: Decent memory foam earbuds
text:
I like to use these for watching shows on my tablet. I prefer wired ones over Bluetooth just for battery life issues. I find the apple headphones hurt my ears after wearing them awhile. I picked these headphones for the price range mostly and the good reviews. Here’s what I like about them.
1: Price point
2: Ear comfort. The earbuds are made of memory foam which is nice for long tv binges.
3. The wire is made with a nice braid texture which makes it not tangle as easily. I love that!
4. The mic and volume buttons are easy to use and respond well to my apple tablet controlling the volume and pausing and starting.

My personal cons:

1: There does seem to be a lot of extra “noise” in the cord that I can hear when I move it around or when it rubs on my arm or shirt.

2: This is minor, foam earbuds are memory foam but they are a little rigid at first. The extra sizes provided are great, but they are just your standard rubber replacements o

title: So good that I had to share
text: I feel the need to start off that I am one of the pickiest and hardest to please when it comes to my earbuds. However, these have been exceptional, even with my tiny ear holes as my kids say. I’ve been through so many headphones that were torture due to fit and sound quality. To fit, I’d use kids ones and for sound quality I’d have to give up my comfort. Not with these, the foam tips are amazing feeling, the sound is exceptional, and add in they are super durable ( child and pet tested and they survived with no damage). I was so happy with them when my boyfriend’s headphones died, instead of subjecting to my back up pair {they suck) I let him use these ones. He has much larger ears and they work perfectly for him as well. He is currently sleeping with them in as I am typing this. As he was happily dozing he said he wanted a pair, so I ordered him his own in another color. I’m positive they’ll last a lot longer than the month I’ve had them and longer than the pair my boyfriend killed at

title: Most comfortable earbuds to date.
text: The rubber earpieces that took over the plastic years ago (although iphone hasn't caught on) was a tremendous improvement, but these foam earpieces are that much more comfortable and secure again.  Who knew it: Foam: the next generation.  I got just what I was hoping for better comfort, better fit, and therefore better sound.  And then to think how little they cost me, good job folks.

text: The ear buds are comfortable and the color makes them easier to find on my desktop. But for my purposes, the connection wire is just too short. I'm using them on a desktop computer, but have to purchase an extension cord to make them work. For a phone, it would be fine, but not anywhere near long enough for a desktop. I also wish they either had a USB model or the double connecting jacks because the microphone isn't working with my desktop.

Cons

title: Sturdy, inexpensive and work well.

The plugs are very comfortable. I don't find these to be noise canceling, but they are at least noise muffling. I primarily use these when I am at work or in a Starbucks or whatever, to help drown out coworkers and others. I cannot really speak to sound quality, at least in terms of music, because I primarily listen to talk shows or Hulu reruns in these situations.

One thing I particularly like is the cord. It is some sort of braided material and seems to be sturdy. This is important to me because I have an earbud holder where you wrap the cords around it and secure the ends in a plastic slot, so I feel like that final step won't damage these earbuds. Also, I had an occasion to

title: Replaced my Headset
text: As someone who used to use Sennheiser gaming headsets, I shocked myself by switching to earbuds. Originally, I bought these because I got a helix piercing and was unable to wear my headset while it was healing. However, it resulted me in getting so used to the earbuds that it felt so strange switching back. I really do love these and they have never failed me for the 6 months I've owned them.

title: Fair at best....Symphonized NRG-X  far superior sound quality, durability!
text: Negs are numerous: fair sound quality; wires tangle easily; only small size ear buds are foam, all others conventional silicone; poor plug grab and ear plug grab connectors/durability. Compared to now 3 different prs of Symphonized, at about same $20, Ludos are significantly inferior....returned and bought another Symphonized NRG-X. To expand, listening fr same cell phone, measured by 3 different people, all claimed the Ludos inferior over full spectrum...fr treble/mid/bass. I have worn ear sets for 15+ yrs, run every day, and these Ludos were ~=to $10 Panasonic's in quality.

UPDATE 3/25/20:
Really good and prompt customer service ready to remedy any issue.
For that alone, I am adding extra stars. Customer service counts.
________________________________________________________________
I was really excited to try these out for basic everyday use (commuting/gym). They look great (stylish) - the memory foam is comfortable - the sound quality with is decent - the cable doesn't tangle.

text: I use these for streaming from my ps5 as backup to my Sony lavalier mic. I really appreciate that they came with both silicone and foam ear pieces. They work great as headphones/monitors but the mic is so strong and so sharp that it picks up everything in the room and has a minor echo - adjusting the volume with the on-wire control doesn't work, I have to do it through mic adjustment on the ps5 and have to tune it very low to keep the echo from showing up. That being said, these are my dedicated backup and I do use them quite often and even if the sound's a little less clear than my Sony mic, it's still really good. These would probably work very well with a better audio setup than the ps5 has.

Then in Nov. 2019 I bought a LETSFIT tracker that the screen cracked on within 2 months, had really inaccurate steps reporting, and said my heart rate was almost 200 during workouts (yea right!).

So after hours of scouring Amazon for a better & more affordable tracker, 9 pages in my search for "Fitness tracker" I stumbled across this and the reviews looked great and the look of the watch looked incredible. I’m disappointed in the accuracy sometimes while other times it seems fine. I wouldn’t put much faith in this watch as a fitness tracker as you might want to, but as a watch that can give you notifications, weather, change your music or so on it is great. text: After my Fitbit Versa died, I wasn’t sure what kind of watch to get. While debating it, I bough a cheap watch on Amazon that someone had raved about in comparison to my previous watch. It was about $35 so I got it to use until I made a decision. It was garbage. Strap broke twice, the format and display were awful. I went through 2 of them only because they replaced the first one. I’m still undecided on a sports watch so looked for a different cheap alternative, that’s when I found this. I’m literally no longer debating a Garmin or Galaxy or Apple Watch. Love this thing! Steps and HR are right on the front like my Fitbit. You can easily move things around to fit how you want to access them both on the watch and on the app. Treadmill runs it allows you to adjust the mileage at the end in case it over counts, which it has a couple times. Logs your sleep and other health things. I can’t say enough good things about it! If I had to pick a flaw, I don’t love the band closure but that’s just

title: Sleep tracking is complete garbage
text: Junk. Bought for sleep tracking and it tracks nothing or misses large chunks of time. If you go to activities and select sleep it sends an error message. No other way to indicate when you are going to sleep and it's useless at figuring it out on its own. Tried it on the inside and outside of both wrists and it's uncomfortable at best.

title: Meh App, Solid Device
text:
You have to download the Mi Fit app to even turn on this device on, and there are a few quirks that come with using it. For example, there is no option to see the weather in Fahrenheit, so you have to read the temperature in Celsius. The sleep tracking is pretty solid, but if you go to bed super late it will just assume that you skipped sleep that day. The cutoff seems to be around four or five in the morning, so it's not an issue most of the time. The pedometer has been accurate as far as I can tell. The heart rate tracking also seems fairly accurate, but of course it's limited in the same way that all watch-based heart rate monitoring is.

The watch is most comfortable, through there is a raised bump on the back of the watch where the heart rate sensor is, and that bump can kind of dig into your wrist if you wear the watch too tight. Besides that, of course the rubber/silicon watch band will leave a pattern on your wrist, and it will remain wet under the band for a pretty l

title: Inconsistent notifications, poor clasp.
text: Well only had it two weeks. Came undone randomly three times, the last time I didn’t realize it while playing disc golf so it’s lost. Does not stay secured. And while I did have it, the notifications rarely worked if at all for texts and phone calls. Very frustrating.

title: Much Love for the Global version, there's no need for a more pricey USA version
text: Mi Smart Band 5 is not officially released in the USA and it cannot show the Dollar sign $. Instead it shows the Chinese Y symbol.  Contacted Xiaomi and they told me tough luck and to bug off they don't support the Global version in USA. Well, last I heard, the USA is part of the Global community. So good luck to them ever selling me anything else.  Having said that you understand the reasoning for 3 stars and not 5. The iPhone App is not totally intuitive, but once you go through it a few times it's fine.  I'm sure when they do begin to sell the Mi Smart Band officially in USA it will be much higher priced than the imported Global version. I whole heartedly recommend the Global version to my fellow US citizens. Turns out there is no need to pay more for a USA version. I can be happy with the Y symbol instead of a dollar sign in the few messages that need it. It's not that big of a deal and saved bucks. Bought this for my wife too. Much love for the less expensive Global version, thank

title: Can recommend
text: Stylish, good software. I really like it.

title: Not Even Close to Accurate
text:
Everything appears to work fine with the Mi Fit. It can be taken in a pool, it connects well to the app, it can track sleep, it has great battery life, the screen can be seen in daylight, there is a good amount of data. It has pretty much all of the features I wanted. But none of that matters when the HR monitor is ridiculously inaccurate. The pedometer also is inaccurate but it's at least a bit closer and less important.

The HR monitor would jump from reading 47 to 98 within a minute when my position didn't change. Even at peak exercise, it read my HR as so low that it didn't think I was putting in any effort. But, I still assumed it must be close enough until I had to go to the ER for what turned out to be AFib. I was connected to a heart monitor and my Mi Fit would be reading me at 50HR when I was actually at 135. And it never really got any closer to accurate. When I was released to go home, I used my wife's Amazon Halo band so we could track my heart rate. It read it ve

title: Great product
text:
I have been using this product for last 1 month every single day. It is in my wrist about 8hours every day. I am rating the watch based on the following factors:
* Mobile App : * 1. Mobile App is decent and supports several Watch face options. There are also additional apps that you can download which makes it easier to download several 100 watch faces to Mi app and synchronize in just few seconds.
* Watch Faces: * There are about 10 to 20 watch faces available in the Mi mobile app. But through the additional free mobile app that you can download for Android, I have so far used 50+ watch faces , all of them look wonderful. I am satisfied with the options available to change the Watch faces.
* Battery Backup *: It comes for 3days for me for an average use with about 10+ notifications each day.
* Bluetooth connectivity: * Notifications are very reliable. You can configure any particular app to receive the notification alert (through vibration only, no sound alert)

title: Almost a good buy
text: I did like several things about this tracker.  Nice size, comfortable, worked well, EXCEPT for the weather function. I could never get that to sync correctly and stay synced.  I tried all the fixes proposed online, but nothing stuck.  I like to know the actual temperature -- not just a range of possible temps for the day. (I did return the tracker.)

title: Not for people who work at night
text: I like this band very much considering how cheap it is. The only thing not working for me is sleep tracking. I work the graveyard shift and sleep during the day. My sleep is always registered as 'nap' because the software only registers sleep if you sleep between 8pm and 5am... Please give us the option to change this in the app...

title: not too shabby
text: I love this cheap thing. It works well for the price. It has music but I still havent figure out how to use it. The only thing is that the weather is not of US measurement and I cant seem to figure out how to change that. The app is very simple but lacking setting options. I would still buy it becuase it measures walking steps well. I am not that active so this works for me. It has heart rate and sleep as well.

title: Doesn't work unless arm is swinging by your side.
text: Does not work when holding anything, including while pushing a stroller, holding a grocery bag, or holding a someone's hand. I purchased this to use while walking around with my family and unless my hand is swinging by my side, it doesn't register any steps. After 3 hours at a zoo with my son I was only at 900 steps due to pushing a stroller or holding his hand most of the time. Very sad that it's basically useless unless for everyday use.

title: Couldn't Even Get It To Work
text: In order to even use it, it urges you to "pair first". Okay, cool. I download the app, try to have it send the verification phone to my phone via text. I never get it. Resend in 60 seconds. 5 attempts later, I get on the website to create my account, and do so via email verification. I click "activate account" in my email, and get "this webpage is not working" , many attempts later, I finally give up and will be returning it. Cheap chinese trash. I'd avoid.

title: So many choices for face screen, Great tracker, but beware of the extra bands that are sold separate
text: I love that you don't have to take anything apart to charge it, the magnetic charger is great. They give you so many options for changing the picture on the face of the tracker. This tracker seems like it would be a really good one, it did track my steps pretty accurately and also check my heart rate regularly which was awesome. The battery life seems great, I only had it for 3 days before I lost it ☹️ thanks to the crap extra bands I purchased separately.  The band that came with this tracker stayed secured on my wrist, however I decided to change it to a brightly colored band which was obviously of lesser quality and now my tracker has disappeared. There is even a find my band thing on the app but I had my bluetooth turned off so it doesn't work 🤦. Please learn from my mistakes, enjoy this awesome band how it comes and keep your Bluetooth on just in case 😉. Magnetic charger and crap bands pictured.

title: Awesome! Arrived a week early
text: Love it! What I don't like is if I do an indoor walking exercise, it will not track it. I did one of Leslie Samsone's walk videos yesterday, and because I didn't leave my house, it wouldn't record my exercise. I have no record of it in my workouts. It did count my steps but not the activity. I will use Treadmill or Freestyle to see if it records my workout next time.

title: Mediocre HR accuracy and steps counter, but good on a budget.
text:
Pros:
1. Battery life is king. This thing conserves power well. That said, it reports that power is low when it's not on one of its screens (not the home screen), so that's pretty confusing.
2. Screen is small but vibrant and feels responsive enough for easy input.
3. Bluetooth to your phone won't kill your phone battery... it barely uses the bluetooth connection, though.
4. Heart rate BPM is fairly accurate for a watch form factor. Works well.
5. The steps counter is presented well and gives you an "amount left until goal" objective to work toward. You have some control over the goal via the mobile app.
6. Stats are stored via the mobile app and seem pretty convenient. Not sure if you can export the data, but I'll look into that.

Cons:
1. Battery indicator on home screen seems accurate, but another screen will contradict it.
2. App leaves a lot to be desired. Many features simply seem to be missing, like a tab for alarm management or

title: Always runs away
text:
The product works fine especially for price.

The band is so comfortable you won't even notice as it slips randomly from your wrist. Always waiting for the opportune moment it runs away when you least expect leaving you searching the floor for where that pesky watch went. So you use a different band only to find it still has that pesky habit of running away. I have a dozen bands, and they all cannot keep that watch on my perfectly normal sized wrist. I am not chopping down trees in the forest or going on kung fu fighting adventures. it is perfectly complacent to rest on my wrist if I'm doing a HIIT workout. It waits, and when you least expect it, it ceases the moment and flees.

I've had it less than a month and it is gone. I haven't left the house, but somehow my watch has escaped it. In the distraction of making dinner it ran away.

title: The watch needs a subscription to work all function
text: Nice watch but without the buyer getting their subscription, many functions will not work.  So be aware if you purchase this watch.  Otherwise it's ok.

title: Great smartwatch worth far more than you'll pay
text: I previously had a Fitbit Alta. This has way more features for way less cost. The charge lasts at least 5-6 days and that's with continuous heart rate monitoring on. The app isn't the fanciest around, but allows you to get what needs to be done. It's not good at tracking movement if your arm isn't moving. Walked around with hands in pocket to keep warm and it didn't track steps. Otherwise it is comfortable, not too large on the wrist, and works great.

title: Band doesn't stay on
text: Wrist band was difficult to put on and didn't stay secure.  Can't give an accurate rating of the smart wristband because it was lost the 1st week my 15 yr owned it.  He wouldn't wear it outside the house because it would fall off and he was afraid he'd lose it.  He was going out sledding and wristband was on counter and I asked why he wasn't wearing it and he said he was afraid of losing it.  I helped him put it on and sent him out. He came in later and said it fell off and he lost it!  What good is a fitness tracker if you can't wear it when active!?!? I've contacted seller and they said contact amazon for a product exchange.  When amazon was contacted they said it's not covered under warranty because the product has to be returned.  I can't return the defective product because it was LOST!  I read lots of reviews and thought this was a good product and perhaps we just got a defective band but not sure it's worth the risk of buying if company doesn't stand behind it's product.  Very d

title: App is impossible to setup
text:
App is impossible to setup, no instructions with item. Had to send it back. Useless!

The I am not a robot test had tiny images, impossible to select on a phone, but that was not the only problem.

title: Works great for the price.
text: I use this device only for the basic activity/fitness tracking and for the vibrating alarm to wake me up early on weekdays, but for that it works great. Paired to iPhone 13 Pro and it works with no issues. Simple, clear display. Wearable in the shower (unlike my old Fitbit). Tells me when I've sat for too long, default displays local weather, tracks HR and steps, all for under $35. Started using it on Sunday afternoon and now on Wednesday morning it still has 82% charge. I don't normally write reviews, but this device works so well for the cost I decided to rate it.

title: The Band Snap needs to be more secure
text: The band itself does not stay on. I lost it because it unsnapped

title: Works for me!
text: I bought this tracker to encourage me to increase my steps.  It works fine for that.  I like that my text messages show up on the watch which can be useful when I'm working out and don't have my phone within reach.  It has many features that I don't really use, but I can see how they can be useful.  I like that the band is not very big and that there is no metal clasp to trigger my nickel allergy.  The screen is a bit tiny so sometimes it's a bit hard for me to read it.  It's nice that it's waterproof.  The Mi Fit app takes some time to figure out.  I still have not figured out how to get the app to sync with the Health app on my iPhone.  Overall though, I'm happy with this tracker.  I ended up buying a second one for my teenage son.

title: Awesome Functionalities and Long Battery Life
text: This is one of my best purchases. The band is small and light with a beautiful screen. It tracks my my sleep cycles, heart rates, and stress levels. Long battery life too! Even with all these functionalities, it only uses about 10% battery a day. Great value!

title: Salt water kills it...completely dead
text:
Visited the beach and it started buzzing and is now dead. It has a swimming option and I've been to the pool with it so only guess is it's salt water.

No redress from company

title: IOS integration is horrible.
text: The app is terrible.  It cannot sync data with other apps even Apple health after the recent IOS update.

title: Better than I thought
text:
After seeing this product In a news article on Google news I decided to get one on the premise of how bad can it be for $30. I was morbidly curious. I picked one up and after getting it and charging it (it was as dead as a door nail) I got it all set up. The app is pretty good and navigating it is easy. Some things took some digging to find but nothing to crazy.

The watch itself has a lot of features and the display is very nice. The band even though it's just shaped rubber is nice and sturdy. I work with my hands all day and rarely catch the watch enough to unsnap it. All the tracking features works as you would expect. They are accurate and informative enough to be useful. My ONLY gripe with this watch is setting a custom do not disturb setting is a nightmare. It can be fixed so I'm not worried.

Overall I'm extremely impressed. I went into it thinking I'd charge it once and not carry after that but it's a great little piece of tech.

title: No so much
text:
I love this so much. Everything works as it should. For the price I'm pretty surprised. Got it as a gift and I love it. Love how you can change the watch faces. I found a Rick and Morty one!

Updating my review and down two stars. The battery stopped lasting longer than 3/4 days. I can no longer customize the watch faces. I could never use my own photos either. Days it's an option, but doesn't work.

title: Great watch, great price
text:
This activity watch is 100% worth it for the price and the features it has.

Now it's not as fancy as the other smart watch options out there but it does the job.

Pros:
Battery life is great
Lots of smart watch options: sleep tracking, pedometer, heart rate, phone notifications, workout tracking.
Accuracy of pedometer is pretty spot on.

Cons:
The accuracy of the calorie counter is off.
The accuracy of the sleep tracker is off.
No built in GPS- but can sync with your phones GPS.

If you want a watch to help you keep active, count steps, help log activity or push you to be more active, this does the trick.

I would highly recommend.

title: Has been unbeatable for the price and feature set
text:
It's insane how cheap this is for how much it competes against the far more expensive trackers like Fitbit and Garmin. The battery generally lasts me over a full week on a single charge even with the heartrate sensor checking every minute in the settings. Tracking has been very consistent and generally accurate for what I need, and the interface is clean and simple to navigate combined with a screen that can get extremely bright if you want it to.

My only real complaints are with the mobile app. At least on iOS, the app can be a little slow and needlessly convoluted to navigate. The watch has a lot of settings you can adjust through the app to make it work just how you need it, including setting multiple alarms and time ranges for when the lift-to-wake function activates (which you should probably do unless you want this thing waking you up in the middle of the night), but you have to open a whole lot of menus just to get to them. I also just wish some of the information was

title: A terrible product & an equally terrible return policy
text: After the initial charge, it worked fine. However when I attempted to charge it a second time, it would not take a charge even after keeping it plugged in overnight. To make matters even worse, when I attempted to return it, I discovered they penalize you 50% of the cost if anything is missing, even the box it came in! Since I tossed out the box, I'm screwed  half the cost. Does a cardboard box really cost over $20? I seriously doubt it!

title: Finally a watch that the battery lasts!
text:
Ok I've only had it for 3 days, but unlike the past 2 watches I purchased this one is still at 94%,the mobile app Is a bit annoying but the watch works great!
Now I've had it for over a week and at 74% yessss love this watch

title: Excellent battery power
text:
The best part of the device is the charge capacity. It lasts for about a week, while my FitBit had to be charged every day. The band is comfortable, and the steps and heartrate are accurate. The downside is some difficulty in use. It's hard to switch modes and I never figured out how to reset the time for Daylight Savings. The instruction booklet is not great; the translation to English should be better.
If you're looking for a basic tracker device with a great battery, this one is cheaper and better than FitBit. If you don't mind charging it every day, you might want to choose a FitBit.

title: Junk
text: Not even a full year old and the entire screen just randomly popped completely off from the tracker as I'm sitting here doing nothing. I don't wear it 24/7. I don't wear it in the shower, and I'm not doing any extremely strenuous activities. I only really walk with it. The app is terrible and the tracker clearly has quality issues.

title: The GPS disctance tracking is flaky. Otherwise great watch
text: Bought this for my 13 year old son. Many features, about 90 % of what my Apple Watch offers for ~ 10 X less money. The only worth mentioning issue is that the GPS tracking is not accurate at all. Huge discrepancies in the distance of up to a factor 2 (!) compared to my very accurate Apple GPS watch. Looking at the tracked map, there are big jumps, so this is not working well. People on forums also complain about this. There is no clear solution I think. Otherwise the pedometer/step counter based distance estimation without GPS is quite accurate and normally within 10% of what my GPS based Apple watch measures.  Overall, a very nice fitness watch for the money !

title: NOT comparable to other trackers
text:
I've had this tracking band for less than four hours and I already hate it. The tiny but thick user manual is next to worthless - it tells how to 'install' and 'uninstall' the band, yet the band comes already installed (assembled). There are only a few pages of basic 'get started' info, repeated in dozens of languages (which makes the manual so thick).

The Mi Fit app is only a little better. On the surface, it seems to be user-friendly. However, it really only lets you set the basic functions. Good luck if you want to know more about each function.

The band itself - I'll give it points for the variety of displays available. Lots to choose from and easy to change from one to another. And they look good! But nothing happens automatically, starting with waking the display. I should be able to turn my wrist and get immediate response. Nope. I have to touch the display in just the right place to activate. If I want it to tell me my heart rate, I have to tell

title: Toy-like sensor accuracy ruins an otherwise feature rich watch for the price.
text: This is a surprisingly feature rich fitness watch that doesn't break the bank, however, two huge cons made me ultimately return it. First, the Mi app is absolute garbage. It's incredibly intrusive and buggy. I used the Notify and Fitness app which was a huge improvement - and recommend you do the same. Sadly, the toy-like accuracy of the various sensors made me return it. It would frequently spike 30-40 beats high or low for no apparent reason and the sleep tracking was rarely accurate. A "get what you pay for" situation in terms of accuracy. Otherwise it would be a great watch, even with the Mi app annoyances.

title: Almost perfect!
text:
This smartwatch is superb in almost ever way. the display is fantastic. Good battery life. looks nice. feels fine on the wrist. Hundreds of watch faces available through good 3rd party apps in the Google app store. Watch's app software is nice and works as it should, also on Google app store.

I like the idea of the watch strap clasp, but it has already "pulled out" on me. I am hoping that a 3rd party will come along with a standard buckle type. Over 24,000 Amazon Mi-4 reviews out there, any entrepreneurs interested? Two months in and already over 3,000 Mi-5 reviews too. This is my only gripe with this smartwatch, and not worth removing a star over it.

I do not use all the bells and whistles, as in sports related fitness stuff, but I assume they work as does the heart rate monitor and notifications. I would not put my life on the line from any watch gadget anyway. Accuracy is not something I would be concerned with in a watch of this price, ball-park results is all

title: Sleep tracking is as good as the precious model
text:
I have been a Mi band user since the second model came out and once this newest one is out I ordered immediately. The upgrade on this 5th gen is huge.
Pros
The design is even better, the screen is hi-res, the way of charging is improved, the band is used with similar but slightly better texture material.
Cons
my biggest complaint is that sleep tracking isn't working properly, it often can't detect that I was sleeping and I never had an issue with any of the previous models.
I like the minimal and low-profile style. I'm not a big fan of a color screen with a tiny size, and there's no customization I can change that.

title: I love this thing
text: It has gotten me up out of mt seat and moving so that I meet my goals for the day. It is so light you can barely tell it is there. It connects thru the mobile app quickly and solidly and shows you a ton of data about your heart rate over time and your steps taken. It uses all this data and information that you input to calculate how many calories you've burned that day. Plus it keeps track of your sleep and shows you at what times you were in deep sleep or restorative REM sleep. I can't say personally how accurate any of it is, but I did take the time to watch several Youtube reviews about the Mi Band 5 before I pulled the trigger and they seem to show it to be actually quite accurate. All I know is that I could probably buy 4-6 of these for the price of one FitBit. You can't beat the price of this fitness band. And I keep hearing good things about Xiaomi's products. So far I have this band and their Basic 2 wireless earbuds and I have been impressed by both items and use them daily. S

title: I liked it
text: You will lose it because the strap/band its not very resistant to normal use.

title: Good for notifications
text: I've had this over a year and it's still working fine.  I set a daily alarm which vibrates to alert.  I get my phone notifications that I can choose which ones to send to the band.  Also I like that I can change the watch faces to different images.  I bought different bands than the original band because I was concerned about the band coming unlatched.  The bands I have are elastic and no way to accidentally come off.

title: No Explanations
text: While I will admit I am not the savviest when it comes to new tech stuff, there are really no instructions on how this product works or what exactly it does. I have no idea how to do or use the product other than the most basic things. There should be an explanation for each of the features and benefits. At this point, for me, it is an expensive step counter.

title: Love the watch, hate the band
text: I think the watch is cool. I love the face options. What I don't like is the watch band. My watch has come off a couple times to where I had to look for it. It's come off other times, too.

title: Good value
text:
I only needed a reliable pedometer but also something to track my cycling. So my requirements are but 2.
After some research I reluctantly purchased this product though I admit it does the job well and at a good price.

I did give this device the acid test for walking; and twice at that. I walked for about 1 km, then I took a bus for 20 minutes (no it did not count the bus distance), then walked for about another km. Then I went to a very crowded underground mall for about 40 minutes then took the metro back and finished another 1 km home. So lots of stop and go and the device actually did well.

Aside from the acid tests (on normal walks), I will say that at 1 in 15 I found instances that unexplained reason the device would detect me stopped (when I was not). The band would vibrate to alert me that I stopped and would do this repeatedly. However, the issue was not often--most of the time it does work well.

My biggest gripe and this may be my own de

title: I had to send it back for a refund!
text: I had to send it back because the blood pressure screen would go black. I got a replacement and it did the same samething.  I just sent it back and got a refund.

title: Great product
text: My second one my last one I had for over 5 years but lost it! This one is even better.

title: Best balance between tracker and smart watch
text: I've used a number of different smart watches, from expensive to cheap, but this one is my consistent favorite. It is reasonably accurate (less so when it comes to sleep tracking, but then I've not found any that do seem to accurately track my sleep) at activity tracking. I also make regular use of notifications on smart watches/trackers and this one supports all the notifications I need (including Gmail, which some others don't), and gives me enough of a message preview to know what kind of message has come in and what it is about. Battery life is fantastic, at upwards of 2 weeks with full use. Screen is bright and clear and the interface is nicely customizable with a large number of optional faces, including some you can customize. Light weight and comfortable, too.

title: Works well for the price
text: I like how easy it was to setup. The app isn't great but it works. Nicely integrates with sms notification and sends steps to Google fit. Don't rely on heart rate or sleep tracker. Good for a step tracking and watch notifications

title: Great fitness band... But...
text: This is a great fitness band as long as you aren't buying it for the sleep tracking. The sleep tracking didn't work at all even after trying multiple suggestions to fix it. That said, everything else is well done. I would have not returned it had the sleep tracking not been one of the main reasons I wanted it.

title: TERRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE. NONE!
text:
NEw update on previous write up. AVOID LIKE A PLAGUE.
IT failed no reason at all. Just stopped.
Contacted their 833 # and might as well have called no one. Just 2 stepped the issue entirely.

I am using a Letscom. Superior in all ways. SAVE YOUSELVES THIS DANCE. It’s frustrating when the mfg dances on its quality.

title: not for swimming
text:
doesnt really work for swimming.
the stop watch kept on turning on and off or pausing as it made contact with the water.

title: Works great
text: Does everything I want for a fraction of the cost of other smart watches

title: Worst App
text: This watch its self caused a burn mark on my wrist in the shape of the loop the band goes through to latch.  The app has lost my information twice and has made it so I have needed to get another account very disappointed..

title: Accuracy could be better
text: Nice fit, very comfortable, however I wish the accuracy was better, especially in regards to the pedometer.

title: Works ok but very firm
text: It's ok but if you have a small wrist like me it's not particularly comfortable

title: DEFECTIVE BATTERY
text:
I've now purchased two of these units and the battery has been defective on both of them. After 1-2 hours, the battery dies and the device is unusable.

Warning that this vendor will charge you to return the item, even if their quality and control process is to blame.

title: Excelente
text: Muy bueno y preciso con las notificaciones de app todo bien la carga dura mucho

title: Great step tracker at a great price
text: I have friends with $100+ fitness trackers or apple watches, and this $30 band does everything those other trackers do.  I've had it for 6 months, and am very happy with it.  The mobile app is really easy to use.  I tested it with another tracker and a walking map, and it seems very accurate.  I read in some reviews that the band can break after a few months, so I bought a pack of 12 bands in all colors.  I rotate between 3, and all are still in good shape.  Easy to pop in and out of the different bands.

title: Poor step counting
text: I got the Xiaomi Mi Band 5 solely to count my steps.  I have a fitbit and my smartphone also count.  But I don't want to carry my phone while walking, and the fitbit frequently falls out of its band.  The Mi Band 5 was recommended by consumer  reports and rated well.  HOWEVER, where the fitbit counted 12,000 steps and the smartphone (which I didn't always carry) counted 10,000, the xiaomi counted 6,000 steps.  When useing all three together and personally counting steps, the first two were roughly equal, and xiaomi was way less.  I wouldn't recommend the  Xiaomi Mi Band 5 for counting steps.

title: Great!
text: Much better than the fourth generation, cost-effective, long battery life, can detect heart rate, pressure, and sleep, including REM, except for taking a bath, basically every day

title: HR inaccurate for me
text: After using the watch a couple days a really enjoyed some a lot of the features and thought I was going to keep it. But unfortunately it was not able to measure my heart rate accurately while cycling. While cycling 30 miles a climbing 1000 feet of elevation it constantly measured my HR less than 100 bpm, much lower than compared to my chest strap wahoo tickr HR monitor. Even after trying multiple strap tightness settings (looser and tighter), different wrist positions positions and both wrists, I could not get an accurate HR reading. I was really hoping it would be more accurate for me but it simply did not work well on my wrist.

title: Lasted 10 months

I suspect it was held in place with adhesive. I can't really give it a super low rating, because it worked great while I did have it. I work in a fairly active environment, and to say this thing takes a beating would be an understatement. It only shows a couple visible scratches after all this time, and even with heart rate monitoring on, it lasts an average of a couple weeks for me (though I did disable the turn on screen feature when lifting your wrist.) All in all, it was a great device. Can't justify getting another one of these, when the latest model is available. Take what you will from this, but my final conclusion is that this is still a good tracker, despite my issue, it still technically works.

title: Perfect for anyone that has a reaction to other fitness trackers
text:
I bought this fitness tracker on a whim, hoping to find one that didn't irritate my skin. I've used other branded fitness trackers, some of them costing up to $200 that would all cause varying levels of rashes, pain and skin abrasion. Turns out I'm allergic to the nickel in the construction of the trackers.

This is the first fitness tracker that I've been able to wear for extended periods of time (more than two days) without having any reaction to it. I think the longest I've worn this tracker was a full week and only took it off for showering or to recharge the unit.

The app that is used with the tracker is good. It has a lot of information and options in it though it doesnt allow for entering in some body metrics that I would like (you can enter in your weight but you cant enter in body fat percentage unless you have the same brand scale).

The only down side I would say is that the display font is a bit small. I can read the time and steps taken

title: A Big Improvement over the Mi Band 4 Except for One Thing

I got the Mi Band 5 as a replacement for my Mi Band 4, which I bought about 3 years ago. I was very happy with my Mi Band 4 because it was much cheaper than other fitness band on the market then and included only the functions I wanted. Best of all, I had to recharge it only once per month, even with daily use. I gave up on my Mi Band 4 only because eventually it wouldn't recharge the battery any higher than 30%.

So, how does the 5 measure up against the 4? Well, it’s a little more expensive but still incredibly inexpensive compared to other fitness trackers. It has more features than the 4 did, including PAI, stress monitoring and even reports on how long you nap during the day (if you do). The 5 also measures heart rate (pulse) and daily steps like my 4 did. The PAI (physiological activity indicator) does constant monitoring of your heart rate to calculate a PAI score. I haven’t used it or the stress monitoring because constant monitoring drains the battery faster.<

When you place one into your ear, it goes "reeep" and makes a little mechanical noise that I suppose sounds like something starting up, but never fails to startle me. Then there is another little chime to indicate that it has found and paired with my phone, which is always pretty instant. Even if I'm already on the phone, I can pop them in my ears (braced for the "reeeep") and boom, no more holding that stupid phone against my head.

title: Not Worth the Price
text:
I bought these for two primary reasons: Noise cancellation and sweat resistance. Neither is worth the difference between the cost of the AirPods Pro and the “plain” AirPods. The noise cancellation feature is practically non-existent. Try as I might, I could tell very little difference between noise cancellation mode and transparency mode. Outside noises came through loud and clear in both modes. It almost seems as if noise cancellation is achieved by stuffing the silicon tips of the AirPods Pro as deeply and tightly in the ear canals as you possibly can. Some folks ask if they can mow the lawn while wearing these. Well, of course you can, but don’t expect to hear much unless you crank the volume up to damaging levels.

I thought the AirPod Pro was sweatproof. I was mistaken. They are sweat resistant. In the hot and humid South where I live and run, this product will have a short lifespan. I’m not going to gamble away $235 in hopes simple sweat resistance provides

title: If looking for independent pairing, don't buy this.

The new version buds, Sesh Evo, can pair independently, but this shouldn't be considered a bonus feature. Ear buds have been around long enough that this should be standard on 1st gen devices, not added as an afterthought in later models.

Battery life is also an issue. Whether alone or paired, battery life is just over 3 hours. My aforementioned cheaper buds, Splendid Sound iQute, lasts over 4 hours each bud. I can get a full workshift of sound out of my buds before they die out.

Always loved Skull Candy wired buds and headphones before, and this purchase was my first wireless Skull Candy pair, but very disappointing.
