text: Love love love these earphones! I missed out when they were on sale before the holidays however I have no regret in paying full price for them. They fit really well with the tips that come on them but there is a smaller and larger one included if you need to switch it out. The battery life of the earphones I could say in active use will last about 3 hours but after about an hour of charging it’s at full charge. The charging case is cute and compact. The case itself holds enough juice to last to the end of the week with regularly charging the earphones. I’ve always loved the skullcandy brand so when I can across this completely wireless I knew I was gonna get a quality product.

title: It is a bit tricky to pair with a device
text: This is a bit difficult to pair because you can easily switch it into mono mode (listen to audio through one earbud) and stereo mode (listen to audio through both earbuds) and not even know it. The instructions don't mention that so you are better off looking up the product on the manufacturer website which breaks things down very well. Pairing is easy once you are familiar with how it works because it doesn't work like most bluetooth devices. Apparently the left earbud is the dominant earbud and it does show up separately on the bluetooth list as a left earbud to pair with instead of the option to pair both. They are small but they do pack a powerful punch with the audio. It does handle noise cancellation well but there isn't an on/off option that I know of. The case charges the earbuds within an hour and the case itself takes about 2 hours to charge. The battery in the earbuds last almost all day.

title: I use them every night
text: These are great! I use them every night when I go to bed, and listen to audio books.  I can’t even hear the air conditioner or the hubby’s snoring 😴

title: Sounds great!

The price seems suitable for the sound quality.

title: Great bass

They are also quite comfy and I've had no issues with them coming out of the ear unexpectedly.

Neutral: I wish the case had a built in charging cord like some of the competition. Not a necessity but definitely convenient. Additionally, I don't care for micro-USB as I have found it can wear out easily over time. A built-in USB cord would have addressed this as well.

The Bad: Lots of connection issues. Lots of intermittent connection, lots of dropped L/R channel, sometimes some interference noise. Mono mode usually worked out well (using left bud only) but often would drop that, too. Even just bending over at the waist would cut them out if my phone was obscured by my body. Skullcandy warra

I tested a friend’s airpods and with it, I was able to move the volume gauge as low as I wanted to (No this is not an airpods commercial). The clarity is pretty good for bluetooth headphones as is the range from my phone.  Others have said it is difficult to hear me during phone calls so they may not be the best for that purpose. title: A very good watch *for the price*
text:

There’s about 3 watch faces to choose from on the watch, then about 20 on the app. However their all slightly unattractive options. And there’s no way to download more or customize them. Which is kinda a bummer.
Beyond that it does the tracking and you’re able to check weather and change songs.
It only works when your phone is connected by Bluetooth.
There’s no passcodes or personal information on the watch so if it were to get lost or stolen you’re just out $39
The watch seems to be decent quality however the screen scratches pretty easily.
I wouldn’t get this as a replacement for Samsung or Apple watches.
But for the budget watch this is the only one worth getting

Big numbers on the face and easy to read, and lots of faces available or put your own image up from your phone library.  Overall good value in my humble opinion.  Oh.....battery life is amazing. Fit
If there's one good thing I can say about the Powerbeats Pro, is that they don't fall off like AirPods, EarPods and most wireless and wired ear buds do. Anyway, the ear tips included with the Powerbeats resulted in either flat out pain or discomfort. Sound

title: Great Bluetooth earphones
text: This is the 2nd pair of these that I have had.  The first pair lasted me about 3 years.  I loved them because they hook over the ear and I’m one of those people that no matter what I buy, they won’t stay in my ear. So the hook for me is perfect.  I used the first pair day in and day out with out any complaints until they stopped holding a charge and I couldn’t get more than 45-60 minutes out of them.  I figured it was time for an upgrade so I bought the Beats Pro fit. The sound from those was infinitely better and the noise cancellation was awesome.  But they kept falling out of my ears. title: Hurts ears!
text:
First off, they sound great (no noise canceling though) and fit snug but after 20+ minutes of use, they begin to hurt my ears. It's the "hooks" that hurt my ears. Seems that the hook material used is way too hard and positioned where it really grasps your ear tight. The best i could describe the pain is a "really deep ache" behind your ear (where the hook rests). If you don't touch your ear, you won't notice any pain but once you go to fiddle with the headphones or take them off, you'll feel the pain and it takes a solid 10 minutes for it to go away after taking them off. This is strange because Im coming from having the Powerbeats 3 and the hooks on those NEVER bothered my ears.

Really disappointed because I was looking forward to using these for my workouts. Going to return them in the morning.

title: Fits over glasses frames, stays put when running
text:
I wear glasses, and these fit comfortably over my ears and frames. I don’t know how they seat and block out sound for non-glasses people, but I still have awareness of surrounding noises, which I consider a good thing. A lot of earbuds get uncomfortable fast, but the buds on these do not press heavily on my ear canals and my ears do not fatigue wearing them.

They don’t come loose when running for a few hours, and allow enough noise in that I am able to hear traffic and be environmentally aware.

Battery life seems good. I used them constantly on a 4 hour 30 minute run, and they did not die (audiobook, not music, not sure if that makes a difference for battery drain).

The on-ear controls are fantastic. I don’t like touch controls, but these are actual buttons, and are simple to operate: button on the ear pauses/unpauses, rocker button on the top of each earpiece controls volume. I haven’t used them for any calls, so I can’t say how they work on that front

These pair much better with iPhone, interaction with siri, etc. With my android, it disconnects about every 4 hours (for no apparent reason) and I have to repair it. Not a huge deal, but a little annoying. Never did try the Beats App on the playstore, but lots of bad reviews there.
They rest nicely in the ears...I often forget which ear it's in if I have to press the pause button. Never had one fall out, and a little light rain is no problem for them.

title: Great when they work, completely useless when they don't
text: I bought these a few months ago when they were on sale. They're great when they decide to co-operate. However, most times, only one of the headphones will connect and the other is simply silent. Then, when you want to pair them with another device besides your phone, they fail to go into discover mode. The only way to get them to go into discover mode is to reset the headphones, which means your previous pairing is also no longer going to work. This gets you into an endless loop of resetting, and pairing, resetting, and pairing. It's a mess. Another issue is the charging case... sometimes the headphones charge, sometimes only one charges, and some times the case goes completely dead overnight because it sat in some sort of "active" mode for no reason at all. Bottom line, these headphones are plagued with bugs. I recommend going with another, more well-established player in the blue tooth headset business.

title: Poor sound quality  - Not mask friendly
text:
Update: I received my Bose QC Noise Cancelling earbuds and right away I was in awe of the sound quality compared to the Powerbeats Pro. I don't mean to trash on Apple, but not sure why anyone would want any Apple H1 Chip earbuds including Airpods Pro other than for looks.

I really wanted to like these. I like the way they look and fit and I really like the iOS integration and automatic reading of text messages. But to be honest the sound quality is very tinny, flimsy with no depth and will almost always come flying off or get tangled when I take off my mask. My comparison is with the Bose Soundsport Free which I had been using for almost 2 years until I lost the right earbud recently. I will have them plugged in all night and then the right one is at 2%, and the left one at 100%. I’ve tried reseated, resetting, and cleaning terminals…nothing works. Update: These seem to want to work again. Perhaps it was a firmware update on Apple's end, but they're working. I'll give them a couple more stars, but not 5, because of the inconsistency.

title: Garbage
text:
They are the absolute worst. Biggest problem is that, while they'll pair with your phone after some fiddling, they are unreliable - the left and right buds frequently lose pairing with each other. When this happens it creates strange sounds and gaps and means you have to stop what you're doing to try to get them paired again. Try doing that in the rain when you're out running. So I gave up using them in both ears, meaning they're not at all suitable for music. I had let the return period run out, so I'm stuck with them, so now I only use them one at a time and only for phone calls and podcasts, although even for that, the max volume is variable and is often not adequate. The microphone doesn't work consistently either. Batteries don't last long on a full charge after the first month or so (maybe 30 minutes). Sound is only so-so when they do work.

title: Poor sound quality.
text:
High quality construction but absolutely TERRIBLE sound quality. Every thing I’ve read online about these say they are great. Makes me question all the “independent “ reviews I read.
It’s very disappointing because these have no bass or low mid Range response at all. I have $19 generic Walmart(wired) earbuds that blow these out of water in regards to sound quality. They only sound good with voices like a phone call, a pod cast or the voice in song. But for music you are screwed. I have to keep them because I need ear buds that stay put for my mountain bike rides and gym workouts. If sound quality doesn’t matter then these are a must have because the design is pretty much flawless. Great pairing, comfortable, well thought out design and looks good. I like my music to pump me up when I work out. With these that cannot happen.

title: Overrated - underperforms
text:
Great deal from Amazon - less than $150 new!
I do like the way they really hang on the ear - bit of a fiddle though to get them in; however once in they stay secure…but rather bulky.
Sound quality is very average - more like a $30 pair.
No bells and whistles like the AirPods Pro which is now priced in the same range.
The killer for me was the phone quality - people complained I sounded ‘muffled’. Not good for business.
So off they go back to Amazon. Disappointed but I’ll stay with the AirPods Pro.

title: Great
text: These work great!  Finally found earbuds that stay in place!  And the quality is great. I’m happy and would recommend these to anyone who struggle with the earbuds.

title: Not worth the price
text: These are good earphones but they're not $200 good.  I bought them because I have a fit issue with most earphones so I need the overear hook to keep the buds in place.  That part works well.  And once I got the right sized silicone tips on, the fit was pretty decent.  But I didn't like the controls (any time I had to re-seat the buds, I'd invariably hit the pause or change the volume, there just isn't a way to touch them without pushing a button) and the sound quality is OK but not amazing for my novice ear.  In the end, I returned them and went with a $30 pair that sounds fine and fits about the same.

title: Powerbeats Pro from a Android User
text: My first impressions of the Powerbeats Pro are positive. They have great sound, loud enough, and fairly connect quick to my Samsung Galaxy Note 8. I did install the Beats App and AirBattery from the Play Store; so I could check for updates and the battery levels. I'm surprised that I had no issues, considering my phone isn't an iPhone. I can't wait to try them on my iPad mini, if I can pry it from my wife's hands. Overall a great purchase.

title: Stays on Ears
text: I always have trouble with earbuds staying in my ears. These are great.  They stay with no problem and the sound is great!

title: Overall… Very happy
text: I first bought the Bose Noise cancelling earbuds and they were awful. They kept falling out so I couldn’t even tell if they had good sound quality or not. Also, trying to pair them to my devices was a nightmare and the controls were difficult to use. I returned them and got the Beats Prob earbuds. What took me days and days to figure out with Bose (and their support team)……it took me minute with Beats Pros. That is to say, they were extremely easy to put on. They stayed in the ears, had good sound, easy controls  and were ridiculously easy to pair with my Apple devices. I was over the moon happy.

title: To much treble
text: Everything about this product is great except the sound quality.  I have lost some of my hearing at the high end and these headphones actually bother my ears when I listen to music.  My cheap earbuds don't have as much treble and they dont bother me when listening to music.  Everything else about these earbuds was great including fit, phone call sound quality, and use while exercising.  I had to return them.

title: I'm not impressed. At all.
text: These were given to me as a gift brand new but I expected better from Apple (I know, wishful thinking). This headset costs $250 and at the most basic of functions, the case cannot charge wirelessly.  You look at any of these cheapy $50 pair of headphones low and behold, they have that functionality.  Secondly, I've only had them for about a month and a half with VERY LIGHT usage and already I have the hissing/buzzing noise in one of them to which that bud stopped working. This is a known issue. Lastly (because honestly, I could go on and on), these reeeeeealy don't sound that great in comparison to lower priced options. A premium feel with budget build. It's almost like putting a ford pinto engine in a corvette shell.

title: Poor functionality
text: No off button. They will stay on and die if you don't put them in the case. Even then sometimes they won't turn off. Sometimes only one dies early. Poor instructions for using. For the most popular headphones in the world they sure didn't put much time into ease of use and instruction, lamest thing I've ever seen. Try the generic ones for 40 dollars. Same sound quality anyway.

title: Problems - even with my second pair.  Don't bother
text:

title: Meh
text:

These need to be in the case to create a bluetooth connection. The case has rounded sides to it and the button you need to hold down for 5 seconds to connect is out near the edge. Additionally the curved additions to the body of the ear buds are right over the button. The buds don't connect via Bluetooth reliably so you end up pushing the the button while the case is rocking around with the buds in your way the whole time...total cluster. I returned these because eventually they wouldn't connect at all and replaced them with a pare of Bose buds that connect the second you take them out if the case and put them in your ears. Way better.

If you are into frustration and time wasting get these.

title: What AirPods should be...
text:
Wouldn't you know it, I bought these for me and the wife a month before Apple came out with the AirPods Pro with noise canceling. Noise canceling is one of those things you think you don't need until you try it. The only people who don't need noise cancelation are people who listen to music in a totally quiet environment.

But these don't claim to be noise canceling, so they absolutely do what they are supposed to do, and do it beautifully. I love these earphones, and you will too. Until you try ones with noise cancelation....D'OH!

text:

They kind of look like hearing aids as well, so that’s a negative for me.
Overall, I wouldn’t pay $200 for them again.. maybe $120.

title: Love!!

Overpriced for the short life span these will give you. Buy some Raycons instead.

text: I had a black Powerbeats pro myself, I bought a white one for my wife. She is using it all day every day now. She keeps her phone on main floor and can go upstairs of downstairs, her reception is not interrupted. Quality of sound is great, reception is over 30 feet, you get what you pay for.

title: Prowerbeats Pro
text: I guess there is always going to be some negative reviews? But! I very happy with the Powerbeats Pro! Yes they are a little expensive, and some think they are too big! But they are what I need! Don’t fall out! Fit comfortably! Look great!! I let you know how they last! They sound just as good as any Bluetooth I’ve own! Easy to operate! Check YouTube for tips operating them!

title: Lasted less than 2 years - Maybe expecting too much!
text: Bought powerbeat pro earbuds in May 2020. Maybe I am expecting too much for the $199 earbuds, but one of them now only lasts about 1 -2 hours and the other 5 hours max. It takes longer than 5 minutes to recharge, in fact considerably longer! Usually, they are pretty comfortable and I can forget I have the hook wrapped around my ear, but some days it gets to be too much. I did buy the extended warranty, but unfortunately I apparently destroyed the box with the serial number for the item and Asurion nor Amazon is able to help with replacement. A refurbished pair only have a 90-day warranty. I am not going back with this brand.

title: These have good sound, but the devil is in pairing and constant one ear only working
text: Product has good battery life and sound.  However it’s all ruined by the constant pairing trying to get one or the other to work and then making sure it’s charging in the case which often leaves one dead if you don’t check.  I find myself dealing with two issues daily.  Ruins the entire experience.  Had to issue a one star because these shouldn’t happen at this price point

title: Love them
text: I really like the Powerbeats Pro.  I had the Jabra Elite Active 65t earbuds but they always fell out when I was working out.  They are great if not in a sweaty environment.  The Powerbeats Pro are perfect for the gym.  No matter what I am doing, they do not fall out.  The sound quality is great.  Highly recommended.

title: Failed after six months
text: Odd problem, but the right earbud just no longer wants to charge. Otherwise, great product, but having it fail in six months just isn't acceptable.

title: Great earphones!
text: I’m so happy I sprung and bought these. They connect as soon as I open the case so by the time I get my shoes on at the gym, I’m already jamming out. I don’t hear any background noise at the gym. - only my music. Big thumbs up!

title: Worked for two months
text: Junk ! Defective! Something - worked for two months. Too much treble

title: Great product!
text: Best wireless I've used. Kind of weird that the box is needed to paid the buds, but oh well.

title: Amazing
text: The best Beats I have ever purchased. It’s going to be difficult for the company to top this. Everything from the sound to the fit is amazing.

title: Didn’t work, sent for repairs, doesn’t work again
text: Been for repairs. Not working again now. First time wouldn’t ‘talk’ to devices. Then only 1 would work. Now other earpiece won’t charge. Apple store sent for repairs, takes two weeks, then two weeks later doesn’t work again. Not a good experience, especially given the price. Husband has a pair too and those have issues too. Two pair, two duds. 0 for 2.

title: Sound quality
text: The sound and noise quality is excellent.  Love it!

title: Uncomfortable and surprisingly low volume
text:
Plastic puts pressure on the rear of your inner ear so that it becomes uncomfortable. You have to turn them 3/4 to full volume to hear much of anything.
Surprising miss on Beats' part. Maybe V2 will be better.

title: Junk
text: These are terrible. You have to forget device and reconnect every use or it will only play out of one bud and it’s a act of congress to get fixed. Less than a 6 months old.

title: Very worthy!
text:
Love working out in these! Don’t fall out. Great sound, not true noise cancellation but not concerned about that as I like to have some awareness around me.
Just wish the case was a bit more compact although not sure if that’s possible considering the size of the ear buds.

title: Worth every penny
text: I tried to be cheap and avoid these as workout buds, but in the end you get what you pay for. I knew I would love these for the sole purpose of the "hooks" that go around your ear. Making these the best workout earbuds. Completely wireless and sweat resistant. The battery is a beast, especially when you factor in the huge case that brings another 20 hours of charge to the buds. The sound quality is excellent and the noise cancelation is driven by a tight seal in the ear canal. I bought these "like new" for a $40 discount. They came packaged as normal without the seal. This is the best route in my opinion!

title: Complete garbage
text:
This is a classic case of fool me once, shame on you...

I own a Garmin Forerunner 25. It works, but it's flaky, so I decided to get a new watch. Plus I needed a watch that I could use for interval training. I read several good reviews of this watch, it had the features I needed for a good price, so I decided to give Garmin another shot.

Apparently the reviewers got the watches that actually work.

I plugged in the watch into a wall charger to charge it as soon as I got it. 18 hours later, nada. The watch wasn't charging and it wouldn't power on. Someone on a Garmin forum suggested plugging it into a laptop to get it going. This worked to get the watch to turn on and charge, but neither the Mac OS nor the Garmin Express software could find the watch. This meant I couldn't sync the watch or install the update.

I couldn't tell you if it works well on runs since I couldn't get past the basics of charging/syncing/updating.

I'm returning

title: Hurry Up and WAIT......
text: I have had several versions of Forerunner and GPS connectivity has been immediate, efficient, and dependable. Poor connectivity is specific to this 35 version / device. I am unable to locate GPS until 10-15 minutes [if at all] after I start my walk or run, irrelevant to the area, whether in the mountains or downtown. Very disappointed with this version and do not even bother wearing it due to the frustration of it not connecting. Get used to seeing the message WAIT as you attempt to locate GPS. I truly wish I would have listened to the other reviewers that posted this similar issue so I could have saved $100's. I have reverted back to my older Forerunner 410 version which connects lickety-split.

title: Nice watch, I was replacing a Vivoactive
text: Nice watch, I was replacing a Vivoactive. I returned and purchased the new Vivoactive THe Forerunner 35 is nce but the screen looks cheap compared to the Vivo ActiveHR.

title: My favorite
text: When I ordered this I sure didn’t know I would be wearing this every single day and to sleep!  I absolutely love it!  It tracks my activity and reminds me to move.  It tracks my sleep and makes me want to go to bed earlier so I get my hours in! It works great to track my runs, gives me splits on my miles, tracks elevation and best times!  Great running watch and activity tracking!  I love being able to monitor my heart rate at any time.  It will even track menstrual cycles if you wish.

title: Frustrating device
text: This is not high on UX. I had to look up the manual on their website and even that didn't help a lot. I've kinda stumbled my way into figuring it out but not totally. All I want to know is my heart rate (zones) while running. I don't wear this all day, just when I run. I bought a chest strap monitor as well to pair for greater accuracy. The watch recognises the chest monitor but then when I click on Outdoor Exercise, it says "Wait" and then stays on that for 25 minutes (I've lost any interest in the watch at that point). I can't get the heart rate screen to stay on. So it's more of a distraction than asset to my workout. I should just return the darn thing since I can't use it.

title: Stops pairing after a few months, severely limiting what data you can access.
text:
Worked great at first. The pairing became balkier and balkier. The support forum and other data on Garmin website not helpful. It just says pair devices and follow on screen prompts. N]but no matter what, I CANNOT get the watch to sync w any of my devices. I’ve reset blue tooth, updated all software. Had the, cheek by jowl for proximity. No dice. And no help from Garmin. So.... $200 forma watch that worked as advertised for 6 months...and then Deteriorating to a watch that cannot provide any but most rudimentary data...of rmust the last 2-3mdays. Really disappointing.
Update. Farming reports syncing issue w my version of iOS. It recommends deleting and re-installing app. So I delete. I go to App Store to reinstall: and the only app available is rated one star, is cluttered w loud annoying mandatory ads and.... still WON’T SYNC to my device. And now I’ve lost all my old data stored in the old app. Aaarrrgh. Way to go Garmin. Please post recommendations in comments for al

title: Great watch for "casual" exerciser
text:
I bought the Garmin 35 about 1.5 months ago to replace a Fitbit Charge 3. I bought it because it was the cheapest option I found that would give wrist based heart rate and also pair with a chest strap for a more accurate heart rate.

At this point in my exercise life, the Garmin is better for me than the Fitbit, although I think if you are just getting into exercise the Fitbit gives better feedback and goals.

When biking or doing cardio I pair the Garmin with a Garmin HRM chest strap. The Garmin with the HRM strap has replaced my Fitbit, my bicycle computer and a Polar chest strap watch which is a big win.

Screen:
The screen is a nice size and is visible in bright sunlight while wearing polarized sunglasses. This is a critical factor for me and a big improvement over the Fitbit. I wish the default display was customizable and I touch on that more below. It would be nice if the watch had a touch screen, but the buttons on the side work fine.<

title: So far working out great.
text: Bought this to replace a different brand device that was not tracking my jogging in the swimming pool very well. this is doing a good job of that. It also tracks my sleep so I can see how well I am getting quality sleep and what affects me getting a good nights rest (too much wine in the evening!! lol)

title: Accurate, comfortable, works as designed.
text: I've been using this product for four months and have completed a half marathon and full marathon using it.  The battery life is good, the display is easy to read while running, the settings are easy to change and navigate, and I haven't had any issues with the Garmin watch.  This is my first GPS watch and I just started running and I'm very happy with my choice. After my runs I sync it up with the MapMyRun app to import the run and see the breakdown which I recommend.  The watch runs with many apps so whatever one you want to use is up to you.

title: to easy to use watch
text: If you are a daily runner looking for a simple, to easy to use watch, this is a great choice!  It tracks everything you could possibly imagine for your daily runs, in addition to calories, steps, and other health metrics.  There is no need to buy a crazy fitness watch when this reasonably priced does more than what most people would need.  It is easy to use, and combines the overall health metrics of the Fitbit with more advanced running features.  Highly recommended!

title: Fits my wrist
text:
I've only had it a few days, so I haven't worked with all the features yet.
This watch has great display and it doesn't look HUGE on my wrist like some others I've tried. I love that it vibrates when I hit a mile marker.

My one complaint is that I would like it to be able to display the time and my mileage at the same time. That way I can cut my workout short if I need to so I can get ready for work on time.

I look forward to using it for intervals!

title: Great watch
text: Great first watch for trail running. I have gone up to 20 miles. This watch is perfect. Not expensive and collects great data. Love this watch. Why pay more for fancy things you don't really need? I don't listen to music either on the tails.

title: Easy to use at reasonable cost
text: My husband uses this watch for long distance running.  He finds it easy to use without unneeded gadgets.  This watch is also water resistant.  The Cost was reasonable.

title: Best Fitness Watch ever owned
text: An awesome watch.  Its simple to use, big bright watch face.  The running features are excellent.  Just straight forward data with no complicated scrolling.  Is very accurate.  The garmin app is simple but w/ great features, tracking and gives you map of your run.  I bought 2 fitness watches and returned them both.  Love this one.  Thanks Garmin...

title: GOOD WATCH
text: This is my third smart watch. The first was a Garmin Vivosmart HR+, which I had for four years, was an OK watch. The second was a less expensive watch with decent reviews on Amazon. I returned that watch after a month of trying to get it to work properly. The app for the watch was poor and after reinstalling the app a few times and completely resetting the watch, I decided enough and returned. This new Garmin WORKS right out of the box. The application works well and the watch took a couple of days to get used to, but I am very pleased with the overall experience. I ride 50+ miles a week in 5 plus events.  My only concern is the accuracy of the milage. I have used Map my Ride in the past and when I have compared the two after the exact same ride, Garmin app shows I have ridden almost 10% farther, plus burned 50% fewer calories? I know the calorie burn is an estimate, so I'm not overly concerned, and I try and ride an additional mile to make up for the difference.

title: Great present for a runner.
text: I gave this watch to my son. He uses it to train for upcoming marathons. He told me it works very well.

title: a good choice
text: way better than fitbit in almost every aspect except for the sleep monitor, which doesn't really work.

title: Excellent activity watch
text: I bought this for my wife since she liked my old Garmin 210 so much.  She really loves it - has all the features that you'd expect from Garmin, with improved satellite acquisition (which is always a pain for my old Garmin) activity tracking and smartphone connectivity.  The heart rate monitor is amazing and makes me a little jealous as I still have to use a chest strap.  Light weight - much lighter than I thought it would be and the battery seems to last quite long even for all the bells and whistles.  I'll probably be upgrading to a new one at some time, I've had mine for 4 years now and it's still going, if this lasts half as long it's more than worth it.  Garmin has never let me down yet.

title: Gps/watch band holder
text: I’ve had this for about two years now and the only complaints I really have are that sometimes it takes a while to connect to the gps and that I’ve needed to replace the watch band holder twice because they ripped. When I’m going for a walk/run sometimes it takes gps about 5 minutes to connect. Also, sometimes it says my phone isn’t connected so the weather doesn’t show, even though it’s connected to the watch via Bluetooth.  Besides that, it is a great product!

title: Good tracker, but has proprietary straps, can't install apps
text:
Has a much better screen (more visible) than my old Vivoactive HR, but it's black and white and you can't install apps on it.

I don't mind the lack of color - it's perfectly usable without it, especially since the Vivoactive HR's screen is really difficult to read when out in the sun (this one is visible) but not being able to install 3rd party apps isn't great.
Also the straps are proprietary and there's no stainless steel milanese loop style straps, so on that part, I'm not all that happy.

Mine is still a few weeks old so the battery holds a charge longer than a week.

title: Perfect for Running
text: It may be a simple thing, but my favorite part is the longer wrist band; my hubby has a large wrist and most bands don't fit.  I love this watch, its cool to see his sleep pattern too.

title: A Great Aid For Encouraging My Running/walking and Further Healthy Lifestyle
text: Oh My God has this been a big help.  It took me a while to be able to figure everything out as I am in my 70's and not that computer literate.  But, with some work and reading the manual more carefully---have figured out what I needed.  I can now track my runs and put in what I want for running and walking time.  It gives you alerts like telling you to move when you've sat too long.  It counts steps, miles, how long you are into deep sleep (if you wear it night and day which I do), and then I can download the info for the day onto my computer and see my progress over the months.  Just love it and it has motivated me to go further.  It is comfortable on my wrist and even though every Garmin is different, I got some advice too from fellow runners.  Absolutely love it.

title: Great!
text: Great watch! My daughter has one, she’s a runner so tracks all her data. Too complicated and advanced for what I wanted so it was returned. Nothing wrong with the watch at all.

title: Hits the spot
text: All I wanted was something to replace a basic pedometer plus having to wear a heart rate belt/watch to track my heart rate, all in one little gadget. The forerunner 35 does this. I don't care about the sleep tracking or anything fancy, just something to accurately track my walking distance, and to show me my heart rate at the same time. I got this "used", "very good", so my cost was extremely reasonable. Once I figured out how to wade through the buttons on the watch and once this hooked up to a satellite, I was good to go. That is all I wanted! I am not interested in some gadget telling me how many calories I eat, or what my sleep patterns are! People are getting so dependent on gadgets to run their lives and the fitness tracker companies are more than eager to control their lives. If you just want a basic fitness tracker without all those life-controllng other features, this one is just fine, and accurate too.

title: Great for Tracking Walking Exercise
text:
Both as gift for my wife who is not a watch person, nor, at 72, a working out person. However, she does like to keep in shape by walking - up to 11 miles a day some days. The Forerunner 35 has been encouraging gift that allows her to challenge herself and feel accomplishment with her efforts. Features used are main screen for time/day/date and battery level, and steps and miles... sometimes heart rate and calories burned. Without the GPS, the battery lasts at least a week before charging. Quite satisfied with even such limited use, and likely will explore more features in the future.
This unit was a replacement - first unit backlight did not work and display would "break up". Amazon return and replacment was easy and quick.

title: Garmin Forerunner 35
text: I bought this for my wife for Christmas to replace a TomTom that had developed problems.  She is just learning how to use it, but so far really likes it.  The Bluetooth connection to the cellphone is super!!  Her only complaint is that the data it supplies is overwhelming!!  Now the next test will be to see how it holds up.  If it lasts more than two years it will beat the TomTom!!

title: Not accurate for measuring heart rate.
text: The heart rate function is 100% inaccurate and inconvenient to use, you have to press the button every few seconds to get a reading, I would not recommend for heart monitoring.

title: Good GPS and decent heart rate (not as good as ...
text:
Good GPS and decent heart rate (not as good as chest strap, but good enough for monitoring during training). I use this for dirt riding motorcycles. Connects to my phone with no issues. I can export the track and HRM data into VIRB Edit software to add those as overlays to video taken on any camera. Battery life is great. HATE the charger because it uses a special connector.

You can see in the first photo that the distance is longer and heart rate lower than the second. Data for the second was from my phone GPS and a chest HRM, both of which are more accurate. But the watch is a good backup and close enough for my MX training.

title: easy to use
text: This has lots of features and yet is easy to use. Love it.

title: A lot for your money
text: Got it for my mom for her to work out and she loves the extra perks it has on it. Worth every penny.

title: Great fitness tracker
text:
I did a good amount of research on other devices before buying this product. I initially wanted a sleek smartwatch that also had great fitness tracking modes, but apparently you either get a pretty smartwatch/OK fitness tracker, or a decent fitness tracker/outdated looking smartwatch. In the end I decided I wanted mainly a fitness tracker that has some smartwatch features, and that I wouldn't use most of the features on an actual smartwatch.

PROS

The app is awesome! My husband is a software developer so I learn a lot about what makes good apps good and what makes bad apps bad. This app is free and user friendly. It has mostly accurate measurements and you can easily track your progress and areas of improvement. It also has a feature that allows whoever you send the link to to track your location, in case you are concerned about getting kidnapped on your run. Very good stuff.

The steps I get seem to be pretty consistent as well as the distance traveled.

title: Wow oh wow! Phone quality is great too!
text:
[[VIDEOID:b7b8180a2e6b2912cb47489991fd619c]] Running: it was really incredible to listen to an audiobook AND hear the birds and the cars around me. Before using an open ear headphone - I had to choose between entertainment or situational awareness.

Swimming: the aeropex headphones worked great when I was treading water. The sound quality was superb and consistent. Swimming laps on the other hand - it tended to cut out. I’m curious if it was a Bluetooth connection issue OR if it was when the charging port got wet.

Phone quality: the phone quality was fantastic! I didn’t have to talk as loud - as compare to when using Apple earbuds or my AirPods.

The aeropex headphones stayed in place really well for running, swimming, and even dancing around the house. The deciding factor for me between these and the XTrainers was 1) the price difference 2) I have the option to download music to my fitness watch (which I’ve never loaded music onto until now - for this).

title: $20 sound quality
text:
I bought these Aftershokz in search of a great sound quality sport earphone that doesn’t fall out of my ears, willing to pay good money for the set that satisfies all needs and durable. I have usually done well with the jammer style but some of those have been falling out of my ears. This is my first time with Sftershokz or Bone resonance technology.
The physical design of these are top notch feels like nothing at all, unless you’re going to lay your head back on something. And then I have to acknowledge that it is state of the art technology, the bone resonance, 2000 Tom Clancy stuff.
Like other reviews, the lack of bass is a draw back... I don’t think I hear ANY base. I can feel some lower frequencies vibrating on the contact point and that’s it. I tried the ear plugs and that didn’t help. If i put my fingers all the way in my ear canals I can hear the bass and then there’s other frequencies that sound muffled. I would say the sound totally lacks good frequency range, vibra

title: Exactly like the aeropex, but for smaller heads.
text:
This is a smaller version of the aeropex, which is an excellent headset for every day use.

If you have a smaller head, (distance around the back of your head is 9 1/2" or less) then these would be a good option to try.

They obviously hug tighter than the original aeropex, but this minimizes interactions between the headband and things like collared shirts, or anything that you might lay your head against.

I absolutely recommend the aeropex in general, especially for listening to spoken word content, like podcasts while going about daily life. They're also great for teleconferencing and phone calls.

They don't sound the absolute best compared to conventional headphones, but what you give up in sound quality is more than made up for in usability and convenience.

The headlining feature for bone conduction is the ability to listen to music as well as your environment at the same time, but they also work with earplugs (which are include

title: I can't say enough Good about these!
text: *Edit* I love these things more and more each day. I utterly forget I'm wearing them and I frequently don't charge them at night. Despite this I've noticed I can get at least a full 2 days worth of battery out of these. Let me be clear. I put them on at 9am in the morning before I shower and I don't take them off till about 1am at night. During that 16 hours they are connected in multipoint paring mode to my cell phone and my Nintendo Switch at all times. Of that 16 hours they are on I listen to music about 3 hours of the day and watch probably an hours worth of videos on my phone. I use them for notifications and phone calls mostly allowing me to be hands free and without bothering those around me. I answer probably 5 calls a day and talk on the phone about an hour a day and then in the evenings I listen to audio books for about 5 hours.  So they are on for 16 hours connected to Bluetooth per day every day with about 10 hours of use and about 6 hours of using them solely for notificat

title: Great for background noise while working, but don't expect much more
text:
I've been using these for about 160 hours per month since I got them a few months ago. Initially I was very impressed by the novelty of bone-conduction audio, but now that I'm used to them I can provide an honest review.

I bought these using a company stipend. The reason I chose these over conventional headphones or earbuds was because I have to be able to hear my surroundings at work. I'd like to keep this review concise for your sanity and mine, so here are some things I like and don't like about these headphones.

The good:
• Reasonably lightweight
• Astounding battery life. These last me an entire 12 hour shift without a charge, after months of use
• Acceptable microphone quality for meetings/phone calls
• Good voice clarity in calls or listening to podcasts
• Fairly simple, easy to find controls
• Low profile. You'll sometimes forget you're wearing them and they don't stick out much (I can wear a half face respirator + safety gogg

title: Great! For What They Are
text: These are not the highest quality audio you will ever hear, but they are not engineered for the audiophile. What they do do they do well.  First, sound quality is good, especially for something not in or over your ears — its actually pretty cool when you think about it. They do leave your ears open but the sound does tend to drown out the other sounds, but you have the volume control so adjust accordingly — on high you will hear very little else, on low you can listen and have a conversation.  They do vibrate on your ears with high bass levels, but nothing you won’t get used to if you have mastery of your id. What these do really really well is allow you to comfortably listen for long periods of time without headphone fatigue; I wear them for hours with no discomfort, including when not using them, and while wearing glasses — they are, bar none, the most comfortable headphones I’ve every used. I mainly bought them to listen to audiobooks but find myself using them to enjoy sound when o

title: LOUD BEEPS!!!!
text:
I'm taking off a couple stars because I'll never be able to use the on board buttons for skip and volume control (without removing the speakers from close to my ears first).

They put in STUPID G*D D*MN LOUD beeps on every function, when they could have had no beeps at all. Why do you need to hear a beep at 100% volume to confirm that you've hit the volume down button?

The beeps cause me physical pain, and create sensitivity which lasts for minutes. These headphones already sound very crisp and sharp, and the aftereffects of the beeps make me too sensitive to the sharp sound quality of my media.

The sad thing is that this hardware has no capacity to connect to a computer for data, so there will be no chance to fix this error with a firmware update. This is really unforgivably stupid. I don't know how this made it through the development and testing process without being caught.

title: Novelty headphones
text: These headphones do not have noise cancellation. They come with ear plugs... The sound quality is that of talking with your friend through tin cans and a string. Would not recommend for any activity.

title: Promising but disapponting
text:
Update #2: I got an RMA and exchanged the pair that didn’t work with some new ones….and the same thing happened to the new ones after a couple of weeks. Buy something else if you’re looking for reliability.

Update: Battery completely died after two months of use. The LED won't turn on and I can't even charge them anymore.

I really love these. I've always wanted something that I can wear at work and still be able to hear the cars driving around me and in case someone is trying to talk to me. AirPods have a transparency feature but it just doesn't work well enough for me so these headphones are almost perfect for me. The build quality feels great, they charge fast and easy and the sound quality is very clear for bone conduction (if you don't turn the volume up too high). The only reason I'm taking off two stars is because I keep accidentally calling people because of this stupid auto redial feature they baked into the headphones. I really wish I could turn off this

title: Loses connection constantly and shuts off for no reason
text: My headphones keep randomly disconnecting and shutting off when I still have 60% + battery life. It has happened multiple times a day for the past week or so that I have owned them while listening to audio books or on phone calls. My phone (brand new pixel 5) is in my pocket or within the same room and should not be having connection issues. This is very disheartening, as I had my previous pair of aftershocks for over 4 years and loved them. I plugged them in last night and this morning I can't even get them to turn on. I will sadly be making arrangements to return. The headphones themselves, when they are working, are most excellent and I love the smaller design vs. the old.

title: Great, except for one unforgivable flaw.
text:
Both of the Trekz I got from. Aftershokz have been great I use them so much I need two pairs to recharge one while I use the other. They last me over 8 hours but I use them constantly. One finally had too much abuse and I so I replaced it with this model.

It's really good. An improvement all around, and I don't even really mind the specialized charger due to the water resistant design.

But the beep from on/off or volume adjustments.

The beep. The BEEEP!!!!

It is painfully loud with no remedy on my Samsung Note. I often have to remove it to use any of the buttons. This is a very, very serious and stupidly easy flaw to avoid for an otherwise perfect headset.

Disable the beep. Entirely. I don't need any beep to let me know I pressed a button Turn it off.

Disable. The. Beep.

Update Nov 13, 2021:

I bumped this up to 4 stars due to the way a replacement was handled. The battery stopped accepting a charge

title: So much better than the Air!
text: I keep needing to feel above my ears to check they're still on! They're so light! Keeps my sanity since I can listen to music when my new born twins are sleeping!

title: Love the concept, but this model is defective and should have been recalled
text:
I love the Aftershokz series. After my Aftershokz Airs were damaged by rain, I bought the Aeropek model. Love them too. Lighter, microphone works fine, but they sound clearer to me.

Update: while I love the concept of these, they are not made well and begin to fail after several short months. The internal mechanism on both sides started to rattle and then the battery charge wouldnt last more then 2 minutes before the battery warning came on. It’s a shame because I’m a aftershokz lover and had their previous series the Titanium model, which I loved.
After reading other horror stories I see that this model is plagued with a defect.
I guess it’s time to look for another brand.

title: Looks nice there it ends.
text: Look nice and feels nice when wearing. There the good ends. Cannot turn volume up past 75% then overwhelms the speakers. Not real bone conduction. My first pair lasted about 1 month. Would not charge after that. Now 1 month in on my 2nd pair and per on switch is bad. Amazon will not w as warranty. Hmmmm

title: Won't charge after less than 10 months
text: One star since it will not charge after less than a year???

title: Perfect if you’ve ever had trouble with traditional in-ear headphones!

title: Love these
text:

The noise canceling is strange.. because it does and it doesn’t at the same time. I do have to stop them to hear clearly what someone is saying to me if I’m listening to something. title: Really impressed
text:
I've been running with the older Titanium model for years now without any real drama. Didn't see any real reason to pay for the Air model but now we're two generations ahead and I'm training for a marathon and this seemed like a good time to upgrade.

I have to say the incremental changes added up. They're noticeably lighter, the audio is definitely improved. In theory they're more waterproof, and that makes sense with the new charger, but I run pretty regularly in storms with the old ones with no problems so I'm not sure what prompted that.

Too early to say how these will hold up - my titaniums did break at once point but they replaced them immediately so I'm not super concerned, but they're so thin hopefully they've thought all that through.

The sound will also depend on the volume you set them at, for those of you familiar with the Fletcher-Munson Curve. The midrange is STUNNING. Perhaps as clear as it gets. The detail is far beyond my expectations. For you base heads out there, you will be disappointed. Compared to a very good pair of headphones, it lacks dearly. title: Not as good as my Airpods Pro, but MUCH more budget friendly for a backup pair.
text:
The charge on my AirPods Pro Buds doesn't last the entirety of my long-haul flights or overnight if I wear them to sleep. Buying a 2nd set of AirPods to always have a charged pair on hand seemed excessively expensive and indulgent. These Beats Studio Buds do the trick for less than 1/3 the price of my AirPods. For my purposes, the Beats Studio Buds work out great.
The sound quality and noise-cancelation features of the Beats Studio Buds are just a step down from my noise-canceling AirPods Pro Buds. With the Beats Studio Buds, I hear a little more of what's happening around me which is disappointing, hence the 3 stars. However, I wouldn't give the AirPods Pro noise canceling feature more than 4 stars. Neither brand of Buds holds a candle to my Beats over-the-ear noise-canceling headphones. It has no stem but the bud itself sticks out your ears a little more, so they are uncomfortable to lay on if you were to watch a movie in bed or so.
They look like bolts in your ears from afar. With that being said, they are comfortable for regular use, at least for me. Although the silicone tips are less pliant and soft compared to the Pro’s. Sound quality is where it shines, with a nice resonating low and crisp highs. Mids is where these fall short. Positive: Strong base sound, very good noise cancellation, OK sound overall. They pair OK with iPhone after initial setup.

Negative: Much weaker Bluetooth range compared to Airpods, poor positioning of controls results in stopping play whenever you push earbuds into ear; don't work well with Apple watch (i.e. whenever you use controls on earbud, they disconnect from Apple watch control). They are also a bit bulkier but that may not be a big issue.

Summary: If you are going to lay out the bucks for good earbuds anyway, spend a few extra and get the Apple product. Just DON'T drop an Airpod into sink water :(

This watch shines when it comes to fitness and health-related features with the inclusion of heart rate, SpO2, sleep, breathing tracking and much more. It comes with a host of workout modes, including running, swimming, cycling, elliptical training and walking.

title: Cost-Effective and Reliable Alternative to Apple Watch
text:
We were looking to get our son a watch that would work along with the iPhone we got him last summer (which he has taken good care of), but I just wasn't ready to invest in yet another expensive gadget for him given how active he is. After some research and looking at a few recommendations, this AmazFit Bip U Pro SmartWatch seemed to fit the bill.
Our son is a very active 6th grader and trust me when I say that he has put this watch through its trials. From rec football practice, to swim team, rough-housing with the neighborhood kids, I was almost certain this watch would give up. Boy was I wrong. This thing is running like a champ.
The Bip U Pro SmartWatch has a lot of the same functions as the Apple Watch, and best of all, it syncs with my son's iPhone SE and alerts him to text messages, emails, among other things. It also helped him find his phone here in the house when little sister hid it under the couch. :)
I would definitely recommend this for any parent getting as

title: If you want/need O2 measurements - don't buy
text:
My Cardio Doctors want me top watch my O2 and Heart Rate at 3 hour intervals as a part of a Senior Cardio Study at Duke. I had seen Smart Watch claims of Heart Rate and O2 Monitor Capability. This is the 2nd Smart Watch I've tried. If O2 monitoring with a Smart Watch is your goal, don't' bother.

Heart rate was occasionally he same, but occasionally 4 or more off, as both of my Finger Oximeters and my Bluetooth Blood Pressure/Heart Rate Monitor, but O2 on the "Bip U Pro" was a waste of time. It frequently read 99% and other times was in the 80's.

If O2 monitoring is among your goals, don't bother with a smart watch. Duke Cardio folks echo the same opinion re O2 monitoring with ANY Smart Watch.

title: A Good Intro Fitness Tracker (B rating) *Not a Smart Watch*
text:
I have been using this fitness tracker watch for about a month now and I give it a B rating for quality and function. It gets about 7 days of use before I need to charge it and easily synch with my iPhone for recording data/updates. The silicone band is comfortable and doesn't cause me irritation or noticeable as I wear it all day. The waterproof is good for showering, swimming, and I have free dove down to about 20 ft with no problems or issues. I am 6'2" and about 220 lbs and it fits my wrist just fine,

Issues:
- The watch face scratches very easily (get a screen protector)
- If your arm isn't moving it won't count steps. (Hands in pockets or pushing a shopping cart equals no steps)
-I have been standing at my desk for a half-hour, walking around the office, and the watch will say I need to stand and move around.
- Display is hard to read with some of the face options. Whish I could customize the things to show and were on the screen.

title: does what it's supposed to
text:
*edit: I've found a con and it bugged me enough to update my review. If your phone is on "Do not disturb" and you leave your phone on at night for emergency calls, the watch still vibrates with every text, regardless of whether they're on your emergency list. This is annoying when you're a light sleeper and you get a text for go cart racing or Dairy Queen at 4 am. I'm assuming it would still go off with a call as well.

I have a Garmin Vivofit that for some reason the notification vibrations will work for a couple months, then stop working for a while, then randomly start working again. I was sick of this, and went looking for something not too expensive and found this. I'm still getting used to it, but it vibrates when I get a call or text, so I'm happy so far. The band is very flexible and comfortable, which is nice, since my Vivofit band was a little stiff.

title: Not a good choice for a fitness watch
text: Calorie consumption, PAI score, etc are all based off of pulse rate. This device constantly looses the heart rate, mainly when exercising. I’ve tried adjusting the band fit to no prevail. Even when doing a manual heart rate, I often get no results. If you want a waterproof watch that has a customizable screen as well as long battery life and fast charging, this may be for you. If your looking for a true fitness watch, I suggest to look elsewhere.

title: Fine watch, but zero customer service
text: The watch is great, when it works, which is 99% of the time. However, if you ever have problems with the watch, don't expect any help from the manufacturer. There is a customer support form on Amazfit's website, but it seems to be just for show because they never respond.

title: Cheap feeling
text: I couldn't get past how cheap the watch felt. Pretty color and navigation was super easy. My daughter liked it. I've just been comparing watches to the Fitbit Sense which feels high end. This just didn't.

title: Good quality for the money
text:
I've had this watch for about two weeks now and like it. The strap is comfortable and there are more than a dozen watch faces to choose. It took a couple of days to get used to how to operate the watch (finding the workouts and finding where to change the brightness) but now it is second nature.

The step count seems to be accurate as is the heart rate. I haven't used the sleep function so I cannot comment on that.

The best thing is that it has a strap with a buckle rather than the type of strap with the post that snaps into a hole and that always seems to come loose.

All in all, a good quality watch of this type.

It accurately tells time, my heart rate, steps and I love this watch. It’s great and is so light on my wrist!!

The sleep monitor is often wrong about awake times so I don’t believe the other sleep data either. The only thing I can believe with this watch is the time of day.

title: Features - 10; Accuracy - 7
text:
This is my second Amazfit watch and quite an upgrade over the previous one. The expanded list of sports for the Workout History is kind of fun. It even has curling, which is my main winter athletic activity. PAI was a new concept to me. Surprisingly it's the one that motivates me the most. One thing I noticed however is that the step count is about 7% below my actual step range. Also, sometimes if you don't actively say you have a sporting activity going you can still lose PAI.

The major downside of this watch compared to my old one is that all these fancy features have resulted in a relatively short battery life. I still recommend the watch as a good value for the price but know what the shortcomings are.

title: Smart Fitbit

It does alert you of calls and messages, but you cannot accept, decline, or answer calls or messages from the watch. title: Phone Calls Only Play Out of the Right Ear Piece
text:
I ordered a pair of the Bose SoundSport Free after having a pair of the SoundSport Pulse headphones which have a connecting wire between the two earphones. The Pulse headphones play both music and phones calls in stereophonic sound, meaning you hear the output in both earphones.

The new SoundSport Free headphones play music out of both earpieces but phone calls come out of only the right side. Completely unacceptable in my opinion for a product costing $250. Appple's Airpod product - costing a $100 less - plays phone calls out of both earpieces.

Fingers crossed that Bose addresses this issue with some form of firmware update.

I tried to lower the volume and realized the volume rocker switch has no haptic feedback, so I had no idea if I was pushing it or not, I pressed harder than the volume dropped. Hmm, not good by maybe I can get used to that. However, I bought these to watch videos on a plane, so I brought up a movie on Netflix and that’s when the real problem started. title: Bose Soundsport Free
text: Great sound, love not being tangled in wires. Not crazy about master/slave connectivity because the sound can momentarily drop out until the master reconnects and then sound feels like a wave moving through your head. Not a major deal for me but it might bug some people. They fit well in my ears and don't fall out. I use them everyday to bike to and from work, while walking my dog.

title: Too expensive for the limited functionality; just get AirPods
text: For a $200 product, these are a major letdown. The fact that all calls, GoToMeeting-style apps etc only channel audio through the right ear is a major limitation and not excusable. It’s not that it’s “terrible” like others complain about; it’s just annoying enough to say “WTF were their engineers thinking when they accepted this major functionality compromise”. Sound quality is definitely worthy of the Bose name, but they aren’t loud/isolating enough to actually drown out noises like airplanes, the train (Chicago CTA drowns our their max volume output easily), street noises etc. I bought these instead of the Jabra Sport Elite (upgraded battery) and the AirPods, and within 30 minutes of use I regretted my purchase. If you want a dedicated sport bud, get the Jabras. If you want an all around bud, get the AirPods. The SoundSport Frees are squarely in the middle of doing nothing particularly well, and their limited functionality is NOT made up for by their sound quality. HARD PASS.

title: Great headphones for someone on the go
text: These headphones stay in your ears comfortably. You can run with them, work out, walk to work, etc. They have easy functions for calls and changing music. My only "complaint" would be that I wish the battery life lasted longer for those days you are on the phone a lot. You can still get in a long conversation and then put them back in the case to recharge. Great purchase. Love this limited edition color!

title: Awesome Bang for Buck
text:
5 stars because of the price. I am quite amazed at these headphones for the price. Like wine, I rate headphones based on their price point and these are, for sure, the best I have tried in this price range.

Pros
- noise canceling. These are not active noise canceling headphones, but they do a great job in isolating outside noise.
- sound quality. I am shocked at how good these sound with music. I also listen to some talk radio and while not as good, they are still pretty great.
- comfortable ear pads
- quickly and easily connected to multiple devices
- long battery life

Cons
- I have a huge head. I buy fitted hats in size "biggest you sell". These are a bit tight on my head but I am going to try and stretch them out as I have heard others have had success. If not, my girlfriend gets these.

Summary
At this price point (I paid $25, usually run a little higher), I can't imagine a better on-ear headphone.

title: Worth Sales Price
text:
I got these JBL wireless bluetooth headphones 50% off for black friday. They are well worth the $25 I paid and I am very satisfied with their sound quality and frequency range. I don't think I would pay the $50 retail price... not becuse they are bad, but because there are others with exceptional sound quality and audio / noise canceling features starting at the $50 price point.

If you can find these for under $35, I highly recommend them! If not, I suggest browsing / comparing other options. Regardless, they are great sounding headphones for the price I paid!

text:

My one-year-old daughter got a hold of them and ripped off the ear pads in an instant. I was able to manage to place this back in carefully but knowing how this was set up make it feel less quality, especially coming from JBL. They had years of comfort and reliability on their products, so why cheap out on bad ear pads that can be easily taken off? Unless this was a design choice to change them out for "alternative colors" this is annoying to place back in. One night I was using this on my Steam Deck and the other pad came loose as I tugged on them. The only consideration is that I can place these back in, but this is a hassle and hopefully, it does happen often as it did for me.

I love the sound quality, I heard from others that I sound great over the phone with these ,and are great to travel with. This does not cover my ears completely so I would consider the

title: Very pleasant sound
text: I use these almost every night to watch tv.  They produce very good sound - voices are very clear and all sounds seem to be very accurate.  They are also very pleasant to use for listening to music — to my ears they are flat, they reproduce all of the bass, midrange and treble that is on the original source accurately.  Obviously I am not listening to lossless audio and these are not $500 headphones, but for listening to cd quality audio by Bluetooth these are excellent headphones.  Also my ears get too hot with almost all headphones, but these are really comfortable - I can wear them for about an hour before having to let my ears breathe for a minute and then I can put them back on for another hour.  And battery life is excellent - I charge them about once a week and they have never given out using them for about 2 hours/day.

title: OUCH!!!  these things squeeze your head!
text:

I have a normal sized head, wear a medium sized baseball hat and yet when I put these on they just squeezed my head and felt uncomfortable. I thought... nah, they are new, I'll give them a bit... NOPE!

a few minutes into wearing them and I could not stand it anymore. I took them off and had my wife and kids try. they hated the headphones.

did I mention my ears are still feeling that squeeze? never had this before with other headphones. very sad as I was hoping these would be amazing since JBL usually makes good stuff. not this time.

title: Strong sound, controls and connection OK
text:
Of course not as fine a sound as studio-grade headphones, but still consistently better than any earbud ever made. Relatively comfortable-- you can wear them longer than the higher-quality shell-type headset. Bass response is good, not great. At least the bass is not over-accentuated, like on some models. Treble range is not brittle, it's balanced. You might hope it would pair up with your sound source a little easier, and I've experienced some glitching when pairing these JBL phones with an .mp3 player that I'm wearing, but that might be a deficiency with the player, b/c when paired with a TaoTronics TTBA07 bluetooth transmitter, it works without error.

Fairly good noise cancelling. I expect there will be some durability issues with the ear pads wearing out. It's typical of headphones, so I try to treat them gently.

title: Affordable and it works
text:
Love these headphones for one reason, it was cheap lol. I have purchased 4 noise-cancelling headphones in one year for my son. Bought a Sony brand, Bose brand, and 2 Beats brand and they all broke. I’m so glad that he has bluetooth capabilities on his computer and phone, bc I think that was the problem for two of the brands.
I’ve asked how well the headphones are and he responds with it works. So I’m happy I’m not spending so much money on needing headphones to just work. If needed, I can buy another one. But he did say the noise cancellation works bc he doesn’t hear the neighbor’s dogs barking at night and the sound is impeccable. He also likes that the headphones are comfy. One thing he doesn’t like, it doesn’t have a cord lol.

title: They are nice. Easy to find because of color.
text:
I did not buy these headphones for their noise cancelling qualities. I got them because my in-ear phones don't always connect via bluetooth without a lot of fooling around.

The sound quality is very good. The fit is too tight, and after an hour become uncomfortable. Noise cancellation is average; I can hear the telephone, the tv, any equipment (vacuum cleaner) I'm operating, for example. But the outside noise does not interfere with understanding what comes over the headphones. Once the volume is set for the task at hand, I don't change it, so volume control is moot. These headphones seem heavier than the advertised weight.

I prefer my in-ear phones that do not have a noise cancelling feature, for fit and weight.

title: Not bad, but could be better.
text: I like the price and the famous name. The sound, I thought was great. The ear muffs begin to irritate after a few hours. I don't even need to charge them very often. My wife got "Over-the-ear" headphones and they were even cheaper had more features and sounded so much better, the bass on hers blew these away. I could wear over-the-ear type for much longer but these were the first I ever owned. Tried earbuds several different kinds and I sweat pretty bad when I train and they all would just fall out. I'm going to upgrade to over the ear and with more control of the sound, perhaps a type that has an app to help set the sound.

title: WOW! I wasn't expecting this...
text:
My profession is i am a sound engineer. I own a pair of Harmon Kardon that I absolutely love! Well, I got to the point that I did not want to use or take my Harmon on the road or to my gigs. So I found these JBL 510BT for a great price. So I took a chance on this unit and man, I am glad I did! These headphone has a great sound! I was not expecting the bass response I got from it from my MP3 player on my phone! In fact, the hi's & kid's are very clear and clean sounding!

The 510BT are so comfortable! The first evening i got them, I wore them for 3 hours and it felt like I could have worn them for another 4 hours!!! I paired them with my bluetooth transmitter coming from my TV, and the sound is sooooo clear!

If you are looking for a pair of good headphones at a great price, look no further!

title: Great economy headphones
text:
I've had these headphones a few weeks now and have used them several times. Connectivity to my three-year-old Samsung Galaxy S10e cell phone was easy, although still haven't been able to connect them to my 2018 Amazon Fire7 or 2014 Dell desktop computer. :(

Sound and range: While not noise cancelling, volume moderation makes listening reasonably focused with minimal external noise detection. Sound quality is very good for the price and range is impressive. I have clear, uninterrupted connectivity/sound throughout my home, inside and out. I walked half a block outside before sound started cutting out.

Battery: Battery life is exceptional. I used the device for several hours then turned it off to store them. Several days later they still had 100% charge and functioned for another few hours. I just rebooted the headphones after a week in a drawer and the battery reading is still 100%!

Comfort: The headphones are adjustable. That said, one reviewer men

title: Made for small/narrow faces; otherwise fine
text:
I bought these on sale without, and I can't quite bring myself to return them because they are otherwise serviceable, but they feel quite tight on my head and there's no proper way to adjust them out, just longer or shorter. Because these are on ear headphones, that lack of adjustability means that my ears are smashed pretty tightly against my head and quickly become uncomfortable and warm. For reference, my head is between 21" and 22" around at the top of my ears... which appears to be average per Google.

TLDR: If you are an adult with an average size head, these may not be as comfortable a fit as you'd expect.

title: Good sound, ear cups a little small
text: These are great for noise cancellation and provide excellent sound. But the ear cups are a little small for me (normal adult male ears). So the padding doesn’t fully encapsulate my ears - just barely. But nothing that is a deal breaker. The control buttons don’t sync well with my iPhone, so I can’t easily change songs, answer calls, or use Siri. But overall good value for price.

I found myself adjusting the headphones to ease the pressure on my upper ear lobes after prolonged use.  That said, I believe this phenomenon is true of any on-ear headset.  And it is likely equally a challenge if you were to use cans (over-ear headphones).  Just something us glasses wearer need to keep in mind.

title: Easy to use.
text:
I have in the last few years had many wireless headphones. All of them in the $100+ price range, so i did not have high hope for these, got them as a backup for my earbuds. What a nice surprise! These are the best of all of them, both in terms of ease of use and comfort. It takes a little fooling around with the articulated ear pieces to get to the recharging port. But after a time of 2 it is a simple operation. with a rated 12 hours per charge, it is very easy to have them charged all the time. The connection is easy the first time and automatic thereafter. I use Linux and have had no issues with the Bluetooth connection. For the price I would say give them a try. I did, and have had around a month of trouble free use so far, roughly 16 hours per day.

Hope this helps

title: Lots of attributes, excepting the actual sound
text:
I wanted to give these a two- or three-star rating, but to be fair, for $25 bucks, the build quality is very good, the comfort and fit is good (and the relatively high pressure they exert on the ears really helps with sound isolation), the battery life is good and the Bluetooth connection to my iPhone and computer is good.

The sound though ... it's fairly bad. I've had some cheap headphones that punch far above their weight clas, but these are not among them. To me, they're a bit out of balance, with too much mid and upper bass, making the mids sound a bit recessed. That's not necessarily bad because these headphones are seriously lacking in clarity and detail. Back to back with a set of wired Philips Fidelio M1 Mk2 headphones (which I bought "refurbished" for $40), the JVCs have a bit of a murky transistor radio sound, and you don't want that right in your face. The heart rate tracker is pretty accurate (give it time to adapt to changes in activity) and compares to the heart rate readings I get from the machines in the gym. I was doing my income tax on Sunday and the watch detected and notified me that I was stressed - and offered to walk me through deep breathing exercises (clever).

title: Warranty does NOT cover water damage
text:
The FIRST time I tried to go swimming with this watch it broke. I explained what happened to Samsung support, they said that the repair would be covered under warranty, then once they received the watch tried to charge me $140 to fix a $200 watch! They said this is because the damage was caused by water intrusion, even though there was only water intrusion in this case was due to a manufacturing defect!
So be warned, the watch is "waterproof" but if the one you get isn't it's not Samsung's fault and they wont pay for it!

It will be more comfortable to sleep in than the Sport I had before.  You can't take calls on it but can talk into text just like the Gear Sport and it seems pretty accurate and keeps the text history.  There's nothing about it I don't like so far but I'll come back and update if I discover something.  The charger is a USB so I can use my phone charger, computer or car and the part that attaches to the watch is as small as the watch face and is a magnet to click on the back.  For the price this is a fantastic smart watch but as the Gear Sport comes down in price you can choose between the 2 based on if you just want a rotating bezel or not... I don't miss it I didn't really use it that much anyway.

title: Atrocious battery life, mediocre performance

The device itself is also pretty sluggish. Not terrible given its mostly single purpose for me, but the experience wasn't great as a watch in general. But mostly I am just confused as to why Samsung released a product aimed at activity and included such a weak battery.

title: The good, the bad, and the obviously wrong
text:
I bought the Galaxy Active for two reasons: my Withings HR Steel was undercounting my calories burned, and I wanted to cycle and run without my phone. I really dislike square watches (Apple Watch and most fitness trackers), and I hate bulky watches (all Android watches and the rest of the Samsung line up).

The Active looks great, especially when you replace the band for something nicer, and it is feature packed. Not as many apps as the Apple Watch or even Wear OS, but certainly has a functional app store that covers most of the bases.

Unfortunately this watch has glaring flaws. While my HR Steel was under counting my calories, the Active is all over the place. Sometimes it will track 600 calories for a brisk 4 mile walk, other times the exact same route will track 350 (more in line with reality but still off), however it seems to be pretty accurate with cycling calories (6 mile cycle at about 300 calories).

The worst part about the watch is Spotify. It

That leads to a lot of problems with apps like Strava and Spotify that need connection. They just don't sync! Applications won't install. There's always trouble when you switch phones.

You manage your watch with Samsung Galaxy App on iOS. It often doesn't work as a connection provider either. The app says the Watch is connected, the watch says it's connected but there's no Internet on the Watch. So more problems with Strava, Spotify and all apps that need Internet connection.
