title: Completely dead after less than 6mos of use.
text: These earbuds were decent at first, nothing fancy, but my husband just wanted some to wear while he was hiking. They stayed in his ears fine. However, after 5mos of casual use and taking perfect care of them, they now refuse to connect in any way to any device. There is no sound at all coming out of the earbuds and no way to connect them via Bluetooth. They are 100% charged, and the just completely stopped working. It's obviously past the point that I can exchange or get a refund, and I honestly would not recommend buying this product. I've gotten less expensive wireless earbuds that have lasted me 5 years with no issues. Keep searching for something better.

title: Gym music
text: It's just like the pictures online and fit really well. sound quality is great for the gym or any place with some background noise.

title: Works Great
text: These work great.  They are very comfortable, the sound quality is good, and the charging case is nice.  I have many other BT headphones but they are attached by a wire to each other.  These are great without that wire especially when working out - gives you complete freedom and no more wire sticking to your neck.  I would highly recommend buying these.

title: Sounds good!
text: Sound is fine, connection is easy, I really like that both headphone have a mic so I can talk on the phone with either one in. The one thing that I have noticed is that it won't always pick up signal from my phone if my phone is in my back pocket, it's not the end of the world but it is slightly annoying especially when I'm out gardening or something. It's the only reason they lost a star from me.

title: It's hard to improve an already good product but they did
text:
I received them today and checked the charge.  Thank you for sending them 90% charged and ready for great listening. After replacing the ear caps with the smallest size, I paired the earbuds to my phone to check out the sound quality and ease of adjusting volume.  

The quality is so good I had listened to six songs before I realized that my testing was almost but not quite done. Given the conditions of the day, I grabbed a facemask and put it on, then took it off, repeating the process two more times.  The new, tighter fitting, ear hooks do not catch the mask ear strings.  Thank you for doing a great job, definite 5 out of 5 on these, better sound, better fit, easier to use controls, all at a great price.

title: Fit well, perform well, my 'go to' ear buds!
text: I've grown accustomed to listening to my Amazon playlist using these ear pods! They are comfortable (most of the time I forget I have them on!). They fit very well and keep out most outside noise while I'm using them. Sound quality isn't great (miss out on the very high and very low ranges a bit) but it's very good for everyday use. I mostly use them as replacements for my lost earpiece that connected to my iPhone for phone calls! I can easily listen to music and still take a call when it comes in! Perhaps the thing I like best about these ear pods is that storing them is a breeze in the charging case! The case not only gives me a place to keep them but it charges them while they're in there! And the LED window allows me to see just how much power remains on the case to charge them with! Keeping my ear pods charged couldn't be easier!

title: Great ear buds for the money! You must check these out.
text: Some of the info in the descriptions is a bit misleading, otherwise a full 5 stars are due.  The 48 hr run time stated in the description is the total run time using the fantastic charging case to charge the buds 6 times with an average actual playtime of 8 hours.  That being said I have purchased over time several ear buds in the 30$ range and so far this set is the finest I've used.  Very small and comfortable, interface buttons that are easy to use, great sound, and the visual charging indicators are great. The stated distance of 30' is pretty darn accurate as well.  For the money, I can highly recommend you try these.  I don't think you will be disappointed.

title: A seriously great deal
text: Seriously, for the price how can you go wrong?  I had some of the well known fruit themed ones and they died within 2 weeks! If these last 6 months I could buy a new pair every six months for years!  They turn on automatically like the other well known brands. They’re really a good deal. I can’t stress that enough.

title: Great Fitness Companion for the Price
text:
Great for the price! I bought these to use when jogging and biking because they are more secure than the standard earbuds.

However, the case is made of hard plastic that feels slightly cheap, but not a deal breaker. The blue tooth is easy to pair and ready to use. There are volume controls on each bud to accommodate a left or right handed person which at times can be hit or miss. I prefer using the phone's volume control. *Update 4.30.2021. Neither volume control works any longer. You must use the volume control on your phone which can be a hassle when you’re riding a bike, etc. so I am changing 4 stars to 3

When it comes to sound performance, I would say for the average person, these are ideal. Nice bass! For the sound aficionado, on a scale from 1 to 10, It would be a strong 7 (weak 8) for the lack of true High Definition music resolution.

As for call quality, I'd give this a 6+ per several people I spoke to said they could hear an echo when speakin

title: 1st SMART WATCH
text: I have nothing to compare this to. I basically got it because of price/"Amazon Choice" after a fiasco with a health plan watch I got that was useless.  Not interested in sleep or exercise tracking. Wanted heart rate & BP due to my age. If it's accurate my BP is really good and I'm walking more than I thought. It was easy to sync to phone which I've never done with any device. Pretty simple to figure out how things worked.  Like the weather/temp part too. What I'm not happy about is that there are supposed to be lots of "faces" for the watch or you can add your own pic. Most of the faces included are redundant and ugly. Very few that aren't just glaring graphics. No instructions at all as to how to use your own photos. Also can't figure out how to delete the 3  "pre-loaded" faces already on watch and it only lets you add 1 additional for a total of 4 to "set/use". One of the biggest reasons I choose this watch was thinking I'd have a variety of pretty pictures to choose from day to day.

title: I really love this watch!
text: I’ve had this watch for about a month, and still really love it. I didn’t like the band that came with it, as it made my skin sweat, so got a nice fabric band. Other than that, I have no issues with it. It shows my blood pressure, heart rate, blood oxygen level, steps, sleep, and the changeable face is large enough for me to see - I can even upload my own photos for the time face!  I turned off the “Quick View” function, and now my battery is only down to 43% after about 8 days. I did scratch the face in the first first days, so need to get a screen protector.  It is not waterproof, but this watch does everything I need it for, and more - thanks for a good product!

title: Waste of Materials - Lucky if it works as a watch
text:
I wanted a simple watch that can also accurately monitor my heart rate. This useless trinket is not that watch.

What are the pros and cons?

Pros - It was less than $25 USD

Cons
1. Scanning the QR code goes straight to Google Play. Useless for us Apple folks.
2. Version 3 of the iOS app specified in the instructions, is NOT in the Apple App Store. However, versions 5 and 8 are in the App Store. Neither of which seem to work with this “watch”
3. The charging cable is proprietary and just barely stays attached with the weakest magnets ever. A cat yawning in the same room as this, would more than likely dislodge the charging cable.
4. NOT being able to fully utilize it due to the iOS app being unavailable.
5. The sensor underneath has an air bubble in it. Doubtful that contributes to the accuracy of monitoring.
6. Too many more to list here. I felt morally compelled to warn people not to purchase this thing masquera

Word of advice, don't lose the charger cord!! Its made specifically for this watch.
You can see just the first few words of an incoming message but you can't respond at all.
The only extra feature on this fancy pedometer is that you can change the screen face. But then again you only have limit choices. Can't put your own image.
Not worth the price in my opinion.

title: Works like a champ
text: A little noisy, but works great. Definitely worth the price compared to other smart watches.

title: Not much luck.
text: About the time I had the watch figured out, it quit on me. The instructions aren’t that good. It’s very pretty and comfortable. The band is terrific.

title: Affordable Smart Watch that doesn’t require subscription
text: I’ve had this smartwatch for a couple months now. I bought it because, as a senior citizen in my 60s, it is affordable, it keeps track of steps taken, heart rate, etc. it even tells me my blood pressure. It does not require a costly subscription to do so. After several months, it is still doing great! This smartwatch is a smart buy!

title: Disappointed
text: Immediately after opening the Amazon packaging, the box seemed to be opened before. The instruction pamphlet was crumpled.  The watch was clearly used, and the backing would not stay on, and kept popping off.  I was able to keep the backing on for a short amount of time, and download the app. Yet the app didn't even work on my phone, stating that it was unsafe via Google Play.

title: I liked very much
text: The item came as told in amazon so yes I would buy again thank you

title: Stopped working in 3 months. Dead...
text: Stopped working in 3 months. Dead...

title: Love it, it's perfect and the prettiest shade of purple
text: I love it it's perfect just as described too and the purple is so pretty too. My only complaint is that the strap irritates my wrist so I can't wear it anymore I only got to wear it for maybe 2-3 days that's it and haven't worn it since

title: Nice Health Watch
text: Product works as advertised and has a stylish look !!

title: Great watch with difficult charging
text:
Sensors work
I haven’t figured out media player yet

Custom background and downloadable backgrounds will only take effect if device has enough charge or is charging.

CON
The charging cable is very short, only fits one way.
It’s magnetic but can be easily slid out of position.

I had mine charging 30 minutes but it fell off shortly after I set it up because it gained no power.

Would recommend a groove that the charger can sink into that might hold better.

title: Strap way too long for a woman's wrist
text: I bought this watch for my girlfriend. I had to return it because the strap was too long for her wrist and cannot be shortened. I tried it on my wrist and it was pretty large on me, and being the excess strap goes inside it was very difficult to put on. I bought her the lavender plastic strap and that is fine, although it wraps completely around her wrist to the watch face on the other side. The features of the watch are all great, but just realize you need a big wrist for these watches. My girlfriend has what I would say is a normal size wrist and the gold watch definitely will not be comfortable on a lady unless she has very large wrists.

title: Fabulous watch priced right!
text: I love this watch! The soft wrist band is vastly more comfortable than my previous watch. The screen is brighter in the sun. I like the square shape so the time shows on a single line instead of two. The old watch died when I pulled the strap off to charge it and it pulled the USB connector out of the watch itself. I was extremely pleased to see the magnetic coupler to charge this one. Note that it must exit under the watch on the side WITHOUT the button. You don't want to reverse the polarity. The image makes it clear which way to put it, but I saw no text instructions to that effect. I was pleased to be able to download the manual, which is much more readable than the sheet that comes with it. I have not crosschecked with an arm band BP test, but based on my knowledge of what to expect, I think this watch is far more accurate than the old one for BP and pulse rate. And this one does far more stuff with my phone than the old one did. And the price is right! You won't find a better wat

title: AWESOME!!!
text: Absolutely perfect.  Exactly as described.  This is an awesome price for such a good product.  Only downside is that you cannot reply to your texts, but they did not advertise that you could, so that’s fair.  Don’t hesitate to purchase this one.

title: Very difficult to set up and hard to charge!
text: Couldn’t even set it up uand the magnetic charger had to sit on it JUST right or it wouldn’t charge. I’m sticking with known brands!! This was a poor purchase. Unless you want to spend an hour or more setting it up and getting the Bluetooth to hook up - it’s not for you!! It’s definitely NOT for me. There are too many others that are simple to use!!

title: Kainco P22B Smartwatch Problems
text:
I love the smartwatch but does it take bp and hr measurements automatically? I went through the ph app an hit measure to get a reading. Also the sleep mode doesnt work. When I play my music in the car it notified me for every record an ive been trying to turn music notifications off. I have until 05/08/21 to return for refund. I should not be having these problems with a new product.
Thank you...Linda

title: Terrible customer service, no help at all
text: The watch itself is not bad, but I was never able to sync my text messages to the watch. The watch did not sync with the Iphone Health app neither. The worst part is not being able to get any type of help. Customer service is null. If you are not real good with Technilogy, and you are expecting help, forget it. Customer service is non existing. I am returning te watch.

title: This Watch landed me in the back of a Rescue Hospital bound
text: Yesterday morning after using this health watch for over a year and recommending it to many has a potentially deadly flaw in that was 14% off on my pulse ox measurement it read 97% and I could not get enough air from breathing .I crawled to phone and called 911 rescue arrived and got me onboard and measured pulse ox and it was 83% at deadly level. At the same time I checked my watch and it still read 97%. Their note says not Medical grade. Well its not even close. And the blood pressure reading is way off as well. The only close reading I obtained was the heart rate and  that was only off by one bpm. I will continue to use it but I certainly not rely on it or recommend it to anyone I want to see live for a while.

title: Grossly inaccurate
text: I loved this watch when I first bought it.  However, it has failed me badly and I will be returning it. Part of the problem might be the metal strap which is almost impossible to make tight because it goes underneath and not on top of the other side of the strap so you cannot reach it to to pull on it.  The day I got this, my BP was good and it read pretty accurately. Well, yesterday I had a rough day and I kept checking the watch and it said my pressure was fine. I finally got around to checking it with my meter last night and the numbers were way off. 30 pts off on the top and 20 on the bottom.  As for the hr?  I was sweeping snow and it said my heart rate was 66 when it was actually 214. That kind of difference can kill.

title: Covers the basics
text: It works alright for the price.  The blood pressure detected is always lower than my actual blood pressure. The sleep tracking usually works, but a couple of times registered no data for those nights.

title: Not accurate for more than a weeks use.
text: Battery life was fantastic, and I loved the screen, but after just a few days it was counting steps while I was laying down,  the sleep app was way off and gave weird times like it would say awake at 8:15 but it would be 5:00 AM in the morning. I also didn't like how the sleep app displays because it gave very lite detail. So, I've now returned 2 so called smart watches, & guess you get what you pay for. So I'm waiting to find a better, highly accurate one I really need due to being a long hauler from Covid. I have yet to find a good one that will stay accurate for more than 5 days. Very disappointed!

title: Adequate exercise measure
text:
I bought this device because it was said to measure pSO2. I used my pulse oximeter to verify results. The result is accurate when reading is over 95. Below 92 the posted result is 96. BP is accurate as is pulse under 100. I did not test the steps result.
For my purpose (I have ILD interstitial Lung disease) this is not helpful. I would conclude that as a fitness device this is a bargain, I will continue to seek a wrist based measuring instrument which addresses my need.

title: First impression
text:
Ordered yesterday, received today.
I'm not thrilled because the charging cable does not hold firmly to the watch. I put it on and lay the watch down and it pops off. Even the slightest pressure breaks the connection. Not going to be easy to keep it charged.
Update: it appears that I forgot to post the original, so here it is, along with another problem I found.
I just woke up and, according to the herobandIII app, I've walked 435 steps today. Either I'm sleepwalking or this watch is not trustworthy.
I've also noted a time or two that the BP readings are a bit higher than the ones I get with my cuff.

title: well.....its a nice watch (kinda only)

(not sure how it could do BP and O2 with a dorsal wrist sensor anyway) but was really hoping for better on the HR.  I tried 2, the first one was obviously incorrect so I ordered a replacement and same thing.  Second one unreturnable for some reason, so Im stuck with a pretty watch.  Cant say I recommend.

text:
These head phones are comfortable and stay in my ear very well. They come with multiple size buds They also have nice sound quality as well as the microphone. The cord is much longer than other brands of ear phones which I appreciate. This was my first magnetic set of buds, at first this was annoying as they would stick to my car, gym locker, metal part of door frame etc. After awhile I got used to this and it does help immensely for the ear buds not getting tangled.
I received these ear buds in early February and used them daily at the gym and a few times a week for phone calls. The ear buds stopped working Mid may, over all they lasted 3 months. The ear buds still appear in great condition and have no loose wires but no sound comes out of them. I do see that they are suppose to come with a 5 year warranty so I will attempt that process and update/ add stars back if the process is simple and the issue is resolved.
Update- Amazing customer service. I emailed the support team

title: I hope you like BASS!
text:
First, the good. These earbuds appear to be made with good materials, and provide multiple sizes and materials in the ear-canal inserts. The wire gauge is good for the purpose and the volume is higher than most. They might last for a long time for normal use. The in-line controls seem to work just fine and the designers placed them in a good place along the wire. Good design principles seem evident.

But I might not use them because of the negatives.

These earbuds put so much bass on the music, voices, and even my white-noise generators that I cannot listen for long and revert to other options like my bulky over-the-ear headphones that I certainly cannot carry out of the house on my normal routine. I used the eq on my phone and dropped the bass out and raised the treble up as high as possible, and still there's too much bass.

I changed the ear canal inserts to their secondary option, a kind of rubber insert that doesn't fit the ear and doesn't eliminate

title: Love these, highly recommend.
text: Love these!  So comfortable and very well made. They stay put in your ears unlike the plastic ones. They also have magnets so the head pieces stay together. The sound is great also. If you are looking for wired ear buds then I highly recommend these.

title: Very nice for the price
text: These ear buds are great for the price! The color is very nice, it works great for what I need them for!

title: Great Ear buds...Great sound and comfortable
text: I received these about a month or two ago and use them primarily for going to sleep.  They have great sound quality, have an extra long cord, and are really well made!  I am extremely happy with these.  I recommend buying them!!!! On/off control on cord, as well as a volume button.

title: Nice long cable. pretty good headphones for the price.
text: Very comfortable to wear with multiple ear bud covers to choose from. I like the longer length of the cable so you can place your phone (or other device) into a pocket or backpack and not have the earphones be jerked out of your ears if you move wrong.  They have magnets on the back of the headphones so they can be connected and stored in the pouch.,  Seems to help prevent tangling. I am giving 4 stars as we felt that the bass quality was lower than we liked. Other than that we really liked these headphones and they seem to be a great value for the price.

title: Hollow sound with zero base, don't stay in ears

I couldn't wear them walking down the street, let alone running on a treadmill. I wouldn't recommend them.

text:

Although cord is long it can be used with tv or stereo with extension cord if needed. It has separate jack for phone and other items. It also came with extra earbuds and a very small clip to use to help cord stay attached to clothes or whatever. I can’t comment on sound quality because I have yet to use them. I had bought them to use for night time listening to tv so I would not bother other family member. However, I am not able to use for that purpose as neither the tv nor the sound system has an jack provided for earbuds/headphones. I refuse to use radiation, cancer causing wireless/Bluetooth earbuds/headphones!! I hope this maker and others continue to produce earbuds/headphones with wires for those of us concerned about our health.

title: Very good quality, not the most comfortable for small ears though
text: The sound quality is great, the cord is long which I really needed, and I love the way the buds will snap together magnetically, so I can wear them around my neck to use as a mic if I want to use my speakers. I do it that way over discord voice chats a lot. The only con for me, and the reason I gave it a 4 star, is that all the buds these come with are very uncomfortable in my ears. Luckily I was able to attach the buds from a different pair of foam ear-buds that were super comfy. My left ear is much smaller than my right so I can only use foam buds. That probably won't be an issue for most others, so I didn't let that affect my rating too much.

title: PS4 and Windows laptop compatible
text: The mic audio is good according to the other party members on PS4 and my teacher can hear me as well during virtual class meetings on my Windows computer. The sound quality is good as well and the bass is nice too. The only thing that really bothered me with these was the "memory foam" it was squishy, weird, and little particles stick to those buds. Thankfully they also include the silicone tips which never fit my ears but the smallest size proves to be comfortable enough for me to wear for long periods of time. Overall a good buy compared to cheaper earbuds.

title: Unimpressed

They do not want to stay in, I tried both sizes and was not impressed even just walking around the house. Great quality, function, but fails in performance for me. Returning.

title: Works well on PS4
text: Great quality, love that they are magnetic and the earbuds will stick to each other. They don't tangle and get annoying because of that. I use them for my PS4 and everyone says they can easily hear me and understand me and that they're good quality.

title: Five Stars

title: Don't last long
text:
Exceptional quality for the money. Excellent customer service. This is my second set. I broke the first set by yanking on the wire when it was wrapped around a button. Totally my fault!
Excellent sound quality. Both highs and bass. I usually listen to music for hours, with no problems.
The only thing that gave me problems with the first pair, was the volume slider. It can catch on something and raise or lower the volume unexpectedly. This pair has more tension on it, so that should be less of a problem. I would definitely recommend them.

title: Not worth it
text: The sound is nice and the magnets in the buds are a nice feature to keep them from getting tangled, but external sounds come through very loud over phone calls making it very hard for the people on the other end to hear me, and these earbuds didn't even last a year. Very disappointing. If you want to take advantage of the warranty, you have to pay to ship them back. It ended up being cheaper to just buy a new pair.

title: Luxury feel, sound quality can be improved
text:
I bought these for my mom and me. She likes the old fashioned in line earbuds.
First glance and feel, they have a nice feel and the material is durable. I like the magnet that holds the earbuds together. Its a decent case to hold everything together.
Sounds quality is not as impressing. The noise cancelation is not truly noise canceling. Phone calls are not very clear and microphone doesn't make me sound clear. Music is not the best if you like heavy bass songs.
For the price I expected a little more.

I have a $30 pair or earbuds from sony that I will continue to use and throw these in a drawer as a backup. Spend the extra $10 and get some nice earbuds that work!!

title: Well made, soft on your ears, affordable, 5 year warranty, sound great! Great value !
text:
For the money, these are excellent phones. I was looking for a great sounding pair that wouldn’t hurt my ears watching TV with my head on a pillow. Also that wouldn’t drain my Roku remote while I listened wireless. My Bose over the ear sound great, but don’t work laying down...and drain 2 AA batteries quickly.
So I started reading reviews. I didn’t want to spend more than twenty dollars, wanted a long warranty(5 Years!), wanted soft phones for my ear canals, wanted excellent sound. These filled all those needs and are soft, sound great and don’t drain the batteries on my remote. Odd feature, they come in a small metal tin like chewing Tabbaco ....and I could not pry it open! I had to cut through the bottom of the tin to get everything out. Includes carry sack AND a splitter so two people can listen to one source. Recommended!

title: Great buy

title: great sound, great price
text: these earbuds are fantastic, i will definitely be buying a second pair. i am using them for gaming on my pc and the sound is just as if not better than my 200 dollar OTE headphones. i switched as i was getting a strange sore neck from wearing my heavy over the ear set, these light weight earbuds did the trick, the neck pain is gone and the sound is terrific, glad i switched! if anything for 20 bucks give it a shot they are worth it! the little magnetic ends are also nice to affix to my metal monitor stand for quick access and removal.

title: Pretty good overall, no noise cancellation

title: Great quality.

title: It Doesn't Get Any Better
text: OMG! OMG! I'm not paid or compensated. I am a videographer, gamer, and love music & big into sound quality. I have NEVER heard anything so clear & it's just amazing! The highs are crisp, the mids give awesome vocals & the bass is rich, it doesn't distort. Pick a comfortable ear cushion, plug in & rock out! Oh you're a gamer? Prepare to hear things you didn't even know were there! If you listen to music with your phone consider this, these ear buds play like there's an amp in them, meaning you don't have to turn them up as loud & you save battery juice! If you have an equalizer & use these, you can truly milk even more awesomeness from these! I just can't say enough about them, they're an amazing piece of kit. If you're still reading, BUY THESE! NOW! THANK ME LATER!

title: Didn't do it for me
text:
I purchased the Klim earbuds as a substitute for wearing gaming headphones, and I was overall disappointed with the product. After about a week of use, I noticed that the left earbud was significantly quieter than the right, making for an unbalanced and awkward experience. Due to this, I will be returning mine.

Assuming not all Klim earbuds have this problem and mine was just defective, I had some other problems with these headphones as well. The product comes with several styles of replacement buds, ranging from different material to different shapes, and even one in a different (red) color. Unfortunately, they all seem to be molded to fit about the same size ear. I have pretty average sized ears, maybe a little small. When plugging my earbuds into my PC (below my desk), I noticed the earbuds were sagging significantly in my ears. The metal material they used on the outside of the earbuds is a little bit heavy, and since the earbuds don't fit me well to begin with, t

title: Long cord.
text: I really like the memory foam plugs. Nice and neat container and pouch. The only thing is that the cord is really long so you have to figure out how to keep it from snagging. Accessories are nice too.

title: 2 pairs failed in 6 months- dishonest, STAY AWAY
text:
I am a pro sound engineer with 20+ years of industry experience.

Update: warranty was honored but in less than 2 months the cord has the same problem, the button on the remote doesn’t work, shielding issues. Stay away. These are junk! Customer service was friendly but lied about refunding my money!

Update- started breaking after 2 months-the ear buds themseves and the cord is breaking. Awful. Hope they honor the warranty.

Huge promises, doesn’t really deliver. This company should spend less on packaging and stickers and more on the product. The build quality itself seems great at first but these fell apart quickly just like cheaper ear buds. But the sound is just ok. Very clear in the mid and high range but bass and low mids are almost non existent. The in ear pads are of nice variety— but there’s not much variation and none fit my ear canal well to isolate sound. They also all fall apart quickly, expect to replace them. These are not noise canceling.

The earbud cover are great a memory foam type deal squeeze and roll then put in ear and expands to size to block out all other noises spiders or wind. Great sound

title: I'm really happy I bought these – I would buy again in a heartbeat
text:
These are nice earbuds for the money. The nose cancellation is accomplished primarily by the memory foam ear plug style buds. These fit over the regular buds -- all of which are included. I really like that, as it makes them comfortable to wear while it's blocking out the noise.

One thing about these is that the wires/plastic covering are really stiff. This means that I have a hard time keeping it from standing up and touching my face (I use them primarily sitting up in bed, as I read and listen to music). If I was upright, maybe working or walking/jogging, it wouldn't be a problem I suspect.

I did contact the company about buying replacement memory foam buds, as my experience with actual foamy ear plugs is that they get dirty and don't form to fit your ears as well. They responded immediately that they do have them, and asked me which size I needed, and which color I wanted. AWESOME! However, I've been using these for a couple of months now, and they aren't dirt

text: Got one for my husband and recently a pair for myself.  My daughter got them for Xmas. She can't keep any earbuds alive for more than a month and these lasted 3 months.

title: Not recommended
text: I've used these for about a month and there's already problems.  There is an audible low humming noise.  I don't appreciate the extra acoustic bass that these earbuds have, I prefer just normal earbuds that don't have an extended chamber that makes sound seem like is it traveling some distance in space, like a hollowed out cavern.  I've connected these earbuds to my phone and have accidentally dropped my phone a couple of times.  These earbuds are sensitive that being dropped causes their sound quality to deteriorate and also the green connector is not bent from originally being straight.  It took some time for me to reach this conclusion but normal earbuds perhaps suit me best, not some earbuds that have some sort of special engineering that doesn't improve sound quality at all.

title: My wife & I both have Fitbits and LOVE them!!!
text: This is a great tool to help motivate you - it will track multiple health aspects such as weight, nutrients consumed calories burned, etc.  It will link with other logging accounts such as My Fitness Pal which makes both programs more accurate and fun to use.  For those of us who are competitive, it is great to compete again people you know, and those you don't - to be ranked based on age, sex, and health demographics...  This is an outstanding product I would, and have, recommend to anyone who is interested in getting healthier.

title: Three Things that Changed My Life!
text:
Three things have changed my life! FITBIT, Walking Workouts and Walking 4 Fun.

FITBIT ONE - I have been faithfully wearing it. Using the calories in and out featured on the app. It has a safety leash and I wear it attached to my bra. I do a Jessica Smith DVD - 10K Walking Workout (see link on bottom of this review) and Leslie Sansone ("Walk at Home"). However, ONE is super accurate on my walks outside and around the office. My bottom line is 10K steps a day but I tend to walk 17K a day. I worked my way up to this since October 2015. I have a sedentary job so walking happens 3 to 4 times a day with a minimum of a mile on each walk. Yes, I have lost weight, @ 40 lbs. There is at least that much more that needs to be lost as well. Hair color is silver if that helps you define the situation.

I love the little activity record feature and use it often (hold down the button to start and hold it down to end when activity is over). This is great when co

title: Tracking one small step...starts with knowing what you want.
text:
I bought my Fitbit One after a considerable amount of research among the various "life activity trackers" on the market. As I read, my focus shifted from the features of the products themselves to the personal goals I intended to accomplish once I started moving around. Ultimately, this helped me make my final purchase.

As you may have found, I discovered that despite the choices available to the consumer, I did not find a particular fitness tracker that measured all daily activities equally well. Design philosophies and marketing strategies seem to conspire to confuse the subject further and make it difficult to match a particular product to your needs. However, many excellent reviews have been published by Amazon-reviewers as well as by online product-review writers which were very helpful.

In any event, I was looking for a decent pedometer and sleep monitor. I can't run anymore due to arthritis, but I do enjoy walking and was pretty sure that I am not

title: Outstanding Device
text:
I've been a FitBit fan since September 2011. My first model was the original, now known as Classic. My next model was the Ultra. Now I have the One. I'm on my second One and it is a vast improvement over the former models. The design is wonderful; it looks like a gem. The two older models were designed in a hairpin shape & served as the clip. The flaw in the design was that the repeated stress on the curve from attaching & detaching it to clothing caused the curved end to crack. The One's design is slim, polished, and elegant. It slips easily into a silicone, non allergenic holder which has a built in clip for attaching to clothing. The sleep band is also improved. The unit slips into a pocket & the band itself is far more comfortable & easier to don.

The prior models required a charging base. The One charges on a simple design USB charger. This let's me charge just about anywhere; my laptop, PC, a USB wall adaptor, even my car stereo. However, the battery life

title: Now addicted to Fitbit!
text:
I purchased and first started using Fitbit One (2) weeks ago today. I set a reasonable goal for myself of losing (1)lb per week and I am right on target with my weight loss plan. I clip it on my bra - it stays in place and no one knows that I'm wearing it but me.

I love being able to track all I eat, and what I do for exercise easily with the Fitbit App. I also used it to monitor my sleep patterns the first (2) weeks, finding I wake much too often:(

Fitbit One has made it easy for me to push myself just a bit more each day than I ever did before, and when I go well above my daily target, my daily calorie intake increases, allowing more food, extra wine or a special treat.

Highly recommended.

title: Addicted
text:
I ordered my first FitBit last February and wore that thing every frigging day. It was addicting and I felt motivated as well to get off the couch if I wasn't at a certain amount of steps or flights of stairs. I also am a My Fitness Pal user (via website and iPhone app), and look forward to the calorie adjustments based on the FitBit syncing up, then based on activity will adjust your caloric intake for the day on your My Fitness Pal account.

Both of my parents, a cousin and 3 friends have them as well and we are all "friends" on the FitBit website so you can see where everyone is step wise for the week. This is also motivating because both of my parents are "step geeks" and I try to push myself to beat them on a weekly basis. This has yet to happen since we all ordered these last year. This is where the addicting aspect comes in, I am addicted to trying to achieve more steps than my parents.

This past February the cover of my FitBit kind of crumbled off, I t

title: Makes walking fun
text: Walking and running can be pretty boring sometimes. This adds a nice competitive edge, especially if you have friends with a Fitbit. The app and website are great for tracking progress. It also syncs with other apps like MyFitnessPal which I find has a nicer food database for tracking your caloric intake. Via the app you can also add different activities like yoga and spinning which are difficult to track with a pedometer. The device is very discrete - I just keep mine clipped to my bra and forget I have it on. I washed it once and it miraculously survived (thought I wouldn't recommend doing that!). I may upgrade to the Flex in the future, but for now this has been a terrific little device.

title: Great tracker, but I think it overestimates calorie burns
text: I have been using this for over a month, along with My Fitness Pal, which I use to track meals and sync with the fitbit.  My wife who is a nurse thinks it vastly overstates the calories burned by the activity.  Also, My Fitness Pal only syncs up to 10,000 steps, so I sometimes don't get all the activities.  It can't be used effectively for biking either.  But it's a nice way to track your steps.  I have tried it a few times to track sleeping.  It shows the number of times I woke up, but if I did wake up that many times, I certainly wasn't aware of it.  Not sure that is accurate at all.

title: Good device with a lousy holder
text:
I upgraded from the Fitbit Zip after the Zip helped me lose a lot of weight. Very shortly afterward, I bought another Fitbit One for my wife.

The display is easy to read and the device syncs seamlessly with various apps on my iPhone. For a pedometer in this price range, it's reasonably accurate and will certainly help you get up, get outside, and walk around. It's a great little device, especially if you have a friend who owns one and friendly competition will prompt you to rack up more mileage. Between this, and changes to my diet, I've lost about 45 lbs., and all without doing anything but tracking my food intake and using the Fitbit to help keep my activity levels up.

The bad news about the Fitbit One is the holder. It's a silicone sleeve with a metal clip. I would call it a belt clip, but it won't attach to most belts, except maybe military webbing belts. It fits well clipped to the inside of a pocket. My wife clips hers to the inside of her bra. Whatever you

title: The device appears to be pretty accurate and will send you awards and notices
text: This was a gift.  Recipient loves it as it is small and unobtrusive.  The device can be sync'd to an app which in turn can link you up with friends.  The device appears to be pretty accurate and will send you awards and notices.

title: Great tracker
text: Great buy, is very accurate, love it counts your water intake and let's you know how much fat you've burned, would purchase again as a gift.

title: Nice product and easy to use
text:
All the other reviews are right. This is a great product. I never realized how little activity I can get in a day. I shoot for 10,000 steps and 20 flights of stairs and this helps motivate me to do this. The sleep tracker is kind of cool, but easy to forget to do.

My fitbit broke after 4 months. The customer service is second to none. Contacted fitbit through their website and they asked for my proof of purchase. I emailed my Amazon receipt to them and a short time later I had a new fitbit in the mail.

The app is easy to use, but I barely use it. Usually my computer picks it up at night and transmits the data.

title: The One is perfect though. It works great
text: I bought this because I wanted to steer clear of the wristband trackers after one of my others broke off my wrist. However, I have since decided that I prefer the wrist one. The One is perfect though. It works great, and I love the Fitbit brand. The customer support is wonderful.

title: Easiest way to track your activity ... and it syncs!
text: I chose the Fitbit One to better record my activity (as an alternate to a pedometer which had iffy accuracy.)  Now that I've had Fitbit One for a few days I must say that I totally love it!  It is small, clips securely to your clothing, the displays are great (including its cheery greetings!) and it automatically downloads info into Myfitnesspal or other diet tracking sites.  Couldn't be easier!  The Fitbit site is great and very useful.  This unit even tracks your sleep patterns!  Would recommend this item to anyone seeking to track and improve their activity levels

title: Five Stars
text: I love my Fitbit.  I am walking more now.

title: Makes Me Move
text: I love my FitBit! If I had to buy another one, I would probably buy the less expensive one that doesn't track "sleep" (really, just how much you move during the night) or stairs climbed (since my Digifit app will do that for me during workouts), but they are both still fun to track. I am definitely less active when I'm not wearing it because I don't think about getting up from my desk as often, and my exercise doesn't feel like it "counts" as much. I also love challenging my friends and coworkers with how many steps we can get!

title: 70 LBS Down and counting!
text: I credit Fitbit with my loss of 70 lbs over a period of approximately 9 months, a loss I've kept off for going on a year.  Using it regularly made me realize how little exercise I was getting, and made me focus on increasing my efforts.  I'm not one for group exercise, but competing daily against myself--my previous Fitbit results-- stirred my competitive juices, and I tried to better my numbers every day.  Now I'm at the point where I feel restless if I haven't walked my five miles a day, and I try to shake up my routine a bit by finding new and different forms of exercise--like skydiving!  When I think of all the money I wasted over the years on exercise equipment, weight loss plans and so forth, I'm grateful for the simplicity and effectiveness of Fitbit.  Not to say that it would work for everyone--motivation is key--but at this price, it's well worth a try.

title: Works as advertised for me
text:
I've owned this for just over 1 year and it has worked well. I've used this device to track steps, flights of stairs, active minutes, and to help estimated calories burned. I've used the Fitbit app to count calories and counting calories has helped me lose 40 pounds.

In my opinion, the device does a good job of counting steps. I have never had problems too many or too few steps. Wearing this fitbit while driving has never given a false reading. It may not be 100% accurate, but it is close enough.

I have had a couple of problems with not syncing, but rebooting the device has solved them. I also had an instance where it counted too many flights of stairs but a reboot fixed that also.

About the weight loss:

The app is great and counting calories made all the difference it weight loss. Steps help you get in shape, but without changing eating habits you weight won't change much.

**********************************************************

title: Great external framework for weight loss.
text: Because it makes critical decisions for me - how many calories to take in and how much to exercise - it removes all psychological barries to weight loss. I have been losing slowly and consistently since the day I got it about 6 weeks ago. There is more functionality and information available than what I use. I imagine just anyone doing exercise with any kind of health goal would benefit from this clever little device. BTW the phone app is the main interface I use, which is far from agile but does the trick.

title: I use it every day and got one for both my parents
text: A very simple and useful device that will make you think about your metrics every day.  I am always deciding to walk to get those extra steps to achieve my goal.

title: I love my fit bit
text: I love my fit bit! I love challenging co workers, family members and friends to compete in the app. I run around a lot at work and wearing the fit bit makes it a little more fun.
